EDCS-10100579.text_edition: Quod pater optabat fieri sibi quod pia mater infelices sic nati sic fecit uterque parentum  Dis Manibus  Aurelio Titiano defuncto annorum  et Aurelio Iustiano defuncto annorum  et Aurelio Iusto defuncto annorum  filiis pientissimis et Suro alumno Aurelius Titus et Aurelia Martilla parentes et sibi vivis posuerunt
EDCS-10100579.translation: What the father hoped would be done for himself, what the pious mother desired, the unhappy children did for both parents. To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Titus and Aurelia Martilla, parents, and for themselves while living, placed this for their most dutiful sons Aurelius Titianus, deceased, Aurelius Iustianus, deceased, and Aurelius Iustus, deceased, and for their foster-child Surus.
EDCS-10100579.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription by Aurelius Titus and Aurelia Martilla for their three deceased sons, Aurelius Titianus, Aurelius Iustianus, and Aurelius Iustus, and their foster-child Surus, also preparing a place for themselves.

EDCS-10100580.text_edition: Aurelio Victorino coniugi et Aureliae Victorinae filiae et Aurelio Vindici genero et Iulio Vindiciano nepoti Aurelia Atilia bbene mmerentibus et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-10100580.translation: Aurelia Atilia, while living, placed this for her well-deserving husband Aurelius Victorinus, her daughter Aurelia Victorina, her son-in-law Aurelius Vindex, and her grandson Iulius Vindicinus, and for herself.
EDCS-10100580.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia Atilia for her husband, daughter, son-in-law, and grandson, as well as for herself, during her lifetime.

EDCS-10100581.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Baebiae meae matri et filiae Titiae nnrum V Baebius Titianus
EDCS-10100581.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For my Baebia, mother and daughter, T. n. n. r. u. m. V. Baebius Titianus.
EDCS-10100581.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Baebius Titianus to his deceased wife Baebia, who is also referred to as a mother and daughter, containing an abbreviated formula that is not fully clear.

EDCS-10100582.text_edition: Dis Manibus Manlio Pontio Valenti Iulia Itache sic coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100582.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Iulia Itache erected this for Manlius Pontius Valens, her well-deserving husband. Thus.
EDCS-10100582.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Manlius Pontius Valens, erected by his wife Iulia Itache, highlighting her affection for her 'well-deserving' spouse.

EDCS-10200191.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Antonius Gaius pro se et suis ponit Imperatore Severo Alexan[dro] III consule
EDCS-10200191.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Antonius Gaius dedicates this for himself and his family, in the third consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander.
EDCS-10200191.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Antonius Gaius for himself and his family, dated to the third consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander (229 CE).

EDCS-10200192.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Bito militi legionis IIII Flaviae stipendiorum XVIII titulum fecit Titus Valerius Valentinus custos armorum legionis eiusdem haeres sic fecit Bito supra scripto vixit annos XXXVIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10200192.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Marcus Aurelius Bito, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, with 18 years of service. Titus Valerius Valentinus, custodian of arms of the same legion, his heir, made this monument. Thus he made it for Bito, mentioned above, who lived 38 years. He placed it for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10200192.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Aurelius Bito, a soldier of the Legio IIII Flavia, erected by his heir, Titus Valerius Valentinus, a custos armorum of the same legion, who died at 38 after 18 years of service.

EDCS-10200193.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Aurelio Glyconiano |(centurioni) legionis Claudiae ex decimipilo legionis IIII Flaviae stipendiorum LII vixit nnos LXX Aelia Severina coniux cum filio sibi et coniugi amantissimo posuit
EDCS-10200193.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Marcus Aurelius Glyconianus, a centurion of the Claudian Legion, formerly a decimipilus of the Fourth Flavian Legion, with 52 years of service. He lived 70 years. Aelia Severina, his wife, with their son, placed this for herself and her most loving husband.
EDCS-10200193.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Glyconianus, a centurion who served in multiple legions for 52 years and lived to be 70, erected by his wife Aelia Severina and their son.

EDCS-10701875.summary: This is a brief inscription likely naming the emperor Hadrian, perhaps from a boundary stone or public work.

EDCS-10701876.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Marcus Cocceius Valens decurio Augustalis coloniae pro salute sua et Polittae coniugis et Cocceiorum filiorum votum solvit
EDCS-10701876.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Marcus Cocceius Valens, decurion augustalis of the colony, fulfilled his vow for his own well-being and that of his wife Politta and his sons Cocceii.
EDCS-10701876.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Sol Invictus Mithras by Marcus Cocceius Valens, a decurion augustalis, on behalf of himself, his wife Politta, and his sons.

EDCS-11201170.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Posidonius decurio municipii Naissi voto posuit
EDCS-11201170.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Aurelius Posidonius, decurion of the municipality of Naissus, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11201170.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Posidonius, a decurion of the municipality of Naissus.

EDCS-11201172.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Iulius Herculanus b(ene)f(iciarius) consularis votum libens merito posuit pro se et suis Imperatore domino nostro et Titiano consulibus
EDCS-11201172.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Iulius Herculanus, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly placed this vow for himself and his family, in the consulship of our lord Emperor and Titianus.
EDCS-11201172.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Iulius Herculanus, a beneficiarius consularis, for himself and his family, dated to the consulship of the reigning emperor and Titianus (likely 245 CE).

EDCS-11201173.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Iulius Satyrus VSIXPS
EDCS-11201173.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus. Iulius Satyrus VSIXPS.
EDCS-11201173.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus by Iulius Satyrus, with an unclear abbreviation following the dedicant's name.

EDCS-11201174.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Marcus Ulpius Herculanus posuit
EDCS-11201174.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus. Marcus Ulpius Herculanus erected this.
EDCS-11201174.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, made by Marcus Ulpius Herculanus, likely on an altar or votive tablet, and probably dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201175.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno C Flavius Sabinus pro se  VM
EDCS-11201175.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus. Gaius Flavius Sabinus for himself. V. M.
EDCS-11201175.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus by Gaius Flavius Sabinus for his own well-being, indicated by 'pro se', with an abbreviation 'V. M.' possibly meaning 'votum meritum' or 'votum solvit merito'.

EDCS-11201176.text_edition: Iovi Paterno Aelius Hermogenes ex voto posuit
EDCS-11201176.translation: To Jupiter Paternus, Aelius Hermogenes placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-11201176.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Paternus, made by Aelius Hermogenes as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11201179.text_edition: Aurelius Lupus decurio ex voto posuit
EDCS-11201179.translation: Aurelius Lupus, decurion, placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-11201179.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius Lupus, a decurion, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11201180.text_edition: posuerunt Idibus Augustis Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus
EDCS-11201180.translation: They placed this on the Ides of August, in the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus.
EDCS-11201180.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument on August 13th, dated to 241 AD during the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus.

EDCS-11201183.text_edition: et sibi viba sic et Aurelius Valentinus veteranus filiae bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201183.translation: And for themselves alive, thus also Aurelius Valentinus, veteran, and for his well-deserving daughter, they placed this.
EDCS-11201183.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument placed by Aurelius Valentinus, a veteran, for his well-deserving daughter and also for himself and his family while still living.

EDCS-11201185.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Florentinae dulcissimae et Victorinae et Candidani sic et Urbiano et liberto Severo et sibi et suis posuit
EDCS-11201185.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Aurelia Florentina, the sweetest, and Victorina, and Candidanus, likewise for Urbianus, and for the freedman Severus, and for herself and her family, she placed this.
EDCS-11201185.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a group of individuals including Aurelia Florentina, Victorina, Candidanus, Urbianus, and the freedman Severus, placed by an unnamed dedicator for herself and her family.

EDCS-11201186.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Alexander vixit annos LXX Claudia
EDCS-11201186.translation: To the Divine Manes, Gaius Iulius Alexander lived 70 years. Claudia [set this up].
EDCS-11201186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Alexander, who lived 70 years, likely set up by Claudia.

EDCS-11201187.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Annus vixit annos XX Plannius pater filio bene merenti et sibi  et Licinio filio Andiae  Rufus Anni vixit annos XXV Annus Dasi pater filio posuit
EDCS-11201187.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Gaius Iulius Annus lived 20 years. Plannius, his father, placed this for his well-deserving son, and for himself, and for his son Licinius. Andia Rufus Anni lived 25 years. Annus Dasi, his father, placed this for his son.
EDCS-11201187.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription recording the deaths of multiple individuals: Gaius Iulius Annus (aged 20), erected by his father Plannius; and Andia Rufus Anni (aged 25), erected by his father Annus Dasi.

EDCS-11201190.text_edition: Fili meus sic dulcis Antonine quem fata tulerunt cum octavo carperet anno disrupit sic mors invida vitae fecit vero terminum vitae mense Septembri indictione nona
EDCS-11201190.translation: My sweet son Antoninus, whom the Fates took away when he was in his eighth year, thus envious death broke the thread of life and truly made an end to his life in the month of September, in the ninth indiction.
EDCS-11201190.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription commemorating Antoninus, a son who died in his eighth year, with the death dated to September in the ninth indiction.

EDCS-11201193.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Hermes votum libens animo posuit
EDCS-11201193.translation: Tiberius Claudius Hermes willingly and with good heart placed this vow.
EDCS-11201193.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Tiberius Claudius Hermes, made willingly as a vow.

EDCS-11201198.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis deabusque immortalibus pro salute dominorum nnostrorum Imperatorum Auggustorum Lucius Aurelius Iustinus vir clarissimus numini maiestatique eorum dicatissimus
EDCS-11201198.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other immortal gods and goddesses, for the well-being of our lords the Augusti Emperors, Lucius Aurelius Iustinus, a most distinguished man, most dedicated to their divine power and majesty, dedicates this.
EDCS-11201198.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other immortal deities by Lucius Aurelius Iustinus, a vir clarissimus, on behalf of the reigning emperors.

EDCS-11201199.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Augusti Atelius Martinus qCuod sic#quod sic#CUD destinavit posuit
EDCS-11201199.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of the Augustus, Atelius Martinus, as he had vowed, placed this.
EDCS-11201199.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Atelius Martinus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of the reigning emperor, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11201200.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertenacis sic Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici Maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Augusto tribunicia potestate V consuli proconsuli res publica sua Ulpiana curante Quinto Anicio Fausto legato Augustorum pro praetore
EDCS-11201200.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, son of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the fifth time, consul, proconsul, the Ulpian republic dedicated this, with Quintus Anicius Faustus, legate of the Augusti, propraetor, supervising.
EDCS-11201200.summary: This inscription is a monumental dedication by the Ulpian republic to the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus (Caracalla), dating to approximately 202 CE, detailing his imperial titles and lineage, overseen by Quintus Anicius Faustus.

EDCS-11201201.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aemilio Aemiliano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli patri patriae
EDCS-11201201.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aemilius Aemilianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the country.
EDCS-11201201.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to the Emperor Marcus Aemilius Aemilianus, listing his full imperial titles, dating to his brief reign in 253 CE.

EDCS-11201203.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Tatto  vixit nnos LXXXVII et ILIVius vixit annosIVI  Severinus heredes bene meritis titulum posuerunt VIItina coniux bene merita m marito titulum posuit
EDCS-11201203.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Tatto lived 87 years, and Ilivius lived 4 years. Severinus, their heirs, placed this monument to the well-deserving. Vitina, his well-deserving wife, placed this monument for her husband.
EDCS-11201203.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Aurelius Tatto and Ilivius, placed by their heirs Severinus, and separately by Vitina for her husband, likely Aurelius Tatto.

EDCS-11201205.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Aurelianus legionis VII C[laudiae &
EDCS-11201205.translation: To the Divine Manes, Aurelius Aurelianus of the Seventh Legion Claudia...
EDCS-11201205.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Aurelianus, a soldier of the Legio VII Claudia, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-11201206.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mal Iulinanus miles legionis VII Claudiae strator legati vixit nnos LX Aelia Fabricia coniux Mal Iulia Mal Ulpianus Mal Iuliana Iulius Valens fili aeius sic superstantes sibi et coniugi bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201206.translation: To the Divine Manes. Mal Iulianus, soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, groom of the legate, lived 60 years. Aelia Fabricia his wife, Mal Iulia, Mal Ulpianus, Mal Iuliana, Iulius Valens, his surviving children, placed this for themselves and for their well-deserving spouse and father.
EDCS-11201206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mal Iulianus, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia and a legate's groom, erected by his wife Aelia Fabricia and his surviving children.

EDCS-11201207.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Nundinianus vixit annos LXXX Valerius Martinianus vixit annos XX Dalmana vixit annos V patri marito filiae posuit bene merentibus Vettiola vixit annos LX
EDCS-11201207.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Nundinianus lived 80 years. Valerius Martinianus lived 20 years. Dalmana lived 5 years. Vettiola lived 60 years. She placed this for her father, husband, and daughter, who were well-deserving.
EDCS-11201207.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by a woman (presumably Vettiola, though her relation to the others is unclear from this text) for her father, husband, and daughter, listing their ages at death.

EDCS-11201209.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Victor vixit annos XVIII titulum posuerunt Publius Aelius Iustinus et Aelia Claudia parentes filio pientissimo et sibi sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-11201209.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Victor lived 18 years. Publius Aelius Iustinus and Aelia Claudia, his parents, placed this monument for their most dutiful son and for themselves. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11201209.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Victor, aged 18, placed by his grieving parents, Publius Aelius Iustinus and Aelia Claudia, also designating it for themselves, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-11201215.text_edition: Deo Apollino sic pro salute domini nostri Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti
EDCS-11201215.translation: To the god Apollo, for the safety of our lord Gordian Pius Felix Augustus...
EDCS-11201215.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Apollo, made for the health and safety of the emperor Gordian III (Pius Felix Augustus), likely dating to 238-244 AD.

EDCS-11201220.translation: To Hercules the Invincible, Saturninus gave this gift.
EDCS-11201220.summary: This inscription records a votive offering or gift dedicated by Saturninus to Hercules the Invincible.

EDCS-11201224.text_edition: Lucii Flavii Proculus et Constans patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201224.translation: Lucius Flavius Proculus and Constans placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-11201224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a well-deserving father, set up by his sons Lucius Flavius Proculus and Constans.

EDCS-11201226.text_edition: Deo Tatoni Patrio Lucius Petilius Aurelianus miles legionis VII Claudiae votum libens posuit
EDCS-11201226.translation: To the ancestral god Taton, Lucius Petilius Aurelianus, soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201226.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the local ancestral god Taton by Lucius Petilius Aurelianus, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia.

EDCS-11201228.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Andia rarissima vixit annis LXV hic sita est sit illi terra levis Rufria Amabilis matri pientissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201228.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpia Andia, most rare, lived 65 years. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon her. Rufria Amabilis had this made for her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-11201228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Andia, aged 65, placed by her daughter Rufria Amabilis.

EDCS-11201230.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli patri patriae et Quinto Herennio Etruscio Messio Decio nobilissimo Caesari et Herenniae Etruscillae coniugi domini nostri Augusti Imperatoris
EDCS-11201230.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the country, and to Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar, and to Herennia Etruscilla, wife of our lord Augustus Imperator.
EDCS-11201230.summary: This is a public dedication to the Emperor Decius, his son Herennius Etruscus (as Caesar), and his wife Herennia Etruscilla, listing their full imperial titles, dating to Decius's reign (249-251 CE).

EDCS-11201232.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto et Caio Vibio Anifino sic Volumniano sic Volusiano Augusto
EDCS-11201232.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, and to Gaius Vibius Afinius Volumnianus Volusianus Augustus.
EDCS-11201232.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, likely dating to the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11201243.translation: From the metal of Imperator Augustus, Quintus Gnorus.
EDCS-11201243.summary: This inscription records the ownership of metal by Quintus Gnorus, associated with the Imperator Augustus, likely indicating an official or imperial source.

EDCS-11201249.text_edition: Deae Bellonae Caius Iulius Longinus decurio quinquennalis ex visu
EDCS-11201249.translation: To the goddess Bellona, Gaius Julius Longinus, quinquennial decurion, in consequence of a vision.
EDCS-11201249.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Gaius Julius Longinus, a quinquennial decurion, to the goddess Bellona, made in response to a vision.

EDCS-11201251.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caelo et Terrae Pontoque Beato ob vindictam fecit Cassius Vict Victor Onesimus aram Scupinorum aedilis decurio votum solvit merito
EDCS-11201251.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Blessed Heaven and Earth and Sea, Cassius Victor Onesimus, aedile and decurion of Scupi, made this altar for vindication and willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-11201251.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Cassius Victor Onesimus, an aedile and decurion of Scupi, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Heaven, Earth, and Sea, to fulfill a vow for some vindication.

EDCS-11201253.text_edition: Deo Libero Patri Marcus Ulpius Iulianus ob inmunitatem!) consecravit consacranorum Marciani et Varidi libertorum votum posuit
EDCS-11201253.translation: To Father Liber, Marcus Ulpius Iulianus consecrated this on account of the immunity granted to his fellow consecrated persons Marcianus and Varidus, freedmen, and made a vow.
EDCS-11201253.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater by Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, made on behalf of the immunity granted to his freedmen, Marcianus and Varidus, who were fellow consecrated individuals.

EDCS-11201256.text_edition: Luci filius Publilia Re Verona con decurio quaestor IIvir col(oniae) Fl(aviae) felicis Dardanorum hoc templum impensa sua faciendum curavit deae Syriae
EDCS-11201256.translation: Lucius, son of Lucius, of the Publilian tribe, from Verona, decurion, quaestor, duumvir of the flourishing colony of Flavia Dardanorum, took care to have this temple built at his own expense for the goddess Syria.
EDCS-11201256.summary: This inscription records that Lucius, a decurion, quaestor, and duumvir from Verona, had a temple built at his own expense for the goddess Syria in the colony of Flavia Dardanorum.

EDCS-11201257.text_edition: Deae Aurelius voto libens posuit NO SEMPRIG et IINFORT
EDCS-11201257.translation: To the goddess Aurelius willingly placed this in accordance with a vow. No Semprig and Infort.
EDCS-11201257.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelius to a goddess, placed willingly in fulfillment of a vow, possibly also mentioning other individuals or terms that are unclear.

EDCS-11201259.text_edition: Publio Septimio Severo Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Arabici Adiabenici et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augustorum nostrorum et fratris nobilissimi Caesaris
EDCS-11201259.translation: To Publius Septimius Severus, to our Augusti, Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Arabicus Adiabenicus and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, and to their most noble Caesar brother.
EDCS-11201259.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, along with Geta (referred to as their 'most noble Caesar brother'), dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11201260.text_edition: Pro salute    Saturninus tribunus militum bis  votum solvit libens  Legio VII Claudia votum libens posuit et Claudius Divus |(centurio)
EDCS-11201260.translation: For the well-being of..., Saturninus, twice a military tribune, willingly fulfilled his vow. The Legio VII Claudia willingly made a vow, and Claudius Divus, a centurion.
EDCS-11201260.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Saturninus, a military tribune, for the well-being of someone unnamed, and notes that Legio VII Claudia and the centurion Claudius Divus also willingly made a vow.

EDCS-11201263.text_edition: Rufriae Maximae matri sanctissimae Libonius Severus vir clarissmus consularis  Scupinorum Ravennatium Ariminensium Vulsinensium Pisaurensium Arretinensium Clusiensium Asisensium Alisciensium Nucerinorum Attidiensium Beneventanorum
EDCS-11201263.translation: To Rufria Maxima, most sacred mother. Libonius Severus, a most distinguished man and consular of Scupi, Ravenna, Ariminum, Vulsinium, Pisaurensis, Arretium, Clusium, Assisium, Aliscium, Nuceria, Attidium, Beneventum.
EDCS-11201263.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the distinguished consular Libonius Severus to his mother, Rufria Maxima, listing the various communities over which he held authority.

EDCS-11201264.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Dis Manibus Florentia serva vilica Furi Octaviani clarissimi viri vixit nnos XXII et Eutychius vixit annos XI et Patamilla vixit annos V hic siti sunt Anicetus pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201264.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To the Spirits of the Departed, Florentia, a slave and estate manager of Furius Octavianus, a most distinguished man, lived 22 years; and Eutychius lived 11 years; and Patamilla lived 5 years. They are buried here. Anicetus, their father, took care to have this made.
EDCS-11201264.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Florentia, a slave and estate manager, and two children, Eutychius and Patamilla, buried together, with Anicetus, their father, commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11201265.text_edition: Marco Insteio Marci filio Scaptia Valenti primo pilo praefecto legionis III Augustae
EDCS-11201265.translation: To Marcus Insteius Valens, son of Marcus, of the Scaptian tribe, chief centurion, prefect of the Third Augustan Legion.
EDCS-11201265.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Insteius Valens, honoring his military career as a chief centurion and prefect of Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-11201266.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Marinus decurio Scupinorum vixit annis LXVIII hic situs est Marcilli ValerKii#Valerii#VALEKII Vitalis eques Romanus et Marinus miles legionis VII Claudiae patri inconparabili sic
EDCS-11201266.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Valerius Marinus, decurion of Scupi, lived 68 years. He is buried here. Marcius Valerius Vitalis, Roman knight, and Marinus, soldier of Legio VII Claudia, [made this] for their incomparable father.
EDCS-11201266.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Marcus Valerius Marinus, a decurion of Scupi, by his sons, Marcius Valerius Vitalis (a Roman knight) and Marinus (a soldier of Legio VII Claudia).

EDCS-11201267.text_edition: Tito Turranio Luci filio Sergia Proculo Flanona primipilo praefecto castrorum legionis XIIII geminae IIviro quinquennali coloniae Flaviae Scupinorum Lucius Valerius Iustus amico locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-11201267.translation: To Titus Turranius, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, Proculus Flanona, chief centurion, camp prefect of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, duumvir quinquennalis of the Flavian colony of Scupi. Lucius Valerius Iustus gave this place to his friend, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11201267.summary: This inscription records a burial plot granted by decree of the decurions to Titus Turranius Proculus Flanona, a distinguished military officer and municipal magistrate, by his friend Lucius Valerius Iustus.

EDCS-11201268.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Turannis vixit nnos LXX hic sita est Titus  Clementianus eques RK(omanus)#R(omanus)#K nutrici bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201268.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Turannis, who lived for 70 years; she lies here. Titus Clementianus, Roman knight, placed this for his well-deserving wet-nurse.
EDCS-11201268.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Clementianus, a Roman knight, for his wet-nurse Turannis, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-11201269.text_edition: tonius Marci filius Maecia Pelagonia Marius veteranus cohortis II praetoriae centuria Valeri Crispini testamento fieri iussit Symphorus libertus faciendum curavit hic situs est
EDCS-11201269.translation: Antonius, son of Marcus, of the Maecian tribe, Pelagonia Marius, veteran of the Second Praetorian Cohort, of the century of Valerius Crispinus, ordered this to be made by his will. Symphorus, his freedman, oversaw its construction. He lies here.
EDCS-11201269.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pelagonia Marius, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, whose monument was erected by his freedman Symphorus according to Marius' will.

EDCS-11201270.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulia Victorina viva sibi et Caio Valerio PudeInti#Pudenti#PUDINTI veterano ex praetorio et Caio Iulio Celeri qui militat praetorianus filiis bene merentibus et Caio Valerio Maximo Augustali coloniae ScEupinorum#Scupinorum#SEUP generi sic bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201270.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julia Victorina, while alive, had this made for herself and for Gaius Valerius Pudeinti, veteran from the Praetorian Guard, and for Gaius Julius Celer, who serves as a Praetorian, her well-deserving sons, and for Gaius Valerius Maximus, Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, her well-deserving son-in-law.
EDCS-11201270.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Julia Victorina for herself, her two sons (one a Praetorian veteran, the other serving), and her son-in-law, a municipal priest in Scupi.

EDCS-11201271.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Terentio Terentiano |(centurioni) legionum qui vixit annis LII hic situs est Iulia Didyme coniugi bene merenti faciendum curavit curante Terente Macrino liberto
EDCS-11201271.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Terentius Terentianus, centurion of legions, who lived for 52 years; he lies here. Julia Didyme, his well-deserving wife, had this made, with Terentius Macrinus, his freedman, overseeing its construction.
EDCS-11201271.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Julia Didyme to her husband, Marcus Terentius Terentianus, a centurion, with the construction supervised by his freedman Terentius Macrinus.

EDCS-11201273.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Mestrianus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae vixit annis L hic situs est Aelia Respecta coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201273.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Mestrianus, veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, lived for 50 years; he lies here. Aelia Respecta placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11201273.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aelia Respecta for her husband, Aurelius Mestrianus, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion.

EDCS-11201274.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Luci filius Galeria Galenus Luco veteranus legionis IIII Macedonicae vixit annos LV militavit annos XXVIII hic situs est heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201274.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, Galenus Luco, veteran of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, lived for 55 years, served for 28 years; he lies here. His heir had this made.
EDCS-11201274.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Valerius Galenus Luco, a veteran of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-11201275.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai filius Voltinia Velox domo Luco Vocontiorum veteranus legionis V Alaudae militavit nnos XXXV vixit annos LX Caius Iulius Valens heres faciendum curavit hic situs est
EDCS-11201275.translation: Gaius Julius, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe, Velox, from Lucus Vocontiorum, veteran of the Fifth Alaudae Legion, served for 35 years, lived for 60 years. Gaius Julius Valens, his heir, had this made. He lies here.
EDCS-11201275.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Julius Velox, a veteran of the Fifth Alaudae Legion, which was erected by his heir, Gaius Julius Valens.

EDCS-11201276.text_edition: Marcus Octavius Marci filius Aemilia Valens Stobis miles legionis V Macedonicae decurio pontifex coloniae Flaviae Felicis Dardanorum vivos sic fecit sibi Octaviae Marcellinae filiae suae defunctae vixit annis XXVI et Catiae Secundae coniugi suae hic se vivo faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201276.translation: Marcus Octavius, son of Marcus, of the Aemilian tribe, Valens, from Stobi, soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, decurion and pontiff of the Flavian Felix Dardanorum colony, made this while alive for himself, and for Octavia Marcellina, his deceased daughter, who lived for 26 years, and for Catia Secunda, his wife. He had this made while he was alive.
EDCS-11201276.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Marcus Octavius Valens, a soldier, decurion, and pontiff, while he was alive, for himself, his deceased daughter, and his wife.

EDCS-11201277.text_edition: Lucius Apuleius Luci filius Scaptia Valens Berua veteranus legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnos L militavit nnos XXII hic situs est heredes faciendum curaverunt Maxima filia et Hesperis liberta itemque coniunx
EDCS-11201277.translation: Lucius Apuleius, son of Lucius, of the Scaptian tribe, Valens, from Beroea, veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived for 50 years, served for 22 years; he lies here. His heirs, Maxima his daughter and Hesperis his freedwoman and also wife, had this made.
EDCS-11201277.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Apuleius Valens, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, erected by his daughter Maxima and his freedwoman and wife Hesperis.

EDCS-11201278.text_edition: Caio Cornelio Cai filio Fabia Magno Beryto vixit annis LXXV militavit annis XXI in legione VII Claudia pia fideli beneficiarius et inmunis decurio et IIvir coloniae Scupinorum hic situs est Publicia Secunda uxor ex testamento faciendum curavit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-11201278.translation: To Gaius Cornelius, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, Magnus, from Berytus, who lived 75 years and served 21 years in the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis as a beneficiarius and immunis, decurion and duovir of the colonia of Scupi. He is buried here. Publicia Secunda, his wife, had this monument built according to his will. This monument shall not pass to his heir.
EDCS-11201278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Cornelius Magnus, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia and a municipal official in Scupi, erected by his wife Publicia Secunda according to his will, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201279.text_edition: Lucius Marcianius Amandus domo SalBonis#Salonis#SABONIS veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit annis XXII beneficiarius Marci Valeri Etrusci legati consularis quaestor IIviLr#IIvir#IIVLR coloniae Fl(aviae) fel(icis) D vixit annis LX titulum fE(ieri)#f(ieri)#E iussit hic situs est Lucius Marcianius Probatus Lucius Marcianius Successus libDerti#liberti#LIDE faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201279.translation: Lucius Marcianius Amandus, from Salona, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, served 22 years as a beneficiarius to Marcus Valerius Etruscus, a consular legate, and was a quaestor and duovir of the colonia Flavia Felix D. He lived 60 years and ordered this titulus to be made. He is buried here. Lucius Marcianius Probatus and Lucius Marcianius Successus, his freedmen, had it built.
EDCS-11201279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Marcianius Amandus, a veteran, beneficiarius, and municipal official, commissioned by him and erected by his freedmen, likely from the the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201280.text_edition: Titus Abudius Titi filius Velina Priscus Aquileia veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis vixit nnos LXXXV hic situs est Felicula coniunx faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201280.translation: Titus Abudius, son of Titus, of the Velina tribe, Priscus, from Aquileia, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, lived 85 years. He is buried here. Felicula, his wife, had this built.
EDCS-11201280.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Abudius Priscus, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, erected by his wife Felicula, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201281.text_edition: Publius Caetennius Publi filius Clemens Salonitanus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit annis XXI vixit annis LXX hic situs est Publius Caetennius Felicianus libertus et heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201281.translation: Publius Caetennius, son of Publius, Clemens, from Salona, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, served 21 years and lived 70 years. He is buried here. Publius Caetennius Felicianus, his freedman and heir, had this built.
EDCS-11201281.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Caetennius Clemens, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, erected by his freedman and heir Publius Caetennius Felicianus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201282.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Pontico veterano legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis deductus Scupos vixit nnos LX hic situs est in praedio suo iugerum  in quadratum Iulia Pontinia
EDCS-11201282.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Julius Ponticus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, settled in Scupi, who lived 60 years. He is buried here on his own property, one square iugerum. Iulia Pontinia.
EDCS-11201282.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Gaius Julius Ponticus, a veteran settled in Scupi, with his burial plot noted, likely erected by Iulia Pontinia, from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201284.text_edition: Claudia Maxumus sic Curico veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XX vixit nnos LX Fortunata et Rhoios liberti heredes titulum faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201284.translation: Claudia Maxumus, also Curico, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, with 20 years of service, lived 60 years. Fortunata and Rhoios, his freedmen and heirs, had this titulus built.
EDCS-11201284.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Maxumus (also known as Curico), a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, erected by his freedmen and heirs Fortunata and Rhoios, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201285.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Valeria Valentina Tiberius Claudius Hermes maritus vivo sic se sic Flavio Turboni nepoti militi cohortis singularium bene merenti posuerunt hic situs est
EDCS-11201285.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Valentina and Tiberius Claudius Hermes, her husband, placed this while he was alive, also for Flavius Turbo, their grandson, a soldier of the cohortes singulares, who deserved well. He is buried here.
EDCS-11201285.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Manes for Flavius Turbo, a soldier of the cohortes singulares, placed by his grandparents Valeria Valentina and Tiberius Claudius Hermes during the latter's lifetime, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201286.text_edition: Iulius Vanno missicius ex ala Flavia militavit annis XXIII vixit annis XXXXV hic situs est Flavius Antiocus heres faciundum curavit
EDCS-11201286.translation: Julius Vanno, a discharged soldier from the Flavian cavalry wing, served 23 years and lived 45 years. He is buried here. Flavius Antiocus, his heir, had this built.
EDCS-11201286.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Vanno, a discharged cavalryman, erected by his heir Flavius Antiocus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201288.text_edition: Tito Statilio Titi filio Tauro Quirina Faustiniano ornato a splendidissimo ordine Scupinorum ornamentis
EDCS-11201288.translation: To Titus Statilius Taurus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, Faustinianus, honored by the most splendid order of Scupi with ornaments...
EDCS-11201288.summary: This is a fragmentary public dedication honoring Titus Statilius Taurus Faustinianus, an individual recognized with honors by the city council of Scupi, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201289.text_edition: praefectus fabrum quaestor IIvir pontifex quinquennalis qui vixit nnos LIIII menses VIII dies XXIII
EDCS-11201289.translation: ...prefect of engineers, quaestor, duovir, pontifex, quinquennial, who lived 54 years, 8 months, 23 days.
EDCS-11201289.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription detailing the extensive municipal career (prefect of engineers, quaestor, duovir, pontifex, quinquennial) and age of an unnamed individual, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11201290.text_edition: Caio Sempronio Cai filio Quirina Lupo decurioni quaestori IIviro coloniae Scupinorum et Terentiae Quinti filiae Quintae parentibus optimis Caius Sempronius Taurus decurio IIvir coloniae Scupinorum
EDCS-11201290.translation: To Gaius Sempronius Lupus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, decurion, quaestor, duumvir of the colony of Scupi, and to Terentia Quinta, daughter of Quintus, excellent parents, Gaius Sempronius Taurus, decurion, duumvir of the colony of Scupi (set this up).
EDCS-11201290.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Sempronius Taurus, a decurion and duumvir of Scupi, to his parents, Gaius Sempronius Lupus and Terentia Quinta, both prominent citizens of the colony.

EDCS-11201292.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Cati qui et Felix sic decurionis qaestoris coloniae Scupinorum vixit nnos CL sic
EDCS-11201292.translation: To the Divine Manes of Gaius Catus, also known as Felix, thus, a decurion and quaestor of the colony of Scupi. He lived 150 years. Thus.
EDCS-11201292.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Catus, also known as Felix, a decurion and quaestor of the colony of Scupi, noting his advanced age of 150 years.

EDCS-11201293.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Glauce vixit nnos XXVIII hic sita est Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus uxori rarissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201293.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Glauce lived 28 years. She lies here. Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, honored with decurional ornaments, had this made for his most rare wife.
EDCS-11201293.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Glauce, aged 28, erected by her husband Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, a man of decurional rank, in memory of his exceptional wife.

EDCS-11201294.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Atilius Hilarus IIIIIIvir Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit nnos XXXV hic situs est Lucius Atilius Eucharistus pater
EDCS-11201294.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Atilius Hilarus, Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, lived 35 years. He lies here. Lucius Atilius Eucharistus, his father (set this up).
EDCS-11201294.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Atilius Eucharistus to his son, Lucius Atilius Hilarus, an Augustalis of Scupi, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-11201295.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Iulius Servandus IIIIIIvir Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum qui vixit nnos LXX hic situs defunctus Titus Iulius Servandus filius faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201295.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Iulius Servandus, Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, who lived 70 years. He lies here, deceased. Titus Iulius Servandus, his son, had this made.
EDCS-11201295.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titus Iulius Servandus the Younger for his father, Titus Iulius Servandus the Elder, an Augustalis of Scupi, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-11201296.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Publicius Tertius IIIIIIvir Augustalis se vivo sibi et Statuleae Placidae coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201296.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Publicius Tertius, Augustalis, placed this for himself while alive, and for Statulea Placida, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201296.summary: This is a funerary monument placed by Gaius Publicius Tertius, an Augustalis, for himself during his lifetime and for his deserving wife, Statulea Placida.

EDCS-11201297.text_edition: Dis Manibus      IIIIIIvir Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit nnos L hic situs est  Valeria Cai filia Asclepiodote nnorum L viva se sibi et infelici marito faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201297.translation: To the Divine Manes. [---], Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, lived 50 years. He lies here. Valeria, daughter of Gaius, Asclepiodote, 50 years old, while alive, had this made for herself and her unfortunate husband.
EDCS-11201297.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed Augustalis of Scupi, who lived 50 years, and was set up by his wife, Valeria Asclepiodote, for herself and her husband while she was still alive.

EDCS-11201298.text_edition: Marus Ellius Marci libertus Phoebus Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit annos LXXV hic situs est heredes faciendum curaverunt Surus Ianuarius Nereus
EDCS-11201298.translation: Marcus Ellius Phoebus, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, lived 75 years. He lies here. His heirs Surus, Ianuarius, and Nereus had this made.
EDCS-11201298.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ellius Phoebus, a freedman and Augustalis of Scupi, who died at 75 years old, erected by his heirs Surus, Ianuarius, and Nereus.

EDCS-11201299.text_edition: Lib  Crescentilla vixit annos XV hic sita est Caius Lib Crescens Augustalis filiae piae faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201299.translation: Liberta Crescentilla lived 15 years. She lies here. Gaius Lib(ertus) Crescens, Augustalis, had this made for his dutiful daughter.
EDCS-11201299.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Lib. Crescens, an Augustalis, for his pious daughter, Liberta Crescentilla, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-11201301.text_edition: Publius Aebutius Proclus vixit annis L et Publius Aebutius Proclianus vixit nnos II hic siti sunt Fabricia Lucilia coniunx et Publius Aebutius Rufus et Publius Aebutius Valens patri pientissimo faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201301.translation: Publius Aebutius Proclus lived 50 years, and Publius Aebutius Proclianus lived 2 years. They lie here. Fabricia Lucilia, their wife/mother, and Publius Aebutius Rufus and Publius Aebutius Valens, had this made for their most devoted father.
EDCS-11201301.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aebutius Proclus, who lived 50 years, and Publius Aebutius Proclianus, who lived 2 years, erected by Fabricia Lucilia (likely his wife and mother of the child) and their sons Publius Aebutius Rufus and Publius Aebutius Valens, for their devoted father.

EDCS-11201302.text_edition: Publius Aebutius Rufus vixit annis XLV hic situs est Publius Aebutius Proclianus patri pientissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201302.translation: Publius Aebutius Rufus lived for 45 years; he is buried here. Publius Aebutius Proclianus saw to the erection (of this monument) for his most devoted father.
EDCS-11201302.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Aebutius Proclianus for his father, Publius Aebutius Rufus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-11201303.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Maxima vixit nnos L hic sita est ius coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201303.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Maxima lived for 50 years; she is buried here. Her husband erected this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201303.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Maxima, who lived to be 50, erected by her husband.

EDCS-11201304.text_edition: Publius Aelius Publi filius Posidonianus vixit annos XVI menses VIIII dies VIIII hic situs est Antonia Saturnina avia memor brevis pietatis faciendum curavit ὅν οἱ θεοὶ φιλοῦσιν οὗτος ἀποθνήσκει νέος
EDCS-11201304.translation: Publius Aelius Posidonianus, son of Publius, lived for 16 years, 9 months, and 9 days; he is buried here. Antonia Saturnina, his grandmother, in memory of his brief devotion, saw to the erection (of this monument). Whom the gods love, that one dies young.
EDCS-11201304.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Posidonianus, a young boy who died at 16, erected by his grandmother Antonia Saturnina, and includes a Greek proverb.

EDCS-11201305.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Afrodisia vixit nnos XXXXIII hic sita est Numerius vixit nnos LX hic situs est sibi et coniugi posuit Numerius haec sunt
EDCS-11201305.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Afrodisia lived for 43 years; she is buried here. Numerius lived for 60 years; he is buried here. Numerius erected this for himself and his wife. These are...
EDCS-11201305.summary: This is a joint funerary inscription erected by Numerius for himself and his wife Afrodisia, recording their respective lifespans of 60 and 43 years.

EDCS-11201306.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Ambia Longinia qui vixit annos LXXX hic sita est VibVius#Vibius#VIVIUS Valentis maritus coniugi incomNparabili#incomparabili#INCONPARABILI posuit
EDCS-11201306.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ambia Longinia, who lived for 80 years; she is buried here. Vibius Valens, her husband, erected this for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-11201306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ambia Longinia, who lived to the age of 80, set up by her husband Vibius Valens.

EDCS-11201307.text_edition: Dis Manibus Annaea Clementilla vixit nnos XL hic sita est vicus Cavadinus faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201307.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Annaea Clementilla lived for 40 years; she is buried here. The district of Cavadinus saw to the erection (of this monument).
EDCS-11201307.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annaea Clementilla, who lived for 40 years, which was erected by the vicus Cavadinus (Cavadinus district).

EDCS-11201308.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Antonia Procula vixit annos XX hic sita est Lucius Valerius Firmus coniugi dulcissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201308.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Procula lived for 20 years; she is buried here. Lucius Valerius Firmus saw to the erection (of this monument) for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-11201308.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Procula, who died at the age of 20, erected by her husband Lucius Valerius Firmus.

EDCS-11201309.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Ateius Vitalis vixit annos XVII hic situs est Fabricia Iulia filio carissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201309.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Ateius Vitalis lived for 17 years; he is buried here. Fabricia Iulia saw to the erection (of this monument) for her dearest son.
EDCS-11201309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Ateius Vitalis, who died at 17, erected by his mother Fabricia Iulia.

EDCS-11201312.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Lucius Attius Severus vixit annis XXXX Valeria Cornelia Lucius Attius Severus et Lucius Severinus patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201312.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Attius Severus lived for 40 years. Valeria Cornelia, Lucius Attius Severus, and Lucius Severinus erected this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-11201312.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Attius Severus, who died at 40, erected by his family: Valeria Cornelia, Lucius Attius Severus (likely his son), and Lucius Severinus.

EDCS-11201313.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aufidia Victoria vixit annis XXVIII hic sita est Caius Iulius Iulianus coniugi bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201313.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aufidia Victoria lived for 28 years; she is buried here. Caius Iulius Iulianus saw to the erection (of this monument) for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201313.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aufidia Victoria, who lived for 28 years, erected by her husband Caius Iulius Iulianus.

EDCS-11201314.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Valens vixit nnos XX parentes hoc monumentum faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201314.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Valens lived for 20 years. His parents saw to the erection of this monument.
EDCS-11201314.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Aurelius Valens, who died at age 20, erected by his parents.

EDCS-11201315.text_edition: annos XL hic situs est Aurelius   bene merenti
EDCS-11201315.translation: Aurelius lived 40 years; he is buried here. To the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201315.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius, who lived 40 years, indicating his burial place and commending him as well-deserving.

EDCS-11201316.text_edition: Bassus Apolloni filius domo Antiochia vixit annos LV hic situs est Lucilia Varena faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201316.translation: Bassus, son of Apollo, from Antioch, lived for 55 years. He is interred here. Lucilia Varena saw to its erection.
EDCS-11201316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bassus, son of Apollo, from Antioch, who lived to 55, set up by Lucilia Varena.

EDCS-11201317.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sextus Caecilius Bio vixit annos XXX hic situs est Caecilia Ianuaria patrono bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201317.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Sextus Caecilius Bio lived for 30 years. He is interred here. Caecilia Ianuaria saw to its erection for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-11201317.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Caecilius Bio, who died at 30, erected by his freedwoman Caecilia Ianuaria, likely his former slave, as a patron.

EDCS-11201318.text_edition: Cesus Mestri filius Macedo Apeunenses sic vixit annis LXX hic situs est Delus coniux et Bithus filius Cesunis sic heres bene merito faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201318.translation: Cesus, son of Mestrius, a Macedonian from Apeuna, lived for 70 years. He is interred here. Delus, his wife, and Bithus, son of Cesus, his heir, saw to its erection for their well-deserving loved one.
EDCS-11201318.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cesus, a Macedonian, who lived to 70, set up by his wife Delus and his son and heir Bithus.

EDCS-11201319.text_edition: lia Claudia coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201319.translation: Julia Claudia placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11201319.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Julia Claudia for her well-deserving husband.

EDCS-11201320.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Firmina vixit annos XX hic sita est Claudia Valeria mater infelix posuit bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201320.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Claudia Firmina lived for 20 years. She is interred here. Claudia Valeria, her unhappy mother, placed this for her well-deserving daughter and saw to its erection.
EDCS-11201320.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Firmina, who died at 20, set up by her grieving mother, Claudia Valeria.

EDCS-11201321.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Lucilia vixit annis LX hic sita est et Titus Flavius Fuscus maritus eius se vivo ambis sic posuit
EDCS-11201321.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Claudia Lucilia lived for 60 years. She is interred here. And Titus Flavius Fuscus, her husband, placed this for both of them during his lifetime.
EDCS-11201321.summary: This is a joint funerary inscription for Claudia Lucilia, who lived to 60, and her husband Titus Flavius Fuscus, who set it up for them both while still alive.

EDCS-11201322.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Claudi Crispi filius Quirina Germanus vixit nnos LXV hic situs est Claudia Tuta uxor et Claudius Crispus et Claudia Vera filii faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201322.translation: Tiberius Claudius Germanus, son of Claudius Crispus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 65 years. He is interred here. Claudia Tuta, his wife, and Claudius Crispus and Claudia Vera, his children, saw to its erection.
EDCS-11201322.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Germanus, who lived to 65, erected by his wife Claudia Tuta and his children Claudius Crispus and Claudia Vera.

EDCS-11201323.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cocceia Marcellina vixit annis LX hic sita est Claudia Octavia matri pientissimae posuit  Dis Manibus Tiberius Claudius Aelianus vixit annis XXX hic situs est mater qu=Cod#quod#COD petierat de sic filia sua sublato ante diem et iuniori nato obito Claudia Octavia fratri pientissimo posuit
EDCS-11201323.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Cocceia Marcellina lived for 60 years. She is interred here. Claudia Octavia placed this for her most devoted mother. To the Spirits of the Dead. Tiberius Claudius Aelianus lived for 30 years. He is interred here. His mother, who had requested from her daughter, having been taken prematurely, and for her younger born child having died, Claudia Octavia placed this for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-11201323.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, first for Cocceia Marcellina (60 years old) set up by her daughter Claudia Octavia, and second for Tiberius Claudius Aelianus (30 years old), also set up by Claudia Octavia, his sister.

EDCS-11201324.text_edition: vixit annos XXX Cocceia Luci Lucilia mater filiae piissimae faciendum curavit   vixit annos XXX Cornelius Crescentio coniugi piissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201324.translation: She lived for 30 years. Cocceia Lucilia, daughter of Lucius, her mother, saw to its erection for her most devoted daughter. He lived for 30 years. Cornelius Crescentio saw to its erection for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-11201324.summary: This is a double funerary inscription: one for a daughter (who lived 30 years) by her mother Cocceia Lucilia, and another for a wife (who also lived 30 years) by her husband Cornelius Crescentio.

EDCS-11201325.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Quintus Cornelius Felicio vixit annis LXXX hic situs est Corneficiae Primitibae sic vivae Quintus Cornelius Felicianus parentibus bene merentibus titulum faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201325.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Cornelius Felicio lived for 80 years. He is interred here. Quintus Cornelius Felicianus saw to the erection of this titulus for his well-deserving parents, and for Corneficia Primitiba, who is still alive.
EDCS-11201325.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Cornelius Felicio, who lived to 80, erected by Quintus Cornelius Felicianus for his well-deserving parents, and also for his mother Corneficia Primitiba, who was still alive when the monument was made.

EDCS-11201326.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Cornificia Valeria vixit annos XXIII hic sita est Lucius Cornificius Vitales sic Iulia Valentina filiae piissimae posuerunt hoc tu nobis facere debuisti filia cuam sic nos tibi  Lucius Seius Maternus vixit annos XXVIIII hic situs est Lucius Cornificius Vitales sic Iulia Valentina genero piissimo bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201326.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornificia Valeria lived 23 years; she is buried here. Lucius Cornificius Vitales and Iulia Valentina placed this for their most devoted daughter. You should have done this for us, daughter, just as we should for you. Lucius Seius Maternus lived 29 years; he is buried here. Lucius Cornificius Vitales and Iulia Valentina placed this for their most devoted and well-deserving son-in-law.
EDCS-11201326.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cornificia Valeria and Lucius Seius Maternus, erected by Lucius Cornificius Vitales and Iulia Valentina for their daughter and son-in-law, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201327.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Dionysius Petroni et Iulia Ingenu vixit annos LX a qui vivo sic se posuerunt
EDCS-11201327.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dionysius, freedman of Petronius, and Iulia Ingenua lived 60 years. While living, they placed this for themselves.
EDCS-11201327.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Dionysius, freedman of Petronius, and Iulia Ingenua, who prepared it for themselves during their lifetime, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201328.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Domitio ilio annorum
EDCS-11201328.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Domitius, son of [---]ius, of [---] years.
EDCS-11201328.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Lucius Domitius, with details about his parentage and age being partially illegible.

EDCS-11201329.text_edition: Dis Manibus Drutie Mestulae filia vixit annos LXX Maema Dioscuridis filia vixit annos  hic sitae sunt se vivis sibi faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201329.translation: To the Divine Manes. Drutia Mestula, daughter, lived 70 years. Maema, daughter of Dioscurides, lived years [---]. They are buried here. They saw to its construction for themselves while alive.
EDCS-11201329.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Drutia Mestula and Maema, daughter of Dioscurides, who had the monument prepared for themselves during their lifetimes, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201330.text_edition: Eutycho statuario Corneli Magni vixit annis XXV hic situs est Publicia Secunda bene merenti
EDCS-11201330.translation: For Eutychus, the sculptor of Cornelius Magnus. He lived 25 years; he is buried here. Publicia Secunda placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eutychus, a sculptor, erected by Publicia Secunda for the well-deserving deceased, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201331.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Parthenopes liberta Tyche vixit annos LX Flavia Parthenope nutriculae et libertae bonae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201331.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Parthenope, freedwoman, Tyche, lived 60 years. Flavia Parthenope placed this for her good and well-deserving nurse and freedwoman.
EDCS-11201331.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Parthenope, a freedwoman also known as Tyche, erected by her former patron Flavia Parthenope for her nurse and freedwoman, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201332.text_edition: Flavia Postumina Flavi filia vixit annos XXX Publius Aelius Menodotus coniugi pientissimae bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201332.translation: Flavia Postumina, daughter of Flavius, lived 30 years. Publius Aelius Menodotus saw to its construction for his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201332.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Postumina, erected by Publius Aelius Menodotus for his devoted wife, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201333.text_edition: Flavia Urbana Titi filia vixit nnos XXX hic sita est Caius Iulius Lucretius coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201333.translation: Flavia Urbana, daughter of Titus, lived 30 years; she is buried here. Gaius Iulius Lucretius placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201333.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Urbana, erected by Gaius Iulius Lucretius for his wife, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201334.text_edition: Manibus Dis  Titus Flavius Fyrmus sic hic situs frugiI#frugi#FRUGI Vividae amatae aqua sibi qu=Cod#quod#COD cupierat bonus vir vivens annos LXXV vivo sibi posuit  hic pietatis honor haec sunt pia dona mariti qui me diligit Valeriae coniug(i) vixit annos L Flavius Fyrmus sic coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201334.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Flavius Firmus is buried here. A thrifty, beloved, and lively man, who desired this for himself, a good man, living 75 years, placed this for himself while alive. This is the honor of piety, these are the dutiful gifts of the husband who loves me, for his wife Valeria, who lived 50 years. Flavius Firmus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201334.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, likely a double epitaph, where Titus Flavius Firmus prepared his own monument while alive, and a Flavius Firmus (possibly the same or a different one) also dedicated a monument to his wife Valeria, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201336.text_edition: Fulcinio Euangelo marito et Sempronio Pino et Lupo filiis hic siti sunt Fulcinia Hedyle mater pietatis causa faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201336.translation: For Fulcinius Euangelus, husband, and Sempronius Pinus and Lupus, sons; they are buried here. Fulcinia Hedyle, mother, saw to its construction for the sake of piety.
EDCS-11201336.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fulcinius Euangelus and his sons Sempronius Pinus and Lupus, erected by Fulcinia Hedyle, the mother, out of devotion, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201337.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulia Marcellina vixit annos XXX  Caius Iulius Caianus  et filius vixit annos XVIII pater titulum faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201337.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Marcellina lived 30 years. Gaius Iulius Caianus and son lived 18 years. The father saw to the construction of this titulus.
EDCS-11201337.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Marcellina and an unnamed son of Gaius Iulius Caianus, with the father arranging the monument, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201339.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Iulius Clemens vixit annis LXX hic situs est Marci Iuli Fuscus Valens Valentius patri pientissimo bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201339.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Clemens lived for 70 years; he is buried here. Marci Iuli Fuscus Valens Valentius had this made for their most devoted and deserving father.
EDCS-11201339.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Iulius Clemens, erected by his children Marci Iuli Fuscus Valens Valentius in his honor.

EDCS-11201341.text_edition: Lucilia vixit nnos XX ex testamento fecit matri Seius Proculus pientissimae
EDCS-11201341.translation: Lucilia lived 20 years. Seius Proculus made this by testament for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-11201341.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucilia, who lived 20 years, set up by her son Seius Proculus according to a testament for his very devoted mother.

EDCS-11201342.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Manlius Valentinus comes vixit annos XL menses  dies V horas B
EDCS-11201342.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Manlius Valentinus, a companion, lived 40 years, [---] months, 5 days, 2 hours.
EDCS-11201342.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Manlius Valentinus, a 'comes' (companion), detailing his age at death as 40 years, 5 days, and 2 hours, with months illegible.

EDCS-11201343.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Maria Maxima vixit annos L Marcus Marius Blicities libertus ex testamento eius ponendum curavit et Valerii Sabinianus et Maximianus filii monumentum parenti bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-11201343.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maria Maxima lived for 50 years. Marcus Marius Blicities, her freedman, had this placed according to her will, and Valerii Sabinianus and Maximianus, her sons, made this monument for their deserving parent.
EDCS-11201343.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maria Maxima, erected by her freedman Marcus Marius Blicities based on her will, and also by her sons Valerii Sabinianus and Maximianus.

EDCS-11201345.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mestylas Momoniae coniugi bene merenti titulum posuit
EDCS-11201345.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Mestylas placed this monument for his well-deserving wife Momonia.
EDCS-11201345.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, recording that Mestylas placed the monument for his well-deserving wife, Momonia.

EDCS-11201346.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Numerianus vixit nnos XXV hic situs est Numeria vixit nnos XXXIII hic sita est Afrodisia mater supervixit dies XXX Numerius filios sic acerbos sic posuit
EDCS-11201346.translation: To the Divine Shades. Numerianus lived for 25 years; he is buried here. Numeria lived for 33 years; she is buried here. Afrodisia, their mother, survived them by 30 days. Numerius placed this for his sons, so cruelly lost.
EDCS-11201346.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numerianus and Numeria, likely siblings, erected by their father Numerius, who also records the short survival of their mother Afrodisia.

EDCS-11201347.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Obellius Crescens vixit nnos LXX hic situs est Iulia Eutychia et Obelli Alexander et Herculanus patri et coniugi bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201347.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Obellius Crescens lived for 70 years; he is buried here. Iulia Eutychia and Obelli Alexander and Herculanus had this made for their deserving father and husband.
EDCS-11201347.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Obellius Crescens, erected by his wife Iulia Eutychia and his sons Obelli Alexander and Herculanus.

EDCS-11201349.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Ostilius Valens vixit annis LXXXVII Maxima coniunx Lucius Ostilius Fronto et Lucius Ostilius VMalens#Valens#MALES Lucius Ostilius Clemens bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201349.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Ostilius Valens lived for 87 years. Maxima, his wife, Lucius Ostilius Fronto, Lucius Ostilius Valens, and Lucius Ostilius Clemens placed this for the deserving man.
EDCS-11201349.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Ostilius Valens, erected by his wife Maxima and his three sons Lucius Ostilius Fronto, Lucius Ostilius Valens, and Lucius Ostilius Clemens.

EDCS-11201350.text_edition: Petillia sic Clara vixit annis IIII  Dis Manibus  Lucius Petilius Lupus vixit annis XXV hic situs est Vesidia Ingenua mater filio dulcissimo posuit
EDCS-11201350.translation: Petillia Clara lived for 4 years. To the Divine Shades. Lucius Petilius Lupus lived for 25 years; he is buried here. Vesidia Ingenua, his mother, placed this for her sweetest son.
EDCS-11201350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Petilius Lupus, erected by his mother Vesidia Ingenua, and it also mentions a Petillia Clara who died at the age of four, possibly a sibling.

EDCS-11201351.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Petronius Valens vixit annos L et Aurelia Marcia vixit annos XXXX Petroni Lucia Valens Marcus fili ponendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201351.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Petronius Valens lived for 50 years, and Aurelia Marcia lived for 40 years. Petroni Lucia, Valens, and Marcus, their children, had this placed.
EDCS-11201351.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Petronius Valens and Aurelia Marcia, erected by their children Petroni Lucia, Valens, and Marcus.

EDCS-11201352.text_edition: hic situs est Lucius Petronius Valens filius et Petronia Marcia nepotia pietatis causa posuerunt
EDCS-11201352.translation: Lucius Petronius Valens, his son, and Petronia Marcia, his granddaughter, placed this here out of piety.
EDCS-11201352.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed individual, erected by their son Lucius Petronius Valens and granddaughter Petronia Marcia out of filial piety.

EDCS-11201353.text_edition: Posis Mestylu sic filius Flavia Delus Mucati filia domo Albanopoli ipsa Delus
EDCS-11201353.translation: Mestylus, son of Posis, and Flavia Delus, daughter of Mucatus, from Albanopolis, Delus herself.
EDCS-11201353.summary: This inscription likely records the names and family origins of individuals, Mestylus and Flavia Delus, specifying Delus's origin from Albanopolis.

EDCS-11201354.text_edition: Quintus Satrius Ferox vixit annis III hic situs est Saturninus et Felicula faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201354.translation: Quintus Satrius Ferox lived for 3 years; he is buried here. Saturninus and Felicula had this made.
EDCS-11201354.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Satrius Ferox, who died at the age of three, erected by Saturninus and Felicula, likely his parents.

EDCS-11201355.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Sollius Primus vixit annos XXIII hic situs est Quintus Sollius Surus nepoti bene merenti fecit
EDCS-11201355.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Sollius Primus lived for 23 years; he is buried here. Quintus Sollius Surus made this for his deserving grandson.
EDCS-11201355.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sollius Primus, who died at 23, erected by his grandfather Quintus Sollius Surus.

EDCS-11201356.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Teufia Nice Teufi Valeri liberta vixit annos LXXX hic sita est Teufia Valeria quae et Ingenua filia faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201356.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Teufia Nice, freedwoman of Teufius Valerius, lived 80 years; she lies here. Teufia Valeria, also known as Ingenua, her daughter, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-11201356.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Teufia Nice and was set up by her daughter, Teufia Valeria Ingenua, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201357.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Semele vixit annis XL Epaphroditus coniugi
EDCS-11201357.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Semele lived for 40 years. Epaphroditus to his wife.
EDCS-11201357.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by Epaphroditus to his wife, Valeria Semele, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-11201358.text_edition: Valerius Faustus vixit nnos II et  Aelia ChryUsis#Chrysis#CRUSIS filio pientissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201358.translation: Valerius Faustus lived 2 years. Aelia Chrysis arranged for this to be made for her most devoted son.
EDCS-11201358.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Valerius Faustus, dedicated by his mother, Aelia Chrysis, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luci Valeri Pudentis qui vixit nnos XXV et Luci Valeri Aeliani qui vixit nnos VI Lucius Valerius Peculiaris et Vivia Sabina parentes
EDCS-11201359.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. For Lucius Valerius Pudens, who lived 25 years, and for Lucius Valerius Aelianus, who lived 6 years. Lucius Valerius Peculiaris and Vivia Sabina, their parents.
EDCS-11201359.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Valerius Peculiaris and Vivia Sabina to their two sons, Lucius Valerius Pudens and Lucius Valerius Aelianus, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201362.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Venuleius Arator vixit nnos LV Valeria Restuta vixit annos XL hic siti sunt Gai Venulei Herculanus Persinus Silvanus fili parentibus pientissimis faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201362.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Venuleius Arator lived 55 years. Valeria Restuta lived 40 years. They lie here. Gaius Venuleius Herculanus, Persinus, and Silvanus, their sons, arranged for this to be made for their most devoted parents.
EDCS-11201362.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Venuleius Arator and Valeria Restuta, set up by their sons, Gaius Venuleius Herculanus, Persinus, and Silvanus, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201363.text_edition: Dis Manibus V Hyperephanus vixit annos XXXI hic situs est Tyche coniugi karissimo ponendum c(uravit)
EDCS-11201363.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. V. Hyperephanus lived 31 years; he lies here. Tyche arranged for this to be placed for her dearest husband.
EDCS-11201363.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hyperephanus, dedicated by his wife Tyche, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201364.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Mamae vixit annos XXXV Caius Ulpius Aphroditus coniugi bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201364.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. For Ulpia Mama, who lived 35 years. Gaius Ulpius Aphroditus arranged for this to be made for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201364.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Ulpius Aphroditus to his wife, Ulpia Mama, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201365.text_edition: sanius ercus vixit nnos XXXII hic situs est sania genia  pientissimo  posuit
EDCS-11201365.translation: Sanius Ercus lived for 32 years; he is buried here. Sania Genia placed this for her most devoted (husband).
EDCS-11201365.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Sanius Ercus, aged 32, placed by Sania Genia, likely his wife.

EDCS-11201371.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with very little legible text, preserving parts of a name and a few letters, making its purpose unclear.

EDCS-11201374.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Οὐλπία Δομιτία ΟΥΕΙ νιxιτ annic sic Αὐρήλιους Διονύσιους χωειουγι βενε μερεντι
EDCS-11201374.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Ulpia Domitia lived [---] years. Aurelius Dionysius for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201374.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Domitia, dedicated by Aurelius Dionysius for his wife, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD, with some text in Greek.

EDCS-11201379.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII consuli III Lucio Coelio Rufo legato Augusti pro praetore colonia Scupinorum milia passuum VIIII
EDCS-11201379.translation: To Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the third time. Lucius Coelius Rufus, legate of Augustus pro praetore. From the colony of the Scupini, nine miles.
EDCS-11201379.summary: This is a milestone or public inscription from the reign of Hadrian (c. 120 AD, given his fourth tribunician power and third consulship), recording the distance from the colony of Scupi and mentioning the imperial legate Lucius Coelius Rufus.

EDCS-11201380.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani nepos divi Nervae pronepos Antoninus Augustus Pius restituit milia passuum VIIII
EDCS-11201380.translation: Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan, great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Antoninus Augustus Pius, restored nine miles.
EDCS-11201380.summary: This is a milestone or public inscription from the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 AD), recording a restoration project over a distance of nine miles.

EDCS-11201381.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto Parthico Maximo tribunicia potestate et Otaciliae Sseverae Augustae mille passus
EDCS-11201381.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, with tribunician power, and to Otacilia Severa Augusta, one thousand paces.
EDCS-11201381.summary: This is a milestone or public inscription from the reign of Emperor Philip the Arab (244-249 AD), also mentioning his wife Otacilia Severa, recording a distance of one mile.

EDCS-11201384.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto patri patriae consuli semel res publica coloniae Scupinorum milia passus sic XX
EDCS-11201384.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul for the first time. The Republic of the colony of Scupini (erected this) for 20 miles.
