EDCS-11201384.summary: This inscription is a milestone or honorary dedication from the res publica of Scupini to Emperor Trajan Decius, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201387.translation: To Apollo, in fulfillment of a vow, Festus (dedicated this).
EDCS-11201387.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Apollo by Festus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11201388.text_edition: Deo Invicto pro  salute Auggustorum nnostrorum templum vetustate dilapsum imNpendio#impendio#INPENDIO suo restituit Apollonides eorundem servus scrutator stationis Lamud Gentiano et Basso consulibus
EDCS-11201388.translation: To the Invincible God, for the welfare of our Augusti, Apollonides, their slave and searcher of the Lamud station, restored this temple, which had fallen into ruin due to age, at his own expense, when Gentianus and Bassus were consuls.
EDCS-11201388.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a dilapidated temple by Apollonides, a slave and official, dedicated to an Invincible God (likely Mithras) for the health of the emperors, dated to 211 AD.

EDCS-11201389.text_edition: Fano Magno Severus sacerdos votum posuit ut antiquam legem Augusti servemus
EDCS-11201389.translation: To Fano Magnus, Severus, a priest, placed this vow so that we may observe the ancient law of Augustus.
EDCS-11201389.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by the priest Severus to Fano Magnus, expressing a commitment to uphold an ancient law established by Augustus.

EDCS-11201390.text_edition: Pro salute Auggustorum Valeri Valentis ex voto posuit Epicadus sacerdos praedicabilis antiquam legem sequamur
EDCS-11201390.translation: For the welfare of the Augusti, Valerius Valens, according to his vow, erected this. Epicadus, a praiseworthy priest: 'Let us follow the ancient law.'
EDCS-11201390.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Valerius Valens for the health of the emperors, fulfilling a vow, with an added exhortation from a priest named Epicadus, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201391.text_edition: Deo invicto Mithrae fano magno pro salute Auggustorum nn(ostrorum) Apollonides eorundem vectigalis Illyrici servus |contrascriptor stationis Lamud quam voverat |contrascriptor stationis Viziani  Apollonidess Viziani  Gentiano et Basso consulibus
EDCS-11201391.translation: To the Invincible God Mithras, in the great sanctuary, for the welfare of our Augusti, Apollonides, their slave, tax collector of Illyricum, and counter-scribe of the Lamud station, erected what he had vowed. Apollonides, counter-scribe of the Viziani station, when Gentianus and Bassus were consuls.
EDCS-11201391.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Apollonides, a slave and imperial official, who dedicated a monument or offering to Mithras for the well-being of the emperors, dated to 211 AD.

EDCS-11201392.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Publius Aelius Ballista eques Romanus a militiis pro se suisque votum solvit
EDCS-11201392.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, and Minerva, Publius Aelius Ballista, a Roman knight from the military, fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-11201392.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Publius Aelius Ballista, a Roman knight, to the Capitoline Triad (Jupiter, Juno, Minerva) on behalf of himself and his household, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201397.text_edition: NK S Gemelinus Dardanos fEines#fines#EINES derexit Q
EDCS-11201397.translation: NK S Gemelinus Dardanos directed the boundaries/end.
EDCS-11201397.summary: This inscription, despite some unusual orthography, seems to record Gemelinus Dardanos as the person who established or marked boundaries.

EDCS-11201398.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Titus Flavius Augustalis vixit annos LXXX hic situs est sodales Platiae Superae per stinum  libertum bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201398.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Flavius Augustalis lived 80 years. He lies here. The members of the Sodales Platiae Superae, through his freedman Stinus, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201398.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Titus Flavius Augustalis, who lived 80 years, erected by the Sodales Platiae Superae through his freedman Stinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201399.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iucunda Bithi filia vixit nnos L hic sita est Abascantos sic libertus patronae bene merenti posuit Abascantus Iucundae libertus vivo se
EDCS-11201399.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iucunda, daughter of Bithus, lived 50 years. She lies here. Abascantus, her freedman, placed this for his well-deserving patroness. Abascantus, freedman of Iucunda, (did this) for himself while living.
EDCS-11201399.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iucunda, daughter of Bithus, erected by her freedman Abascantus, who also prepared a space for himself, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201400.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius Caius vixit annos L hic situs est sodales Platiae Superae per Iulium Castum filium bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201400.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Caius lived 50 years. He lies here. The members of the Sodales Platiae Superae, through his son Iulius Castus, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201400.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Iulius Caius, who lived 50 years, erected by the Sodales Platiae Superae through his son Iulius Castus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201401.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius LiEbonius#Libonius#LEBONIUS Catus vixit nnos L hic situs est Libonia
EDCS-11201401.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Libonius Catus lived 50 years. He lies here. Libonia (erected this).
EDCS-11201401.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Libonius Catus, who lived 50 years, erected by Libonia, likely his relative, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201402.text_edition: Publicia Callirhoe vixit annos L hic sita est Lucius Viracius Fortis coniugi faciendum curavit
EDCS-11201402.translation: Publicia Callirhoe lived 50 years. She lies here. Lucius Viracius Fortis had this made for his wife.
EDCS-11201402.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Callirhoe, who lived 50 years, commissioned by her husband Lucius Viracius Fortis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201403.translation: Tetricus oversaw the making (of this) for his mother.
EDCS-11201403.summary: This inscription records that Tetricus was responsible for the creation of a monument or object in honor of his mother.

EDCS-11201404.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Varanus miles legionis IIII Flaviae librarius consularis vixitX#vixit#VIXIX annis XXXV  StatLius#Statius#STALIUS   NALE  bene merenti
EDCS-11201404.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Valerius Varanus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, a consular clerk, lived for 35 years. Statius Nale (set this up) for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201404.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Statius Nale to Marcus Valerius Varanus, a soldier and consular clerk of the Fourth Flavian Legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201405.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Viracius Fortis vixit nnos LXX hic situs est Lucius Viracius Fortis et Viracia Ingenua patri bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201405.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Viracius Fortis lived for 70 years; he lies here. Lucius Viracius Fortis and Viracia Ingenua took care of having this made for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-11201405.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Viracius Fortis and Viracia Ingenua for their father, Lucius Viracius Fortis, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-11201406.text_edition: cum qua conubio vixit non plus quam quos miseros citius fato mors  et miseros liquit genitores
EDCS-11201406.translation: With whom he lived in marriage, not more than whom unhappy ones death quickly left by fate, and it left unhappy parents.
EDCS-11201406.summary: This appears to be a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely part of a poetic lament, recording the briefness of a marriage and the sorrow of surviving parents.

EDCS-11201409.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, preserving a partial name, Pudens, and indicating that the individual's age at death was recorded.

EDCS-11201410.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Bellus |(centurio) cohortis primae Thr=RHacum#Thracum#TRHACUM veteranus cohortis eiusdem vixit annis LX hic situs est  Iulius Gallus Lumia Andia Dasi filia fratres et heredes mei faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201410.translation: Marcus Iulius Bellus, centurion of the First Cohort of Thracians, a veteran of the same cohort, lived for 60 years; he lies here. Iulius Gallus and Lumia Andia, daughter of Dasus, my siblings and heirs, took care of having this made.
EDCS-11201410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Bellus, a centurion and veteran of the First Cohort of Thracians, set up by his siblings and heirs, Iulius Gallus and Lumia Andia.

EDCS-11201411.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pamphorus hic situs est vixit annos XVI Heracla et Zoe et Pamphorus cognominato posuerunt
EDCS-11201411.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pamphorus lies here; he lived for 16 years. Heracla and Zoe and Pamphorus (the one) surnamed placed this.
EDCS-11201411.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young individual named Pamphorus, who died at 16, erected by Heracla, Zoe, and another person named Pamphorus.

EDCS-11201417.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Maximus Timentis hic situs est vixit annos LX
EDCS-11201417.translation: Marcus Ulpius Maximus, son of Timentis, is buried here. He lived for 60 years.
EDCS-11201417.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Marcus Ulpius Maximus, son of Timentis, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-11201418.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Flavia Severa vixit annos XXXV Marcus Ulpius Bassus miles legionis IIII Flaviae Antoninianae strator legati coniugi pientissimae posuit manu mea scribsi sic
EDCS-11201418.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Severa lived for 35 years. Marcus Ulpius Bassus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Antoninian Legion, orderly of the legate, placed this for his most devoted wife. I wrote this with my own hand.
EDCS-11201418.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Severa, who died at 35, set up by her devoted husband Marcus Ulpius Bassus, a soldier and legate's orderly of the Fourth Flavian Antoninian Legion, notably inscribed by his own hand.

EDCS-11201419.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Maximianus vixit annis LX Cocceia Sabina vixit annis XXX mater Maximina piissimis bene merentibus fecit
EDCS-11201419.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aelius Maximianus lived for 60 years. Cocceia Sabina lived for 30 years. Their mother Maximina made this for her most dutiful and well-deserving children.
EDCS-11201419.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the mother Maximina to her deceased children, Publius Aelius Maximianus and Cocceia Sabina.

EDCS-11201422.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Titanus sta dum venio et cum venero stabes
EDCS-11201422.translation: Gaius Iulius Titanus: Stop until I come, and when I come, you will stop.
EDCS-11201422.summary: This is a unique, possibly apotropaic or cautionary, inscription associated with Gaius Iulius Titanus, instructing passersby to halt, perhaps near a tomb or property boundary.

EDCS-11201425.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cilices pro salute dddominorum nnnostrorum posuerunt contirones Dextro et Prisco consulibus
EDCS-11201425.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The Cilicians, fellow recruits, placed this for the safety of our lords, when Dextrus and Priscus were consuls.
EDCS-11201425.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Cilician fellow recruits for the safety of their lords, dated to the consulship of Dextrus and Priscus (283 AD).

EDCS-11201426.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  pro salute domini nostri Severi Alexandri Augusti Claudius Lupercus miles legionis IIII Flaviae Severianae Alexandrianae ex voto posuit libens merito
EDCS-11201426.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. For the safety of our lord Severus Alexander Augustus, Claudius Lupercus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Severan Alexandrian Legion, placed this willingly, deservedly, as a vow.
EDCS-11201426.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by Claudius Lupercus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Severan Alexandrian Legion, for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander (reigned 222-235 AD).

EDCS-11201427.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum nomine suo Aemilius Dolens optio
EDCS-11201427.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, in his own name, Aemilius Dolens, optio.
EDCS-11201427.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aemilius Dolens, an optio, likely on a small altar or votive tablet.

EDCS-11201428.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Barbius Ulpianus cornicularius legionis IIII Flaviae Felicis
EDCS-11201428.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Barbius Ulpianus, cornicularius of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion.
EDCS-11201428.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Barbius Ulpianus, a cornicularius of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, indicating a military votive offering.

EDCS-11201429.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Crescens votum posuit libens merito Probo consule
EDCS-11201429.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Pater. Aurelius Crescens placed this vow willingly, deservedly, when Probus was consul.
EDCS-11201429.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Pater by Aurelius Crescens, made willingly and deservedly during the consulship of Probus (276 AD or other years with a consul Probus).

EDCS-11201430.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Crescentio lapidarius pro salute sua et suorum
EDCS-11201430.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paterno. Aurelius Crescentio, stonecutter, for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-11201430.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paterno by a stonecutter named Aurelius Crescentio, made for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-11201431.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Titus Aurelius Lnus ex voto posuit
EDCS-11201431.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paternus, Titus Aurelius Lnus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11201431.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, placed by Titus Aurelius Lnus as fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11201432.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunonique Minervae Publius Terentius Aquilinus et Triitenica Sabina conssistentes Aquileiae votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-11201432.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to Juno and Minerva. Publius Terentius Aquilinus and Triitenica Sabina, residing in Aquileia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-11201432.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Terentius Aquilinus and Triitenica Sabina, who resided in Aquileia, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, and Minerva.

EDCS-11201433.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Terrae Matri Libero Patri et LibeIrae#Liberae#LIBIRE sacrum Titus Aurelius Atticus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae ex signifero praefecti kastrorum quinquennalis Singiduni decurio coloniae Sirmiensium  votum libens merito posuit
EDCS-11201433.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to Mother Earth, Father Liber, and Libera. Titus Aurelius Atticus, veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, formerly standard-bearer to the camp prefect, quinquennalis of Singidunum, decurion of the colony of Sirmium, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-11201433.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication by Titus Aurelius Atticus, a veteran with a distinguished career, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Mother Earth, Liber Pater, and Libera.

EDCS-11201435.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely naming Aelius Valentinus as part of a larger dedication or record.

EDCS-11201436.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Aufidius Ingenuus optio D P
EDCS-11201436.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Aufidius Ingenuus, optio, D P.
EDCS-11201436.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Nemesis Augusta by Aufidius Ingenuus, an optio, likely indicating a votive offering, with 'D P' potentially signifying 'de suo posuit' (placed at his own expense) or a similar formula.

EDCS-11201437.text_edition: Paterno  Aurelius Diogenes ex voto posuit
EDCS-11201437.translation: To Paternus, Aurelius Diogenes placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11201437.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Paternus by Aurelius Diogenes, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11201439.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Diogeniae vixit nnos IIII et Aurelio Diogeniano vixit annum I Aurelius Demetrius strator consularis legionis IIII Flaviae et Valeria Marcella filiis karissimis et Aurelio Eumeneti stratori legati legionis eiusdem vixit annos XL contirunculo fratri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201439.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelia Diogenia, who lived 4 years, and Aurelius Diogenianus, who lived 1 year. Aurelius Demetrius, consular groom of the Fourth Flavian Legion, and Valeria Marcella, for their dearest children, and for Aurelius Eumenes, groom of the legate of the same legion, who lived 40 years, their well-deserving little brother-in-arms, placed this.
EDCS-11201439.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelius Demetrius and Valeria Marcella for their two young children and for Aurelius Eumenes, a comrade and relative, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD given the legionary context.

EDCS-11201440.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Papiae veterano legionis IIII Flaviae ex beneficiario legati vixit annos LXX Aelia Iusta coniux et Aurelia Iustina filia Aelius Herculanus custos armorum legionis eiusdem gener bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201440.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Aurelius Papias, veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, formerly a beneficiarius of the legate, who lived 70 years. Aelia Iusta, his wife, and Aurelia Iustina, his daughter, and Aelius Herculanus, custos armorum of the same legion, his son-in-law, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201440.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Iusta, Aurelia Iustina, and Aelius Herculanus to Marcus Aurelius Papias, a 70-year-old veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion.

EDCS-11201441.text_edition: Dis Manibus perpetuae securitati Marco Ulpio Manliano veterano legionis IIII Flaviae ex beneficiario tribuni vixit nnos XLIIII Aelia Flora coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201441.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for perpetual security. For Marcus Ulpius Manlianus, veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, formerly a beneficiarius of the tribune, who lived 44 years. Aelia Flora placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11201441.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelia Flora in memory of her husband, Marcus Ulpius Manlianus, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion who lived 44 years.

EDCS-11201442.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpio Valentino vixit annos III Publius Aelius Valens beneficiarius praefecti filio carissimo
EDCS-11201442.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Ulpius Valentinus, who lived 3 years. Publius Aelius Valens, beneficiarius of the prefect, for his dearest son.
EDCS-11201442.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Publius Aelius Valens, a beneficiarius, for his beloved son, Ulpius Valentinus, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-11201443.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Albanius Luci filius Flavia Septiminus Sirmio imaginifer legionis IIII Flaviae stipendiorum XXIIII iussu ipsius sepulcrum Caius Titius Crescens veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae Lucius Albanius Hermes et Philomusus Andronici heredes fecerunt
EDCS-11201443.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Albanius, son of Lucius, of the Flavian Septiminus tribe, from Sirmium, standard-bearer of the Fourth Flavian Legion, with 24 years of service. At his own instruction, Caius Titius Crescens, veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, Lucius Albanius Hermes, and Philomusus Andronici, his heirs, made this tomb.
EDCS-11201443.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Albanius, a standard-bearer of the Fourth Flavian Legion with 24 years of service, erected by his heirs including a fellow veteran, as per his own wishes.

EDCS-11201444.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Crescens Scupis vet(eranus) legionis IIII Flaviae felicis ex equite militavit annos XXVI vixit annos XLV vixit annos XLV Petronia Secunda heres coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201444.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Crescens, from Scupi, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion Felix, formerly a cavalryman, served 26 years, lived 45 years. Petronia Secunda, his heir, placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11201444.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Petronia Secunda for her husband, Crescens, a cavalry veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion who served 26 years and lived 45 years.

EDCS-11201445.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassio Ingenuo militi legionis IIII Flaviae qui vixit nnos XL Cassia Atta fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201445.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Cassius Ingenuus, soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, who lived 40 years. Cassia Atta placed this for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-11201445.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cassia Atta for her brother, Cassius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-11201446.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Victoria vixit nnos L Aurelius Mercator veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae Antoninianae coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201446.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Victoria lived 50 years. Aurelius Mercator, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion Antoniniana, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201446.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Mercator, a veteran, to his wife Valeria Victoria, likely dating from the Antonine period due to the legion's epithet.

EDCS-11201447.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Victor veteranus et Aurelia Victoria coniuges vivi sibi posuerunt titulo suo sic et Aurelio Alpino patri genetivo qui vixit nnos XC et Publio Aurelio Alumno qui vixit annos  et Aurelio Apro vixit annos   CEFIA ET ICVS annonario posuit
EDCS-11201447.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Divine Manes. Aurelius Victor, a veteran, and Aurelia Victoria, his wife, placed this for themselves while living, on their own monument, and likewise for Aurelius Alpinus, their natural father, who lived 90 years, and for Publius Aurelius Alumnus, who lived [---] years, and for Aurelius Afer, who lived [---] years. [---] placed this for the grain supply official.
EDCS-11201447.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Victor and Aurelia Victoria for themselves while living, and also commemorating their father Aurelius Alpinus and other relatives, with an additional, fragmented dedication to an annonarius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201448.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Apphion vixit nnos II menses VIII Caius Valerius Valens veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae et Valeria Elpis parentes filiae carissimae
EDCS-11201448.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Apphion lived 2 years, 8 months. Gaius Valerius Valens, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, and Valeria Elpis, her parents, [placed this] for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-11201448.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Valerius Valens, a veteran, and Valeria Elpis for their young daughter, Valeria Apphion, likely dating from the Antonine period given the legion's epithet.

EDCS-11201450.text_edition: Dibus Manibus Infernis Nunnio Prisciano decurioni coloniae Singiduni qui vixit nnos XXXVI non dignus ceIlerius#celerius#CILERIUS infrTa#infra#INFTA decessit Aurelius Priscillianus et Septimia Lupercilla
EDCS-11201450.translation: To the Infernal Divine Manes. For Nunnius Priscianus, decurion of the colony of Singidunum, who lived 36 years, not deserving of such a swift death. Aurelius Priscillianus and Septimia Lupercilla [placed this].
EDCS-11201450.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nunnius Priscianus, a decurion of Singidunum, erected by Aurelius Priscillianus and Septimia Lupercilla, lamenting his premature death.

EDCS-11201453.text_edition: Dis Manibus Domitia Ursa vixit nnos XXV Albanius Septiminus hospitae bene merenti posuit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis  Dis Manibus Sereniae Quartae interfectae ab hoste vixit nnos L Cocceius Montanus tutor Titianus scribendum curavit
EDCS-11201453.translation: To the Divine Manes. Domitia Ursa lived 25 years. Albanius Septiminus placed this for his well-deserving guest. She is laid here; may the earth be light upon you. To the Divine Manes of Serenia Quarta, killed by the enemy, who lived 50 years. Cocceius Montanus, guardian. Titianus had it inscribed.
EDCS-11201453.summary: This inscription comprises two distinct funerary dedications: one by Albanius Septiminus for his guest Domitia Ursa, and another for Serenia Quarta, who was killed by an enemy, commissioned by her guardian Cocceius Montanus and inscribed by Titianus.

EDCS-11201454.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Postumio Cornuto et Postumiae Caleni Quinto Postumio Felici ti Crescentioni IA qui   parentibus
EDCS-11201454.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus Postumius Cornutus and Postumia Caleni, [and] Quintus Postumius Felix, [and] Crescentio[na] (or Crescentio) [---] parents.
EDCS-11201454.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Postumius Cornutus, Postumia Caleni, Quintus Postumius Felix, and Crescentio (or Crescentiona), likely erected by their children or other family members.

EDCS-11201455.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Valerius Maximianus vixit annos XIII Valerius Longinus pater filio bene merito
EDCS-11201455.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Valerius Maximianus lived 13 years. Valerius Longinus, his father, [placed this] for his well-deserving son.
EDCS-11201455.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valerius Longinus to his young son, Gaius Valerius Maximianus.

EDCS-11201456.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Asc Leuc Valerius N annos IV
EDCS-11201456.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Asc. Leuc. Valerius N., 4 years old.
EDCS-11201456.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Publius Asc. Leuc. Valerius N., who died at the age of four, likely from a tombstone or memorial monument.

EDCS-11201463.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudius Valentinianus eques Romanus decurio IIIIvir municipii Apulensis Aureliae Probillae coniugi carissimae quae vixit annos XXVIIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201463.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudius Valentinianus, Roman knight, decurion, duumvir of the municipium of Apulum, placed this for Aurelia Probilla, his dearest wife, who lived 29 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-11201463.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudius Valentinianus, a Roman knight and municipal official, to his beloved wife Aurelia Probilla.

EDCS-11201464.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Valentinus  vixit nnis L    vixit nnis LX   ius bene merenti posuit  Lucilla liberorum III coniux Aureli  vixit annis XXX
EDCS-11201464.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Valentinus lived 50 years. [---] lived 60 years. [---]ius placed this for the well-deserving. Lucilla, wife of Aurelius [---], mother of three children, lived 30 years.
EDCS-11201464.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription commemorating Aurelius Valentinus, another individual who lived 60 years, and Lucilla, wife of Aurelius [---] and mother of three, likely erected by various family members.

EDCS-11201465.translation: To the god, Marcus Ulpius Thallus placed this.
EDCS-11201465.summary: This inscription records a dedication to an unspecified deity by Marcus Ulpius Thallus, likely an altar or votive offering.

EDCS-11201466.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute sua et suorum Aurelius Marcellinus vir egregius voto posuit
EDCS-11201466.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for his own well-being and that of his family, Aurelius Marcellinus, an egregius vir, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11201466.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Marcellinus, a member of the equestrian order, in gratitude for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-11201467.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno M V |(centurio) legionis
EDCS-11201467.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paternus. M. V., centurion of the legion.
EDCS-11201467.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus by M. V., a centurion of an unspecified legion, indicating a military votive offering.

EDCS-11201470.text_edition: Dei in domu Hermogenes votum fecit
EDCS-11201470.translation: Hermogenes made a vow to the gods in the house.
EDCS-11201470.summary: This inscription records that Hermogenes made a vow to the gods within a house, suggesting a private religious act or dedication.

EDCS-11201471.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Flora, likely part of a tombstone or a dedicatory monument, but the specific context is not fully clear from the text alone.

EDCS-11201472.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Agellius Zoticus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201472.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Agellius Zoticus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201472.summary: This inscription records Gaius Agellius Zoticus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-11201474.text_edition: Iovi sacrum Iuppiter Crescens Mucati votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201474.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Jupiter Crescens Mucati willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201474.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication by Jupiter Crescens Mucati, who fulfilled a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-11201475.text_edition: Iuppiter Auguste Dines Celsi votum solvit libens
EDCS-11201475.translation: To Jupiter Augustus. Dines Celsi willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201475.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication by Dines Celsi, who fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Augustus.

EDCS-11201476.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Sextilianus
EDCS-11201476.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Sextilianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-11201476.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Aelius Sextilianus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely for a votive offering or monument.

EDCS-11201477.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius [dedicated this].
EDCS-11201477.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication by Aurelius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-11201478.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iuppiter Marcus Aurelius Crescentio prP(o)#pr(o)#PP se et suos sic et cives sic  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201478.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Crescentio, acting pro PP for himself and his people, and for his fellow citizens, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201478.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Crescentio fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus on behalf of himself, his people, and his fellow citizens.

EDCS-11201479.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Bitio BiPti#Biti#BPTI libens votPum#votum#VOPU solvit
EDCS-11201479.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Bitio Biti willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201479.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Bitio Biti to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11201480.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Vitalis pro (se) et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201480.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-11201480.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Vitalis to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-11201482.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Sentia Flaventilla votum merito libens solvit
EDCS-11201482.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus. Sentia Flaventilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11201482.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication by Sentia Flaventilla to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11201483.text_edition: Deo Mithrae S(oli) pro salute Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Augusti Gaius C---ius Marcellus et Marius Victorinus posuerunt libentes merito
EDCS-11201483.translation: To the god Mithras Sol, for the welfare of the Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus. Gaius C---ius Marcellus and Marius Victorinus willingly and deservedly erected this.
EDCS-11201483.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius C---ius Marcellus and Marius Victorinus to the god Mithras Sol for the welfare of Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201485.text_edition: Silvano Augusto Conservatori Marcus Ulpius Terentius cum suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201485.translation: To Silvanus Augustus the Preserver. Marcus Ulpius Terentius with his family willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201485.summary: This inscription records Marcus Ulpius Terentius and his family fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Augustus, the Preserver.

EDCS-11201487.text_edition: Caius Cetroniusanus com votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201487.translation: Gaius Cetroniusanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201487.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Gaius Cetroniusanus, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, though the specific deity is not mentioned.

EDCS-11201488.text_edition: Marcianus  cohortis II Aureliae novae |(miliariae) equitatae Antoninianae votum dedit et votum solvit
EDCS-11201488.translation: Marcianus, of the Second Aurelian new milliary mounted Antoninian cohort, made and fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-11201488.summary: This inscription records Marcianus, a soldier of the Second Aurelian new milliary mounted Antoninian cohort, making and fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11201489.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Ascius vixit nnos C et Iulia Proculina vixit nnos XL Licinia Bonosa coniugi et filiae carissimis et Iulio Iuliano genero veterano alae Itureorum qui vixit nnos LX et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-11201489.translation: To the Manes. Iulius Ascius lived 100 years, and Iulia Proculina lived 40 years. Licinia Bonosa, while alive, placed this for her dearest husband and daughter, and for Iulius Iulianus, her son-in-law, a veteran of the Ituraean cavalry wing, who lived 60 years, and for herself.
EDCS-11201489.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Licinia Bonosa for her husband Iulius Ascius, her daughter Iulia Proculina, her son-in-law Iulius Iulianus (a veteran), and for herself while still living.

EDCS-11201490.text_edition: Valetudinarium cohortis II Aureliae novae |(miliariae) equitatae civium Romanorum Titus Bebenius Iustus praefectus Imperatore Commodo II et Vero II consulibus
EDCS-11201490.translation: The hospital of the Second Aurelian new milliary mounted cohort of Roman citizens, [erected by] Titus Bebenius Iustus, prefect, when Commodus and Verus were consuls for the second time.
EDCS-11201490.summary: This inscription records the construction of a hospital for the Second Aurelian new milliary mounted cohort of Roman citizens by Prefect Titus Bebenius Iustus, datable to 179 AD based on the consular dating.

EDCS-11201491.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Claudia Casta vixit nnos XXXII et Aurelia Flora vixit nnos L Aurelius Acutio miles cohortis II Aureliae novae coniugi et matri bene merentis sic et sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-11201491.translation: To the Manes. Claudia Casta lived 32 years, and Aurelia Flora lived 50 years. Aurelius Acutio, a soldier of the Second Aurelian new cohort, while alive, made this for his deserving wife and mother, and for himself.
EDCS-11201491.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Acutio, a soldier, for his deceased wife Claudia Casta and mother Aurelia Flora, as well as for himself while still living.

EDCS-11201492.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Victorinus veteranus cohortis II Aureliae novae vixit nnos LX Aurelia Rufina coniugi bene merenti et Publius Aelius Acutianus et Publius Aelius Victorinus patri et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-11201492.translation: To the Manes. Publius Aelius Victorinus, a veteran of the Second Aurelian new cohort, lived 60 years. Aurelia Rufina, for her deserving husband, and Publius Aelius Acutianus and Publius Aelius Victorinus, while alive, placed this for their father and for themselves.
EDCS-11201492.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Victorinus, a veteran, by his wife Aurelia Rufina and his sons Publius Aelius Acutianus and Publius Aelius Victorinus, who also included themselves while still living.

EDCS-11201493.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Luciae vixit annis XLV Aurelius Bardibalus decurio collegii centonariorum coniugi et Aurelius Lucilius matri bene merenti et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-11201493.translation: To the Manes of Aelia Lucia, who lived 45 years. Aurelius Bardibalus, decurion of the college of centonarii, for his wife, and Aurelius Lucilius, for his deserving mother, while alive, placed this for themselves.
EDCS-11201493.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aelia Lucia by her husband Aurelius Bardibalus, a decurion of the centonarii guild, and her son Aurelius Lucilius, who also included themselves while still living.

EDCS-11201494.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelii Vitalis vixit annos XXVII et Firmus vixit nnos XXV et Marcus Aurelius Respectus et Aurelia Veneria filis bene merentibus posuerunt et sibi vivis posuerunt cum Marcis Aureliis Respectiano et Hilerione sic filiis pientissimis
EDCS-11201494.translation: To the Manes of Aurelius Vitalis, who lived 27 years, and Firmus, who lived 25 years. Marcus Aurelius Respectus and Aurelia Veneria placed this for their deserving sons, and for themselves while living, along with their most devoted sons Marcus Aurelius Respectianus and Hilerio.
EDCS-11201494.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Aurelius Respectus and Aurelia Veneria for their deceased sons Aurelius Vitalis and Firmus, and for themselves and their living sons Marcus Aurelius Respectianus and Hilerio.

EDCS-11201495.text_edition: Dis Manibus Chrysophoro et Primitivae Adiiutoris sic servis tus fratris sic bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-11201495.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Adiiutor dedicated this to Chrysophorus and Primitiva, his very deserving siblings, as if his own servants.
EDCS-11201495.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Adiiutor for his deceased siblings, Chrysophorus and Primitiva, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201496.text_edition: Publio Covirio Gentili Trevero Publius Covirius Optatus frater fecit
EDCS-11201496.translation: To Publius Covirius Gentilis, a Treveran. Publius Covirius Optatus, his brother, made this.
EDCS-11201496.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Publius Covirius Optatus erected a monument for his brother, Publius Covirius Gentilis, a Treveran.

EDCS-11201498.text_edition: Dis Manibus Embasia Antoni filia vicsit sic annis XX posuit Antonius Germani filiae bene merenti
EDCS-11201498.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Embasia, daughter of Antonius, lived for 20 years. Antonius, son of Germanus, placed this for his deserving daughter.
EDCS-11201498.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Antonius, son of Germanus, for his deceased daughter, Embasia, who lived to be 20 years old.

EDCS-11201499.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Quinti filio Sergia Rufo Liburno Flavia Marulla coniugi pientissimo vixit annos LXI hic situs est
EDCS-11201499.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Gaius Iulius Rufus Liburnus, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe. Flavia Marulla placed this for her most devoted husband. He lived for 61 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-11201499.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavia Marulla to her most devoted husband, Gaius Iulius Rufus Liburnus, who lived for 61 years, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-11201500.text_edition: Iulius nio posuit sibi et coniugi Pusiae pientissimae et filiae Primitivae karissimae et Diophaneti Dionisi sic genero pio vixit  nns XX
EDCS-11201500.translation: Iulius placed this for himself and for his most devoted wife Pusia, and for his dearest daughter Primitiva, and for his dutiful son-in-law Diophanes, son of Dionysius, who lived for 20 years.
EDCS-11201500.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulius for himself, his wife Pusia, his daughter Primitiva, and his son-in-law Diophanes, who passed away at 20 years old.

EDCS-11201501.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Venusta vixit annos LXX Demetrius
EDCS-11201501.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Venusta lived 70 years. Demetrius [made this].
EDCS-11201501.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Valeria Venusta, who lived 70 years, likely set up by Demetrius.

EDCS-11201502.text_edition: $ S]uavis Diniceni vixit nnos LX Rhodope Valentis coniugi et sibi viva et Valerius Valens socro bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201502.translation: [To the spirits of the departed,] Suavis, son of Dinicenus, lived for 60 years. Rhodope, wife of Valens, placed this for her husband and for herself while still alive, and Valerius Valens placed this for his deserving father-in-law.
EDCS-11201502.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Rhodope for her husband Suavis (who lived 60 years) and for herself while still living, and by Valerius Valens for his father-in-law, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-11201503.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Martinus.
EDCS-11201503.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Flavius Martinus.

EDCS-11201504.text_edition: I Cornelio Marci filio IV O Iulia Secunda coniugi pientissimo et filio
EDCS-11201504.translation: To Cornelius, son of Marcus, [who lived] 4 [years]. Julia Secunda [set this up] for her most devoted husband and son.
EDCS-11201504.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Julia Secunda for her deeply devoted husband and for her son, Cornelius, who lived four years.

EDCS-11201505.text_edition: Dis Manibus M Pra nnrum XX Ianuarius AVIS
EDCS-11201505.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Pra[---], [who lived] 20 years. Ianuarius AVIS [---].
EDCS-11201505.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Marcus, who lived 20 years, likely erected by Ianuarius.

EDCS-11201524.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary monument, for Gaius Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-11201526.translation: Aurelianus, a probator of the Emperor, approved Iustus.
EDCS-11201526.summary: This inscription records that Iustus was approved by Aurelianus, who was an imperial inspector or probator.

EDCS-11201527.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Pinnes Dasi vixit annos L Ceuna coniunx vixit annos XL Sever Proradi vixit annos L Aurelii Dasius Licinius Maxima parentibus pro pietate posuerunt hic situs est!
EDCS-11201527.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Aurelius Pinnes, son of Dasus, lived for 50 years. Ceuna, his wife, lived for 40 years. Severus, son of Proradus, lived for 50 years. Aurelii Dasius, Licinius, and Maxima, in their piety, placed this for their parents. He is buried here!
EDCS-11201527.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Pinnes, his wife Ceuna, and Severus, likely a relative, set up by their children Aurelii Dasius, Licinius, and Maxima, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-11201529.translation: Sacred to the god B[---]. Of Victoris.
EDCS-11201529.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to an unnamed deity, perhaps a local god, associated with Victoris.

EDCS-11201536.text_edition: Deo Aeterno Publius Aelius Hermes pro salute sua suorumque votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201536.translation: To the Eternal God, Publius Aelius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own welfare and that of his family.
EDCS-11201536.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to an "Eternal God" by Publius Aelius Hermes for his and his family's well-being, indicating a personal religious devotion.

EDCS-11201537.text_edition: Diis Angelis Marcus Aurelius Celsus votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-11201537.translation: To the Angelic Gods. Marcus Aurelius Celsus willingly and deservedly placed his vow.
EDCS-11201537.summary: This is a votive inscription where Marcus Aurelius Celsus dedicated a monument to the Angelic Gods, fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-11201539.text_edition: Domino et Dominae sacrum Crescentilla votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201539.translation: Sacred to the Lord and Lady. Crescentilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11201539.summary: This is a votive inscription where Crescentilla fulfilled a vow, dedicating it willingly and deservedly to a Lord and Lady, likely deities.

EDCS-11201542.text_edition: Deo sancto Herculi |(centuriae) VIIII principis prioris Ulpius Valens optio legionis VII Claudiae commanipularibus bonis ex voto posuit libens meritO  IMP C  A DOILLO et MANNI consulibus
EDCS-11201542.translation: To the holy god Hercules, Ulpius Valens, an optio of the Seventh Claudian Legion, ninth centuria of the prior princeps, willingly and deservedly placed this for his good comrades, as vowed, in the consulship of Imperator Caesar A. Doillius and Mannius.
EDCS-11201542.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Ulpius Valens, an optio of the Legio VII Claudia, on behalf of his comrades, placed during a specific consular year (likely 2nd or 3rd century AD).

EDCS-11201543.text_edition: Herculi Victori Marcus Securius Censorinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11201543.translation: To Hercules Victor, Marcus Securius Censorinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201543.summary: This inscription records Marcus Securius Censorinus dedicating a monument to Hercules Victor in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201544.text_edition: Cocceius Cassius cot sic beneficarius legati vovit beneficarius consularis solvit
EDCS-11201544.translation: Cocceius Cassius, beneficiary of the legate, vowed; beneficiary of the consular governor, fulfilled.
EDCS-11201544.summary: This inscription records Cocceius Cassius's fulfillment of a vow, made first as a legate's beneficiary and then fulfilled as a consular governor's beneficiary, indicating his career progression.

EDCS-11201545.text_edition: Dio sic Heroni Aurelius Gaius veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae Antoninianae votum solvit
EDCS-11201545.translation: To the god Heron, Aurelius Gaius, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion Antoniniana, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201545.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Heron by Aurelius Gaius, a veteran of the Legio IIII Flavia Antoniniana, fulfilling a vow, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201546.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Maximus MVA
EDCS-11201546.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Maximus MVA.
EDCS-11201546.summary: This is a votive dedication by Aurelius Maximus to Jupiter Best and Greatest, with the meaning of 'MVA' unclear.

EDCS-11201548.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Prusius Apollonius v(otum)? s(olvit) libens merito
EDCS-11201548.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Prusius Apollonius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11201548.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Prusius Apollonius, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-11201549.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius.
EDCS-11201549.summary: This is a simple dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Marcus Ulpius, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-11201550.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Cassius Gemellus aquilifer votum posuit
EDCS-11201550.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver, Cassius Gemellus, an aquilifer, set up this vow.
EDCS-11201550.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Cassius Gemellus, an aquilifer, to Jupiter Best and Greatest in his aspect as 'the Preserver'.

EDCS-11201551.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque et Laribus domesticis Lucius Domitius Anthi po
EDCS-11201551.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to the gods and goddesses, and to the household Lares, Lucius Domitius Anthi...
EDCS-11201551.summary: This is a polytheistic dedication by Lucius Domitius Anthi, addressing Jupiter Optimus Maximus, various gods and goddesses, and the domestic Lares.

EDCS-11201557.text_edition: Deae Reginae Nemesi Aelius Pompeianus quod adiutor promisi frumentarius posui
EDCS-11201557.translation: To the Queen Goddess Nemesis, Aelius Pompeianus, a grain distributor, set up this monument because I promised it as an assistant.
EDCS-11201557.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Pompeianus, a frumentarius (grain distributor), to the goddess Nemesis Regina, fulfilling a promise made in his capacity as an assistant.

EDCS-11201558.text_edition: Scholae Genio mensorum et legionis VII Claudiae pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Alexander Fortunati dispensator horreorum dedicat  III Kalendas Decembres Gallo et Volusiano Augustis consulibus
EDCS-11201558.translation: To the Genius of the guild of surveyors and of the Seventh Claudian Legion, for the welfare of our lords the Augusti, Alexander, dispensator of the granaries, son of Fortunatus, dedicates this on the 3rd day before the Kalends of December, when Gallus and Volusianus were Augusti and consuls.
EDCS-11201558.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the Genius of a guild and legion by an imperial freedman, for the health of the emperors, dated to December 253 CE.

EDCS-11201559.text_edition: Deabus Silvestris sic Achilleus ex voto libens posuit
EDCS-11201559.translation: To the Woodland Goddesses, Achilleus thus willingly set this up as a vow.
EDCS-11201559.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Achilleus to the Silvestres Deae (Woodland Goddesses).

EDCS-11201561.text_edition: pro salute  Calpurni Honoratiani praefecti legionis VII Claudiae Caius Iulius Ingenuus librarius legionis factus ab eo votum solvit libens merito et exacti consularis beneficiarii et frumentarii  no et Rufo consulibus
EDCS-11201561.translation: For the welfare of Calpurnius Honoratianus, prefect of the Seventh Claudian Legion, Gaius Julius Ingenuus, the legionary clerk appointed by him, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, also having been a beneficiary and frumentarius of the consular staff, in the consulship of [--] and Rufus.
EDCS-11201561.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a legionary clerk for the welfare of his commanding officer, a prefect of the Seventh Claudian Legion.

EDCS-11201564.text_edition: Marcus Tertius Chrestus frumentarius et Tertia Val Romana filia eius
EDCS-11201564.translation: Marcus Tertius Chrestus, a grain distributor, and Tertia Val Romana, his daughter.
EDCS-11201564.summary: This inscription records the names of Marcus Tertius Chrestus, a frumentarius, and his daughter, Tertia Val Romana, likely on a tombstone or family monument.

EDCS-11201570.text_edition: pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Augusti Valerius Crescentio miles legionis IIII Flaviae Severianae Alexandrianae votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-11201570.translation: For the welfare of Imperator Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Augustus, Valerius Crescentio, soldier of the Fourth Flavian Severian Alexandrian Legion, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-11201570.summary: This inscription records the placement of a vow by a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion for the well-being of Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-11201571.text_edition: Pro salute Augusti  Caius Valerius Vibianus nautarum quinquennalis signum Matris Deum et ad restitutionem templi Neptuni ssestertium IIduo milia nummum dono dedit
EDCS-11201571.translation: For the welfare of Augustus, Gaius Valerius Vibianus, quinquennial chief of the boatmen, gave as a gift a statue of the Mother of the Gods and two thousand sesterces for the restoration of the Temple of Neptune.
EDCS-11201571.summary: This inscription records the donation of a statue and money for temple restoration by a quinquennial chief of boatmen, made for the welfare of the emperor.

EDCS-11201574.text_edition: [ Cai Fulvi] [Plautiani] praefecti praetorio clarissimi viri necessarii dominorum nnnostrorum Flavius Optatus procurator Auggustorum
EDCS-11201574.translation: To Gaius Fulvius Plautianus, praetorian prefect, most distinguished man, kinsman of our lords, Flavius Optatus, procurator of the Augusti [dedicates this].
EDCS-11201574.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to Gaius Fulvius Plautianus, a praetorian prefect and kinsman of the emperors, by an imperial procurator.

EDCS-11201575.text_edition: Egnatius Marinianus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-11201575.translation: Egnatius Marinianus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power.
EDCS-11201575.summary: This inscription identifies Egnatius Marinianus as an imperial legate with pro-praetorian authority, likely part of a public monument or an honorific inscription.

EDCS-11201576.text_edition: Marco Antonio Marci filio Fabia Fabiano procuratori XL Galliarum et portus item argentariarum nnum conductori portori Illyrici patrono bono Mercator libertus
EDCS-11201576.translation: To Marcus Antonius Fabianus, son of Marcus, procurator of the Forty Gallic [Customs] and the port, also of the silver mines, contractor of the Illyrian customs, a good patron, his freedman Mercator [dedicates this].
EDCS-11201576.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Mercator, a freedman, to his patron Marcus Antonius Fabianus, who held several important financial administrative roles.

EDCS-11201577.text_edition: Dis Manibus Albinae quae vixit annis XV diebus XXXVI Albinus Iuliorum servus vilicus et Irene parentes filiae carissimae posuerunt
EDCS-11201577.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Albina, who lived for 15 years and 36 days. Albinus, a slave and bailiff of the Julii, and Irene, her parents, placed this for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-11201577.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by parents, a slave bailiff and his partner, for their young daughter Albina.

EDCS-11201578.text_edition: Dis Manibus Baebiae Amoenianae vixit annis XXXIX Caius Cassius Niger decurio municipii Aeli Viminacii coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201578.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Baebia Amoeniana, who lived for 39 years. Gaius Cassius Niger, decurion of the municipium Aeli Viminacii, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11201578.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument placed by Gaius Cassius Niger, a decurion, for his deceased wife Baebia Amoeniana.

EDCS-11201579.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Cornelius Rufus decurio augur municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit nnos LXX Ulpia Rufina uxor et Caius Cornelius Pacatus decurio flamen municipii eiusdem et Corneliae Rufina et Bassa filii et heredes bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11201579.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Cornelius Rufus, decurion and augur of the municipium Aeli Viminacii, lived for 70 years. Ulpia Rufina, his wife, and Gaius Cornelius Pacatus, decurion and flamen of the same municipium, and Cornelia Rufina and Bassa, his children and heirs, ensured this was made for their well-deserving [father and husband].
EDCS-11201579.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Gaius Cornelius Rufus, a decurion and augur, erected by his wife, son, and daughters, who were his heirs.

EDCS-11201580.text_edition: Lucio Sevio Rufo sacerdoti municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit nnos XLV Saenia Supera coniugi
EDCS-11201580.translation: To Lucius Sevius Rufus, priest of the municipium Aeli Viminacii, who lived for 45 years. Saenia Supera, his wife [dedicates this].
EDCS-11201580.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Saenia Supera to her husband, Lucius Sevius Rufus, a priest.

EDCS-11201583.text_edition: Valeriae Hilarae Marcus Aurelius Timon |(centurio) legionis III Gallicae Severianae Alexandrianae coniugi piissimae posuit
EDCS-11201583.translation: To Valeria Hilara, Marcus Aurelius Timon, centurion of the Legio III Gallica Severiana Alexandriana, set this up for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-11201583.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Timon, a centurion, to his beloved wife Valeria Hilara.

EDCS-11201584.text_edition: Quinto Sicinio Quinti filio Sergia Maximo Xviro stlitibus iudicandis tribuno militum legionis IIII Flaviae felicis vixit nnos XXIII
EDCS-11201584.translation: To Quintus Sicinius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe, decemvir for judging lawsuits, military tribune of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix. He lived for 23 years.
EDCS-11201584.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sicinius Maximus, a young man who held several civic and military offices before his early death.

EDCS-11201585.text_edition: Lucius Aemilius Luci filius Fabia Flaus Bracara eques legionis IIII Flaviae felicis stipendiorum XIIII vixit nnos XXXII
EDCS-11201585.translation: Lucius Aemilius Flaus, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, from Bracara, a cavalryman of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, served 14 years and lived for 32 years.
EDCS-11201585.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Flaus, a cavalryman from Bracara, indicating his military service and age at death.

EDCS-11201586.text_edition: Publius Publicius Fabia Constans Brixxia sic eq(ues) legionis IIII Flaviae felicis testamento fieri iussit hic situs eFst#est#FST
EDCS-11201586.translation: Publius Publicius Constans, son of Publicius, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia, a cavalryman of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, ordered this to be made by his testament. He is buried here.
EDCS-11201586.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Publicius Constans, a cavalryman, indicating it was erected according to his will and marks his burial place.

EDCS-11201589.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tiberius Tettius Euphemianus primus pilus legionis VII Claudiae hic situs est Fulvia Chrysis marito pientissimo
EDCS-11201589.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Tiberius Tettius Euphemianus, primus pilus of the Legio VII Claudia, is buried here. Fulvia Chrysis [set this up] for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-11201589.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Tettius Euphemianus, a high-ranking centurion, dedicated by his wife Fulvia Chrysis.

EDCS-11201590.text_edition: nociusto ex  Carthaginiensi |(centurioni) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis pro RO I sorores heredes optimo piissimo amantissimo fratri fecerunt pius vixit annos LXV |(centurio) militavit annos XXXV hic situs est ex his agris monimento excepti sunt in fronte pedes XX introitus pedes XX et iter
EDCS-11201590.translation: To Nociustus, from Carthage, centurion of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis. His sisters, as heirs, made this for their best, most devoted, and most loving brother. He lived 65 pious years. As a centurion, he served 35 years. He is buried here. From these lands, 20 feet in front and 20 feet for the entrance and pathway were set aside for the monument.
EDCS-11201590.summary: This is a detailed funerary inscription for Nociustus, a centurion from Carthage, erected by his sisters, specifying his age, military service, and the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-11201592.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Senecianus beneficiarius consularis vixit nnos XLV Ulpia Faustina coniux et Aurelia Firmina filia posuerunt
EDCS-11201592.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Senecianus, a consular beneficiarius, lived for 45 years. His wife Ulpia Faustina and daughter Aurelia Firmina set this up.
EDCS-11201592.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Senecianus, a beneficiarius, erected by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-11201593.text_edition: Iuliae Abitae sic vixit nnos XIIII Iulius Ianuarius beneficiarius consularis libertae bene merenti posuit hic sita est
EDCS-11201593.translation: To Julia Abita, she lived for 14 years. Julius Januarius, a consular beneficiarius, set this up for his well-deserving freedwoman. She is buried here.
EDCS-11201593.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Abita, a freedwoman who died young, set up by Julius Januarius, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-11201595.text_edition: $ militavit] annos XXIII vixit annos XLVIII Flavius Palma beneficiarius legati heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201595.translation: [He served] 23 years, lived 48 years. Flavius Palma, beneficiarius to the legate, as heir, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201595.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed individual, erected by Flavius Palma, a beneficiarius, noting the deceased's military service and lifespan.

EDCS-11201597.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Maximus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae ex corniculario tribuni vixit nnos LVII Aebutia Taburtilla  et Vale bene m(erenti) pos(uerunt)
EDCS-11201597.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Valerius Maximus, a veteran of the Legio VII Claudia, formerly a tribune's clerk, lived for 57 years. Aebutia Taburtilla and Vale [---] set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201597.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Maximus, a veteran who served as a clerk, erected by Aebutia Taburtilla and another individual.

EDCS-11201598.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Valenti militi legionis VII Claudiae beneficiario procuratoris stipendiorum XXV vixit nnos L Lucius Iulius Valens filius et heres faciendum curavit hic situs est
EDCS-11201598.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Gaius Iulius Valens, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia, a beneficiary of the procurator for 25 stipends. He lived for 50 years. Lucius Iulius Valens, his son and heir, took care of making this. He is buried here.
EDCS-11201598.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Iulius Valens, a soldier and beneficiary, erected by his son and heir, Lucius Iulius Valens.

EDCS-11201599.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Dizzoni aquilifero legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XLVI Flavia Nata coniux et Flavia Viatorina filia heredes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201599.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Marcus Aurelius Dizzo, standard-bearer of the Seventh Legion Claudia. He lived for 46 years. Flavia Nata, his wife, and Flavia Viatorina, his daughter, his heirs, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201599.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavia Nata and Flavia Viatorina to Marcus Aurelius Dizzo, a standard-bearer of the Seventh Legion Claudia, who lived to be 46.

EDCS-11201602.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Vitalis veteranus ex mensore tritici legionis VII Claudiae et Aurelia Macedonia coniux memoriam vivi fabrikabimus sic et quod nescientes accepimus inviti reddidimus ταῦτα
EDCS-11201602.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Vitalis, a veteran formerly a grain measurer of the Seventh Legion Claudia, and Aurelia Macedonia, his wife, will fabricate this memorial while alive, for we unwillingly surrendered what we received unknowingly. These things.
EDCS-11201602.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Vitalis, a veteran and former grain measurer, and his wife Aurelia Macedonia, who had this memorial prepared during their lifetimes.

EDCS-11201603.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Cassianus Potens miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit annis XI vixit annis XXVIII Cassiana Valentina bene merenti fratri posuit
EDCS-11201603.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Cassianus Potens, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, served for 11 years, lived for 28 years. Cassiana Valentina placed this for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-11201603.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cassiana Valentina for her brother, Lucius Cassianus Potens, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia, who died at 28.

EDCS-11201604.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Quintino veterano legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XLV et Lucio Valerio Celso militi legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos XX militavit annos II Licinia Celsina coniugi karissimo et filio incomparabili bene merentibus posuit h s m e
EDCS-11201604.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Valerius Quintinus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia, who lived for 45 years, and to Lucius Valerius Celsus, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia, who lived for 20 years and served for 2 years. Licinia Celsina placed this for her dearest husband and incomparable son, both well-deserving. He is buried here.
EDCS-11201604.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Licinia Celsina to her husband, Lucius Valerius Quintinus, a veteran, and their son, Lucius Valerius Celsus, a soldier, both of the Seventh Legion Claudia.

EDCS-11201605.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Valens Fabia Heraclea veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis vixit annos LXX Valeria Veneria liberta patrono bene merenti et sibi pos(uit)
EDCS-11201605.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Valerius Valens of the Fabia tribe, Heraclea, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, lived for 70 years. Valeria Veneria, his freedwoman, placed this for her well-deserving patron and for herself.
EDCS-11201605.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Valens, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia, erected by his freedwoman Valeria Veneria, who also intended the monument for herself.

EDCS-11201608.text_edition: IIar nnum XXV hic sit- est Lucius Fabius Crescens |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris libert- bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201608.translation: He was here for 25 years. Lucius Fabius Crescens, centurion of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris. His freedmen placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201608.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fabius Crescens, a centurion of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, erected by his freedmen.

EDCS-11201609.text_edition: Sabinianus protector ortus in provincia Dardania regione Ulpianensi  Dasantis qui vixit annos L qui donec superstaret vitam iucundam exegi sibi et amicis qui a me hodie exoptantur et optantur ibidem terra levis habeat semper vosque iter carpite legite  vos vitam iucundam exigatis post mortem nihil est nisi est vilis umbra vox bonae memoriae castaque   Sulpitus posuit
EDCS-11201609.translation: Sabinianus, protector, born in the province of Dardania, Ulpian region, of the Dasanti; who lived for 50 years; who, as long as he lived, spent a pleasant life for himself and his friends who are longed for and wished for by me today, there too. May the earth be light to them always. And you, make your journey, read, may you spend a pleasant life. After death there is nothing but a worthless shadow, a voice of good and chaste memory. Sulpitus placed this.
EDCS-11201609.summary: This is a philosophical funerary inscription for Sabinianus, a protector from Dardania, expressing reflections on life and death, erected by Sulpitus.

EDCS-11201611.text_edition: Dis Manibus Paridi alumno vixit annos XVII Caius Reg Callistianus praep(ositus fecit
EDCS-11201611.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Paris, a foster child, who lived for 17 years. Gaius Reg(ius) Callistianus, a supervisor, made this.
EDCS-11201611.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Paris, a foster child who died at 17, made by Gaius Regius Callistianus, a supervisor.

EDCS-11201612.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely commemorating Aurelius Vale, possibly a soldier or official from a Roman cohort.

EDCS-11201613.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Secundinus veteranus vixit annos LX Aurelii Secundina Secundio Iulia et Lidira patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201613.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran, lived for 60 years. Aurelii Secundina, Secundio, Iulia, and Lidira placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-11201613.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelii Secundina, Secundio, Iulia, and Lidira to their father, Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-11201616.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Petronius eter vixit annos LXV nia Marcia coniugi et Petronius Cogitatus Petronius Fuscus Marcia Marcellina Petronius Amynianus fili patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11201616.translation: To the Divine Manes. Petronius Heter lived 65 years. Marcia, his wife, and Petronius Cogitatus, Petronius Fuscus, Marcia Marcellina, Petronius Amynianus, his children, placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-11201616.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a tomb by Marcia, wife of Petronius Heter, and their four children, honoring their deceased father and husband, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11201617.text_edition: I et  mil  nnos Xvixit nnos  fili- ETCONAce liberta bene merenti  GAIIV et sibi
EDCS-11201617.translation: And ... years he lived ... years ... son, ETCONAce, to the well-deserving freedwoman, Gaius and for himself.
EDCS-11201617.summary: This fragment records a funerary monument dedicated to a freedwoman, likely by her son and Gaius, and possibly includes their own burials.

EDCS-11201618.summary: This is a fragment mentioning a veteran, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory inscription.

EDCS-11201619.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that mentions a veteran, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-11201621.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Agathopus vixit nnos XXXV Aelia Valentina uxor bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11201621.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Agathopus lived 35 years. Aelia Valentina, his wife, placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11201621.summary: This funerary inscription records Aelia Valentina dedicating a tomb to her deceased husband, Publius Aelius Agathopus, who lived to be 35 years old.

EDCS-11201622.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius  orill vivus vivis fecit salvus sis qui legis
EDCS-11201622.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius ...orill, while living, made this for the living. May you be safe who reads this.
EDCS-11201622.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Aelius for himself and his family while still alive, invoking the Divine Manes and offering a blessing to the reader, suggesting a date in the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201623.text_edition: Aelius Meritus avunculus eorum E bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-11201623.translation: Aelius Meritus, their uncle, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11201623.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aelius Meritus, an uncle, to his deserving relatives, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-11201624.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Birulius filius Anticho Suro casum sic fecit die sic III Kalendarum sic Septenbrium sic
EDCS-11201624.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Birulius, son, made this for Antichus Syrus, who died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-11201624.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelius Birulius, the son, dedicating a monument to Antichus Syrus and noting his date of death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201625.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Cornelius Quinti libertus Zosimus vixit annos LX Cornelia Zosime filia et Cornelius Trophimus libertus et coheredes fecerunt hic situs est
EDCS-11201625.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Cornelius Zosimus, freedman of Quintus, lived 60 years. Cornelia Zosime, his daughter, and Cornelius Trophimus, his freedman, and his co-heirs made this. He is buried here.
EDCS-11201625.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a tomb for Quintus Cornelius Zosimus by his daughter Cornelia Zosime, his freedman Cornelius Trophimus, and his co-heirs, marking his burial place.

EDCS-11201627.text_edition: Eusebius filius Antianu sic civis Germaniceus ex vico Abdarmisu sic qui bixit sic annus sic XXIII qui filius patri facere debebat ego iam
EDCS-11201627.translation: Eusebius, son of Antianus, a German citizen from the village of Abdarmisu, who lived 23 years, who ought to have made this for his father, now I...
EDCS-11201627.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription, likely for Eusebius, a German citizen, made by someone lamenting that the son, who died at 23, should have been the one to bury his father, not the other way around.

EDCS-11201628.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Iulius Prispientissimus, who lived 66 years.

EDCS-11201629.text_edition: a XIX cuius post obitum Marcianus pudicitiae vinctus amore dicat hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-11201629.translation: ...at 19, after whose death Marcianus, bound by love for her chastity, dedicates this monument. It will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-11201629.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcianus, possibly a husband or partner, to a woman who died at 19, emphasizing her chastity and ensuring the monument will not be inherited by others.

EDCS-11201635.text_edition: Dis Manibus ius  filius Iul  vixit annos V ia Titia   annos IV pat
EDCS-11201635.translation: To the Divine Manes. ...son of Iulius, lived 5 years. ...Titia, ... 4 years. Father...
EDCS-11201635.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, recording the deaths of a son of Iulius, who lived 5 years, and a person named Titia, who lived 4 years, presumably erected by their father.

EDCS-11201639.text_edition: XV IApollonius  coniugi bene merenti cum filia posuit
EDCS-11201639.translation: 15. Apollonius placed this for his well-deserving wife with their daughter.
EDCS-11201639.summary: This is a funerary monument placed by Apollonius for his well-deserving wife and their daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201647.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a freedman, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-11201651.text_edition: ani  Valgia  ima coniugi bene merenti p(osuit)
EDCS-11201651.translation: Valgia set this up for [---]anima, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11201651.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Valgia for her deceased husband.

EDCS-11201656.text_edition: Marina honista sic femina cultrix dei quae vixit cum virginio suo annos XXX Publio LIicinio#Licinio#IICIN Constanciolo sic sita est
EDCS-11201656.translation: Marina, an honest woman, a worshipper of God, who lived with her husband for 30 years, is buried here with Publius Licinius Constantiolus.
EDCS-11201656.summary: This funerary inscription identifies Marina as an honest woman and worshipper of God, noting she lived with her husband for 30 years and is buried with Publius Licinius Constantiolus, likely her husband, indicating a Christian context.

EDCS-11201657.text_edition: Sitha quae quiescit in pace Romae ABIDVA nnorum XLV deposita VI Idus Februarias
EDCS-11201657.translation: Sitha, who rests in peace in Rome, a constant woman, 45 years old, laid to rest on the 6th day before the Ides of February.
EDCS-11201657.summary: This Christian funerary inscription records the death and burial date of a woman named Sitha in Rome, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-11201661.text_edition: Cai Iuli Iuniani diabsoricum sic ad aspritudinem et claritatem  Cai Iuli Iuniani crocodes aspritudinis  Cai Iulii?  O C on croc(odes CC
EDCS-11201661.translation: Of Gaius Julius Junianus, 'diabsoricum' for roughness and clarity. Of Gaius Julius Junianus, 'crocodes' for roughness. Of Gaius Julius...? O C on croc(odes) 200.
EDCS-11201661.summary: This inscription appears to be a list or catalog of medical or chemical preparations associated with Gaius Julius Junianus, detailing substances like 'diabsoricum' and 'crocodes' for specific properties.

EDCS-11201666.text_edition: nus rcianus enus nus itanus    Marcus Aurelius I Marcus Aurelius F Marcus Ulpius  Caius Iulius     IND
EDCS-11201666.translation: ...nus Marcianus enus nus itanus... Marcus Aurelius I, Marcus Aurelius F, Marcus Ulpius, Gaius Julius... IND...
EDCS-11201666.summary: This fragment appears to be a list of names, likely recording individuals with typical Roman nomina and cognomina, possibly part of a larger administrative or commemorative document.

EDCS-11201667.text_edition: s ns s ns tius    Caius Iulius  Titus Aelius M Caius Valerius  Caius Iulius
EDCS-11201667.translation: [---]tius, Gaius Iulius, Titus Aelius M. Gaius Valerius, Gaius Iulius.
EDCS-11201667.summary: This fragmentary inscription lists a series of Roman names, possibly indicating a group of individuals or co-dedicants, but its exact purpose is unclear due to its fragmentary nature.

EDCS-11201668.summary: This is a very brief inscription listing two names, Maurico and Marcus Valerius, whose relationship or purpose is unclear due to its brevity.

EDCS-11201718.text_edition: Marti sacrum Titus Flavius Sapi ex viso
EDCS-11201718.translation: Sacred to Mars. Titus Flavius Sapi [set this up] according to a vision.
EDCS-11201718.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Mars, made by Titus Flavius Sapi in response to a divine vision, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201719.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription to Silvanus Domesticus, a Roman deity associated with forests and the household, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11201722.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the god Hero[n], with Uponsus likely being the dedicator, but its full context is lost.

EDCS-11301514.text_edition: Aesculapio et Saluti Titus Flavius Adiectus
EDCS-11301514.translation: To Aesculapius and Salus. Titus Flavius Adiectus [made this offering].
EDCS-11301514.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Roman healing deities Aesculapius and Salus, made by Titus Flavius Adiectus, likely as a vow fulfilled or for health.

EDCS-11301516.text_edition: Dianae Augustae  Titus Aelius Mucianus et Tullia coniux ex voto posuerunt et templum fecerunt
EDCS-11301516.translation: To Diana Augusta. Titus Aelius Mucianus and Tullia, his wife, placed this ex voto and made the temple.
EDCS-11301516.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Titus Aelius Mucianus and his wife Tullia fulfilling a vow by dedicating a temple to Diana Augusta, a Roman goddess, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11301517.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Marcus Aurelius Quadratianus tribunus templum a solo ex voto restituit
EDCS-11301517.translation: To Diana Augusta, Marcus Aurelius Quadratianus, a tribune, restored the temple from its foundations, as a vow.
