EDCS-16800239.translation: To Hercules the Invincible. Aurelius Herodes and Julius Super, for the health of their college, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-16800239.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules the Invincible by Aurelius Herodes and Julius Super for the welfare of their college, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-21000721.text_edition: Legio VII Claudia legatus Augusti S---  Aurelius Herculanus  Martinianus fecit XX
EDCS-21000721.translation: The Seventh Claudian Legion, legate of Augustus. Aurelius Herculanus Martinianus made [this] for the twentieth [time/work].
EDCS-21000721.summary: This is a building or milestone inscription, recording a construction or restoration project by the Legio VII Claudia under an Augustan legate, possibly Aurelius Herculanus Martinianus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-24500535.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Genio castrorum legionis IIII Flaviae Nassius Dexter praefectus [castrorum &
EDCS-24500535.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the Genius of the camp of the Fourth Flavian Legion. Nassius Dexter, prefect of the camp [placed this].
EDCS-24500535.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the camp of Legio IIII Flavia by Nassius Dexter, the camp prefect, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600680.text_edition: Dis deabus  Placidi filius Ni Ulpianus laetus  ex voto pro  libens posuit
EDCS-26600680.translation: To the gods and goddesses. Ulpianus, son of Placidi, happily and freely placed this from a vow on behalf of ---.
EDCS-26600680.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the gods and goddesses by Ulpianus, son of Placidi, made joyfully and willingly in fulfillment of a vow, likely for a personal or family benefit.

EDCS-26600681.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Aurelius Gaius miles legionis VII strator consularis posuit
EDCS-26600681.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of our lords the Augusti, Aurelius Gaius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, a consular strator, placed this.
EDCS-26600681.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Gaius, a soldier of the Legio VII (likely Claudia) and a consular strator, for the health of the reigning emperors, probably from the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600682.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Marcus Aurelius Valens miles legionis IIII strator consularis posuit
EDCS-26600682.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our Lords Augusti, Marcus Aurelius Valens, a soldier of the Fourth Legion, consular strator, set this up.
EDCS-26600682.summary: This is a votive offering dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier, Marcus Aurelius Valens, for the health of the reigning emperors.

EDCS-26600683.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Augusti Marcus Ulpius Martinus strator consularis legionis VII ClI(audiae)#Cl(audiae)#CI Severianae Fusco et Dextro consulibus
EDCS-26600683.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our Lord Augustus, Marcus Ulpius Martinus, consular strator of the Seventh Claudian Severian Legion, in the consulship of Fuscus and Dexter.
EDCS-26600683.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the emperor's health, made by a military officer, Marcus Ulpius Martinus, and dated to the consulship of Fuscus and Dexter (217 CE).

EDCS-26600684.text_edition: Iovi Optimo F Maximo Capitolino ex vBoto#voto#BOTO posuerunt Nica Hermes pro salute domini nostri
EDCS-26600684.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, Nica and Hermes set this up from a vow, for the well-being of our Lord.
EDCS-26600684.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, dedicated by Nica and Hermes to fulfill a vow, for the health of the emperor.

EDCS-26600685.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Capitolino Deo Ulpius Rufus ex voto posuit
EDCS-26600685.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, God. Ulpius Rufus placed this from a vow.
EDCS-26600685.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus by Ulpius Rufus, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600687.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori et Iunoni Reginae et Deo Libero et Hilarae Gaius Iulius Servandus veteranus et Flavii Numisius et Numisianus
EDCS-26600687.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator and Juno Regina and Liber and Hilara, Gaius Julius Servandus, a veteran, and Flavii Numisius and Numisianus [set this up].
EDCS-26600687.summary: This is a dedication to a pantheon of deities—Jupiter, Juno, Liber, and Hilara—made by a veteran, Gaius Julius Servandus, and two Flavii, Numisius and Numisianus.

EDCS-26600688.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caia Aurelia Rufina vixit nnos XXX Titus Claudius Valerianus cornicularis tribuni coniugi pientissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-26600688.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Caia Aurelia Rufina lived 30 years. Titus Claudius Valerianus, a cornicularius of the tribune, saw to the erection of this monument for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-26600688.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Caia Aurelia Rufina, set up by her husband, Titus Claudius Valerianus, a military administrative officer.

EDCS-26600689.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Vindici veterano vixit nnos LX et Aeliae Serenae eius vixit annos XXX Cai Iuli Hermocora sic Nicias Florus Mestrius et Tzitzis liberti et Aelius Fecundus Cosmus bene merentibus faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-26600689.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Julius Vindex, a veteran, who lived 60 years, and Aelia Serena, his [wife], who lived 30 years. Gaius Julius Hermocora, Nicias Florus, Mestrius, and Tzitzis, freedmen, and Aelius Fecundus Cosmus, saw to the erection of this monument for the well-deserving.
EDCS-26600689.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Julius Vindex, a veteran, and his wife, Aelia Serena, erected by their freedmen and Aelius Fecundus Cosmus.

EDCS-26600690.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Aelianus vixit nnos XXV Atilia Ingenua coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600690.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ulpius Aelianus lived 25 years. Atilia Ingenua placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-26600690.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Aelianus, set up by his wife, Atilia Ingenua.

EDCS-26600691.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Valentina vixit annis XX Aurelius Mi
EDCS-26600691.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelia Valentina lived for 20 years. Aurelius Mi--.
EDCS-26600691.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Aurelia Valentina, who lived for 20 years, likely set up by Aurelius Mi--, presumably a family member.

EDCS-26600692.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertenaci sic Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori XI consuli III proconsuli res publica sua Ulpiana curante Quinto Anicio Fausto legato Augustorum pro praetore
EDCS-26600692.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, son of divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of divine Commodus, grandson of divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of divine Nerva, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the tenth time, acclaimed imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the third time, proconsul. The Ulpian Res Publica [set this up], with Quintus Anicius Faustus, legatus Augustorum pro praetore, in charge.
EDCS-26600692.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus, detailing his extensive imperial titles and ancestry, erected by the Ulpian community under the charge of their legate, likely dating to 202 CE (based on tribunician power X).

EDCS-26600693.text_edition: Iuliae Domnae Augustae matri castrorum res publica sua Ulpiana curante Quinto Anicio Fausto legato Augustorum pro praetore
EDCS-26600693.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, Mother of the Camps. The Ulpian Res Publica [set this up], with Quintus Anicius Faustus, legatus Augustorum pro praetore, in charge.
EDCS-26600693.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Empress Julia Domna, recognizing her title 'Mother of the Camps,' erected by the Ulpian community under the charge of their legate, Quintus Anicius Faustus.

EDCS-26600694.text_edition: Maximo et super omnes fortissimo Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae
EDCS-26600694.translation: To the greatest and most valiant Imperator Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Country.
EDCS-26600694.summary: This is an honorific dedication to Emperor Marcus Julius Philippus, also known as Philip the Arab, listing his numerous imperial titles and virtues.

EDCS-26600696.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Valens miles legionis IIII Flaviae vixit nnos XXXI militavit nnos VII Valerius Valerianus et Valeria Candida fratri carissimo et sivi sic vivi posuerunt
EDCS-26600696.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valerius Valens, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, lived 31 years and served 7 years. Valerius Valerianus and Valeria Candida placed this for their dearest brother and for themselves while living.
EDCS-26600696.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, dedicated by Valerius Valerianus and Valeria Candida to their brother Valerius Valens, a soldier of the Legio IIII Flavia, and for themselves.

EDCS-26600698.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno  pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris kastrorum Achilleus eorundem servus posuit Kalendis Novembribus Sabino II et Anulino consulibus
EDCS-26600698.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the well-being of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, Achilleus, their servant, placed this on the Kalends of November, in the consulship of Sabinus II and Anulinus.
EDCS-26600698.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus, made by Achilleus, a servant of the imperial household, for the health of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna in 220 AD.

EDCS-26600699.text_edition: Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto imperatore pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXXV legio IIII Scythica legio V Macedonica
EDCS-26600699.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of Augustus, Augustus, imperator, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 35th time. The Fourth Scythian Legion, the Fifth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-26600699.summary: This inscription records the legions associated with an imperial dedication to Tiberius, likely a milestone or building inscription from 33-34 AD.

EDCS-26600700.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII pater patriae consul III montibus excisis anconibus sublatis viam refecit
EDCS-26600700.translation: Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus, son of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, father of his country, consul for the 3rd time, restored the road by cutting down mountains and raising retaining walls.
EDCS-26600700.summary: This is a monumental inscription, probably a milestone, commemorating Emperor Trajan's road construction work, likely dated to 100 AD.

EDCS-26600705.text_edition: Genio castrorum legionis IIII Flaviae felicis et ddominorum nnostrorum Diocletiani et Maximiani Auggustorum Aurelius Maximianus ex praefecto legionis eiusdem votum posuit
EDCS-26600705.translation: To the Genius of the camp of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion and of our lords Diocletian and Maximian Augusti, Aurelius Maximianus, former prefect of the same legion, placed this vow.
EDCS-26600705.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix and the emperors Diocletian and Maximian, by Aurelius Maximianus, a former legionary prefect, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-26600707.text_edition: Dis Manibus Genis Domiti vixit nnos LX et Affuti Aristonis coniugi eius vivo Aelius Domitianus immunis librarius legionis IIII Flaviae parentibus piissimis posuit
EDCS-26600707.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Genis Domiti, who lived 60 years, and of his wife Affutia Aristona, while she was living. Aelius Domitianus, an exempt clerk of the Fourth Flavian Legion, placed this for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-26600707.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Domitianus, a librarius of the Legio IIII Flavia, to his parents, Genis Domiti and Affutia Aristona, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600708.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Victorino viro eF(gregio)#e(gregio)#F infelicissimo iuveni qui vixit annos XXV menses VIIII dies VII Aelius Proculinus signifer legionis IIII Flaviae fratri piissimo bene merenti
EDCS-26600708.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aelius Victorinus, a most unfortunate excellent young man, who lived 25 years, 9 months, 7 days. Aelius Proculinus, standard-bearer of the Fourth Flavian Legion, placed this for his most devoted and well-deserving brother.
EDCS-26600708.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Proculinus, a signifer of the Legio IIII Flavia, to his brother, Aelius Victorinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600709.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Blassius Nigellio speculator legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XXXV
EDCS-26600709.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Blassius Nigellio, a speculator of the Seventh Claudia Legion, lived 35 years.
EDCS-26600709.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Blassius Nigellio, a speculator of the Legio VII Claudia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600710.text_edition: Dis Manibus Oppiae Agatheni matri vixit annos LX et Iulio Quadrato patri veterano legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos LXV Herculano fratri vixit annos VIII Iulius Bassus miles legionis III Parthicae strator consularis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-26600710.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Oppia Agathene, his mother, who lived 60 years, and to Julius Quadratus, his father, a veteran of the Seventh Claudia Legion, who lived 65 years, and to Herculanus, his brother, who lived 8 years. Julius Bassus, a soldier of the Third Parthian Legion, a consular cavalryman, placed this for his well-deserving relatives.
EDCS-26600710.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julius Bassus, a strator consularis of the Legio III Parthica, to his mother, Oppia Agathene, his father, Julius Quadratus (a veteran of Legio VII Claudia), and his brother Herculanus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600711.text_edition: Caius Refidius Cai libertus Eutychus faber argentarius vixit  hic situs est Caius Refidius Rufus optio legionis IIII Flaviae felicis heres posuit
EDCS-26600711.translation: Gaius Refidius Eutychus, freedman of Gaius, a silver craftsman, lived [---]. He is buried here. Gaius Refidius Rufus, an optio of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, his heir, placed this.
EDCS-26600711.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Refidius Eutychus, a silver craftsman, dedicated by his heir, Gaius Refidius Rufus, an optio of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600715.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Chr=RHestos#Chrestos#CRHESTOS Tito Flavio Iuliano filio anni I monumentum posuit
EDCS-26600715.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Chrestos placed this monument for his son, Titus Flavius Iulianus, who lived for one year.
EDCS-26600715.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Flavius Chrestos to his son, Titus Flavius Iulianus, who died at the age of one.

EDCS-26600716.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Iulius Seneca vixit annos XL Ulpia Nigrina
EDCS-26600716.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Julius Seneca lived for 40 years. Ulpia Nigrina.
EDCS-26600716.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Quintus Julius Seneca, who lived for 40 years, erected by Ulpia Nigrina, presumably a relative.

EDCS-26600717.translation: To the Divine Manes. Proculus [son] of Cipeius.
EDCS-26600717.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Proculus, likely by his father Cipeius, as indicated by the patronymic form.

EDCS-27400513.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Epistratus Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus a splendidissimo ordine
EDCS-27400513.translation: Marcus Ulpius Epistratus, Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, honored with the ornaments of a decurion by the most splendid order.
EDCS-27400513.summary: This inscription records Marcus Ulpius Epistratus, an Augustalis of Ratiaria, being honored with decurional ornaments by the local council.

EDCS-27400515.text_edition: Deae Orciae sacrum pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Diocletiani et Maximiani Auggustorum ordo amplissimus splendidissimae coloniae Singiduni per Marcos Aurelios Cutiam et Sosonem IIviros curante Simplicio viro egregio dominis nostris Deiocletiano sic III et Maximiano Augustis consulibus
EDCS-27400515.translation: Sacred to the Goddess Orcia, for the welfare of our lords Diocletian and Maximian, Augusti. The most ample order of the most splendid colony of Singidunum, through Marcus Aurelius Cutia and Marcus Aurelius Soson, duumvirs, with Simplicius, excellent man, overseeing, when our lords Diocletian and Maximian were consuls for the third time.
EDCS-27400515.summary: This is a religious dedication to the goddess Orcia for the health of Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, made by the decurional order of Singidunum, overseen by duumvirs and an official, and dated to the third consulship of the emperors.

EDCS-27400516.text_edition: Volcano et  et Valerius Varius IIviri et Aureli la et Maximinus aediles et Aureli Mucianus et Ingenianus quaestores Quieto et Veldemiano sic consulibus
EDCS-27400516.translation: To Vulcan and --- and Valerius Varius, duumvirs, and Aurelii La and Maximinus, aediles, and Aurelii Mucianus and Ingenianus, quaestors, when Quietus and Veldemianus were consuls.
EDCS-27400516.summary: This inscription records the names of various municipal officials—duumvirs, aediles, and quaestors—and is dated to the consulship of Quietus and Veldemianus.

EDCS-27400517.text_edition: M - Iovi votum Aelius Marcus Apamenus donum dedit
EDCS-27400517.translation: To Jupiter, Aelius Marcus Apamenus gave this gift as a vow.
EDCS-27400517.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter by Aelius Marcus Apamenus.

EDCS-27400518.text_edition: Lucio Calpurnio Tertio veterano ex aquilifero legionis IIII Flaviae felicis vixit nnos XXX Lucius Calpurnius INAMORIO TIVS CONCITATVS armatura legionis eiusdem
EDCS-27400518.translation: To Lucius Calpurnius Tertius, a veteran from the standard-bearer corps of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, who lived for 30 years. Lucius Calpurnius Inamorius Tivs Concitatus, from the armor-bearers of the same legion, [set this].
EDCS-27400518.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Calpurnius Tertius, a veteran standard-bearer of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, set up by Lucius Calpurnius Inamorius Tivs Concitatus, an armor-bearer from the same legion.

EDCS-27400520.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Piae domo Vindobona vixit annos XXI Aurelius Maximus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae coniugi pientissimae et Ulpiae Gaiae socrae et matri eius et sibi vivus
EDCS-27400520.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Ulpia Pia, from Vindobona, who lived 21 years. Aurelius Maximus, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, [set this] for his most devoted wife, and for Ulpia Gaia, her mother-in-law and his mother, and for himself while living.
EDCS-27400520.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Maximus, a veteran of Legio IIII Flavia, to his deceased wife Ulpia Pia, his mother-in-law Ulpia Gaia, and also for himself, anticipating his own death.

EDCS-27400521.text_edition: D O  SemproEnio#Sempronio#SEMPRENIO Cobae principi vixit nnos VI Claudiae Bubae et Dotus fPilis#filis#PIL CAE Daizinis uxori eius et filiis eorum pater Valens beneficiarius praefecti EV  IA Singiduniensi
EDCS-27400521.translation: To the Best and Greatest God. For Sempronius Coba, a princeps, who lived for 6 years. To Claudia Buba and Dotus [Pilis], his wife Daizinis, and their children, his father Valens, a beneficiarius of the prefect of Singidunum, [set this].
EDCS-27400521.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Sempronius Coba, a 'princeps', made by his father Valens, a beneficiarius of the prefect of Singidunum, for Coba, his wife, and their children.

EDCS-27400529.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius N Severus vixit
EDCS-27400529.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius N. Severus lived.
EDCS-27400529.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius N. Severus, noting his existence, likely marking a tombstone or burial monument.

EDCS-27400532.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Chr=RHeste#Chreste#CRHESTE vixit nnos XLII hic sita est Velox unicus maritus Iulianus Valentina Velocianus filii matri pientissimae bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-27400532.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Chreste lived 42 years; she lies here. Velox, her only husband, and Iulianus, Valentina, Velocianus, her children, ensured this was made for their most devoted and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-27400532.summary: This is a funerary monument for Flavia Chreste, erected by her husband Velox and their children, Iulianus, Valentina, and Velocianus.

EDCS-27800783.text_edition: Aurelius Lucianus miles legionis IIII Flaviae vixit annis XXXV Cemeia mater et Aelia ca coniux carissima et fratres posuerunt  bene merenti Dis Manibus
EDCS-27800783.translation: Aurelius Lucianus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, lived for 35 years. His mother Cemeia and his dearest wife Aelia [---]ca and his brothers placed this for the well-deserving. To the Divine Spirits.
EDCS-27800783.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Lucianus, a soldier of Legio IIII Flavia, set up by his mother Cemeia, his wife Aelia, and his brothers.

EDCS-27800786.text_edition: Nonius SaIgnus qui et signo Dalmatius beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae vixit annos XXXX menses III dies XVIII stipendiorum XXIII Iulia Heraclia coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27800786.translation: Iulia Heraclia placed this for her well-deserving husband Nonius Saignus, also known as Dalmatius, a consular beneficiarius of the Fourth Flavian Legion, who lived 40 years, 3 months, 18 days, and served for 23 years.
EDCS-27800786.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Heraclia to her husband, Nonius Saignus (Dalmatius), a consular beneficiarius of Legio IIII Flavia, recording his age and years of service.

EDCS-27800788.text_edition: Alma lavacrorum de saxis decido lympha et sunt ex lapide perfectae balneae pulchrae laetis inque locis natus lacus id tamen ipsis tunc cum sospes erat coniux sacravit in usum emeritis quondam Alexandreo nomine dignae raucisoni lapidoso cadunt de fonte liquores tam laudati operis dominus famosus et auctor in suae memoriam voluit consecrare maritae ut tamen et lector nomen cognoscere possis singulae declarant exordia litterae primae Aelia cum Tertia subole de coniuge Fausta
EDCS-27800788.translation: I, the nurturing spring, fall from the rocks, a clear stream, and beautiful baths, built of stone, are perfect. A lake was born in pleasant places, yet my husband consecrated this to their use when he was safe, a worthy offering for those once honored by the name Alexander. Murmuring waters fall from a stony spring, and the famous master and author of such praised work wished to consecrate it in memory of his wife. So that you, reader, may know the name, the first letters of each line declare it: 'Aelia cum Tertia subole de coniuge Fausta' (Aelia, with her third child, from her wife Fausta).
EDCS-27800788.summary: This lengthy poetic inscription dedicates a complex of baths and a lake, built by a famous master, in memory of his wife, Aelia Fausta, and also mentions their third child, with the name of the master perhaps hinted at by an acrostic.

EDCS-27800789.text_edition: In honorem Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti Caius Memmius Cyriacus Augustalis IIviralibus ornamentis  ab ordine et ab ordine in se conlatum statuam argenteam ex pondo libris XL
EDCS-27800789.translation: In honor of the Emperor Caesar Augustus, Gaius Memmius Cyriacus, Augustalis, gifted with the ornaments of a duumvir by the order, dedicated a silver statue weighing 40 pounds.
EDCS-27800789.summary: This inscription records Gaius Memmius Cyriacus, an Augustalis and recipient of duumviral honors, dedicating a 40-pound silver statue to the Emperor Caesar Augustus.

EDCS-27800790.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Quesidio Cai filio Praesenti decurioni et quinquennali primo municipii Publi Aeli Dru et decurioni municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit annos LXIII Lucius Quesidius Praesentianus filius et Lucilius Regulinus IIvir quinquennalis municipii Aelii et Aurelius Florus quinquennalis municipii Aeli Viminacii sribendum curaverunt hic situs est
EDCS-27800790.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Quesidius Praesens, son of Gaius, decurion and first quinquennial of the municipality of Publius Aelius Drus and decurion of the municipality of Aelia Viminacium, who lived 63 years. Lucius Quesidius Praesentianus, his son, and Lucilius Regulinus, duumvir quinquennial of the Aelian municipality, and Aurelius Florus, quinquennial of the municipality of Aelia Viminacium, arranged for this to be written. Here he lies.
EDCS-27800790.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Quesidius Praesens, a respected decurion and quinquennial of two municipalities, erected by his son and two other quinquennales.

EDCS-27800791.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Telestae vixit annos XXXX Tittha mater heres bene merenti fecit
EDCS-27800791.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Telestas lived 40 years. Tittha, his mother and heir, made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-27800791.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Telestas, who lived 40 years, dedicated by his mother and heir, Tittha.

EDCS-27800792.text_edition: coniugi  et sibi viva   cum filiis
EDCS-27800792.translation: To her husband and to herself, alive, with her sons.
EDCS-27800792.summary: This inscription indicates a monument set up by a woman for herself and her husband while she was still living, along with her sons.

EDCS-27800793.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus templum Terrae Matris conlapsum restituit sub cura Cassi Ligurini procuratoris Augusti instantia sic Publio Fundanio Eutychete et Publio Aelio Muciano colonis
EDCS-27800793.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus restored the collapsed temple of Terra Mater, under the care of Cassius Ligurinus, procurator Augusti, at the instance of Publius Fundanius Eutyches and Publius Aelius Mucianus, colonists.
EDCS-27800793.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a collapsed temple of Terra Mater by Emperor Septimius Severus, overseen by a procurator and two colonists.

EDCS-27800794.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aemilia Sda vixit nnos  Tiberius Claudius Ins coniugi e
EDCS-27800794.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aemilia Sda lived for [many] years. Tiberius Claudius Ins [set this up] for his wife.
EDCS-27800794.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tiberius Claudius Ins to his wife, Aemilia Sda, recording her lifespan and the dedication by her husband.

EDCS-29000035.text_edition: Dolicheno Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Surianus ex voto renovavit
EDCS-29000035.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Aurelius Surianus renewed this according to a vow.
EDCS-29000035.summary: This inscription records the renewal of a monument or offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Aurelius Surianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29000036.text_edition: IL Aurelius Nicomachus beneficiarius consularis legionis VII Claudiae animo libens votum solvit Imperatore Antonino IIII et Balbino II consulibus
EDCS-29000036.translation: Lucius Aurelius Nicomachus, beneficiarius consularis of the Seventh Claudian Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow when Antoninus was consul for the fourth time and Balbinus for the second time.
EDCS-29000036.summary: This inscription records Lucius Aurelius Nicomachus, a beneficiarius of the Legio VII Claudia, fulfilling a vow during the consulate of Antoninus (IV) and Balbinus (II), dating it to 236 CE.

EDCS-29000037.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Pisto militavit annis XXVII beneficiarius vixit annis LXXIII Valeria Faventina coniux vixit annis LXXII Valerius Paulinus tribunus legionis Vincentius Exsuperius et Pistus circitor filii Sisia filia patri bene merenti sepulchrum honoraverunt
EDCS-29000037.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Pistus, who served 27 years as a beneficiarius and lived 73 years. Valeria Faventina, his wife, lived 72 years. Valerius Paulinus, tribune of the legion, Vincentius Exsuperius, and Pistus, circitor, his sons, and Sisia, his daughter, honored their well-deserving father with this tomb.
EDCS-29000037.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Pistus, a beneficiarius who served 27 years, and his wife Valeria Faventina, erected by their children, including a tribune and two other sons and a daughter.

EDCS-29000039.text_edition: Lucio Anatinio Luci liberto Phoebo Augustali coloniae Ulpiae Ratiariae ornato ornamentis decurionalibus immunitate munerum publicorum concessa ab ordine coloniae eiusdem Augustales coloniae eiusdem aere conlato decreto decurionum
EDCS-29000039.translation: For Lucius Anatinus Phoebus, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis of the colony of Ulpia Ratiaria, adorned with decurional ornaments, with immunity from public duties granted by the order of the same colony. The Augustales of the same colony, by collected money and decree of the decurions, dedicated this.
EDCS-29000039.summary: This inscription records the Augustales of Ulpia Ratiaria dedicating a monument to Lucius Anatinus Phoebus, an Augustalis and freedman who received decurional honors and immunity from public duties.

EDCS-29000040.text_edition: Augustales coloniae Ulpiae Ratiariae Tito Iulio Paletho ornato decurionalibus ornamantis
EDCS-29000040.translation: The Augustales of the colony of Ulpia Ratiaria dedicated this to Titus Julius Palethus, adorned with decurional ornaments.
EDCS-29000040.summary: This inscription records the Augustales of Ulpia Ratiaria dedicating a monument to Titus Julius Palethus, who had received decurional honors.

EDCS-29000041.text_edition: elius Sura fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29000041.translation: Aelius Sura placed this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-29000041.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelius Sura in honor of his brother, indicating a fraternal dedication.

EDCS-29000042.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Macedo |(centurio) legionis VII Claudiae stipendiorum XXVI probatus Paullo et Aproniano consulibus vixit annis L socer eius bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29000042.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Valerius Macedo, centurion of the Seventh Claudian Legion, served 26 years, proved his worth when Paullus and Apronianus were consuls, and lived 50 years. His father-in-law placed this for his well-deserving son-in-law.
EDCS-29000042.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Macedo, a centurion of Legio VII Claudia, who served 26 years and died at 50, erected by his father-in-law in 167 CE during the consulate of Paullus and Apronianus.

EDCS-29000043.text_edition: XXX Flavius Iucundusr et Aeli Anns et Iucundus s et Gaia heredes  Gaianus filii C hic sit- est
EDCS-29000043.translation: Flavius Iucundus and Aelia Anna and Iucundus and Gaia, heirs of Gaianus, their son, are buried here.
EDCS-29000043.summary: This is a funerary inscription from heirs for Gaianus, their son.

EDCS-29000044.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cnaeus Clodius Cnaei filius Classicianus tribunus cohortis XIIX voluntariorum civium Romanorum et translatus ab optimis maximisque Imperatoribus in cohortem I Ulpiam Pannoniorum |(miliariam) equitatam votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29000044.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gnaeus Clodius Classicianus, son of Gnaeus, tribune of the 18th Cohort of Roman Citizen Volunteers, and transferred by the best and greatest emperors to the 1st Ulpian Cohort of Pannonians, a thousand-strong cavalry unit, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29000044.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a Roman military tribune, Gnaeus Clodius Classicianus, commemorating his service and transfer to a cavalry unit.

EDCS-29000045.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Iulianus miles libens posuit merito
EDCS-29000045.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus. Aurelius Iulianus, a soldier, willingly and deservedly placed this.
EDCS-29000045.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus by Aurelius Iulianus, a soldier, expressing his willing and deserving dedication.

EDCS-29000046.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Iulius SepRtumus sic#Septumus sic#SERTUMUS votum posuit
EDCS-29000046.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris. Iulius Septumus placed this vow.
EDCS-29000046.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to the god Silvanus Silvestris by Iulius Septumus.

EDCS-29000047.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Faustinianus signifer legionis IIII Flaviae vixit nnos XXXII militavit nnos XII Faustina filia vixit nnum I mensem I Aelia Maxima coniugi et filiae bene merentibus posuit sibi ipsa heres
EDCS-29000047.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Aurelius Faustinianus, standard-bearer of the 4th Flavian Legion, lived 32 years, served 12 years. Faustina, his daughter, lived 1 year and 1 month. Aelia Maxima placed this for her well-deserving husband and daughter, and as her own heir.
EDCS-29000047.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia Maxima for her husband, Aurelius Faustinianus, a standard-bearer of the Legio IIII Flavia, and their daughter Faustina, with a provision for herself as heir.

EDCS-29000048.text_edition: rior decurio municipii Singiduni vixit nnos XXV et Cais Cassiis Verae vixit annis XIII et Firmio vixit annis IIII filiis eorum Cassia Firma filia heres parentibus bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-29000048.translation: Decurion of the municipality of Singidunum, lived 25 years, and Cassia Vera lived 13 years, and Firmius lived 4 years, their sons. Cassia Firma, their daughter and heir, placed this for her well-deserving parents.
EDCS-29000048.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Cassia Firma for her parents, a decurion of Singidunum and Cassia Vera, and their two sons.

EDCS-29000049.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corneliae Cai filiae Priscae quae vixit nnis XVI mensibus VI diebus XVIII Cornelius Priscus RISVIRC
EDCS-29000049.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: for Cornelia Prisca, daughter of Gaius, who lived 16 years, 6 months, 18 days. Cornelius Priscus...
EDCS-29000049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Prisca, a young woman who lived 16 years, 6 months, and 18 days, erected by Cornelius Priscus.

EDCS-29000517.translation: To Gaius Dindius Blandus, and to Octavia Quarta, daughter of Publius, and to Gaius Dindius Blandus, their son, who lived 8 years.
EDCS-29000517.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by parents to their deceased son, Gaius Dindius Blandus, and also commemorating themselves.

EDCS-29100119.text_edition: Dis Manibus memoriae Numitoriae Proculae Cai Numitori Senecae veterani legionis VII Claudiae
EDCS-29100119.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: in memory of Numitoria Procula, (wife of) Gaius Numitorius Seneca, veteran of the 7th Claudian Legion.
EDCS-29100119.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Numitoria Procula, likely the wife of a veteran of the Legio VII Claudia, Gaius Numitorius Seneca.

EDCS-29100120.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Aebutio Luci filio Papiria Grato vixit annos LVI hic situs est Aebutia Marcia coniugi
EDCS-29100120.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: for Lucius Aebutius Gratus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, who lived 56 years; he is buried here. Aebutia Marcia (placed this) for her husband.
EDCS-29100120.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aebutia Marcia for her husband, Lucius Aebutius Gratus, who lived 56 years.

EDCS-29100121.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Aurelio Diocletiano Pio Felici Invicto Augsto
EDCS-29100121.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Aurelius Diocletianus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus.
EDCS-29100121.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to the Emperor Diocletian, listing his full titulature and common imperial epithets, likely from a statue base or public monument around the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-29100122.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Aurelius Andronicus veteranus ex signifero legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos LIII Lucius Aurelius Salvianus librarius consularis filius
EDCS-29100122.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Lucius Aurelius Andronicus, a veteran and former standard-bearer of the 7th Claudian Legion, lived 53 years. Lucius Aurelius Salvianus, consular clerk, his son.
EDCS-29100122.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aurelius Andronicus, a veteran standard-bearer of the Legio VII Claudia, erected by his son, Lucius Aurelius Salvianus, a consular clerk.

EDCS-29100123.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Speratus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae ex beneficiario consularis decurio municipii Aeli Viminacii praefectus cohortis I Aquetanorum sic vixit annos LV obitus in Brittannia Lucia Afrodisia coniugi bene merenti et sibi vivae posuit
EDCS-29100123.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Marcus Valerius Speratus, veteran of the 7th Claudian Legion, former beneficiarius consularis, decurion of the municipality of Aeli Viminacium, prefect of the 1st Aquitanian cohort, lived 55 years; he died in Britain. Lucia Afrodisia placed this for her well-deserving husband and for herself while alive.
EDCS-29100123.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucia Afrodisia for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Valerius Speratus, a distinguished veteran who held several military and municipal offices and died in Britain, and also for herself while still alive.

EDCS-29100124.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Barbio Luci liberto Nymphodoto vixit nnos XVII Barbius Delphicus et Quarta parentes faciendum curaverunt hic situs est
EDCS-29100124.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: for Lucius Barbius Nymphodotus, freedman of Lucius, who lived 17 years. Barbius Delphicus and Quarta, his parents, saw to its erection; he is buried here.
EDCS-29100124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Barbius Nymphodotus, a freedman who lived 17 years, erected by his parents, Barbius Delphicus and Quarta.

EDCS-29100126.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Priscus filius et heres matri pientissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29100126.translation: Marcus Iulius Priscus, son and heir, erected this for his most devoted and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-29100126.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Iulius Priscus for his mother.

EDCS-29100128.text_edition: vixit annos XX militavit menses VIII Publius Aurelius Marcianus miles legionis VII Marcianus veteranus legionis VII pater et Aurelia Marcia mater se viva sibi et filiis bene merentibus posuerunt Aurelia Rufina brutes
EDCS-29100128.translation: He lived for 20 years, served 8 months. Publius Aurelius Marcianus, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, and Marcianus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, his father, and Aurelia Marcia, his mother, placed this for themselves while living and for their well-deserving children, Aurelia Rufina Brutes.
EDCS-29100128.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by parents for themselves and their deceased son, Publius Aurelius Marcianus, a soldier of Legio VII, and their other children, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29100130.text_edition: Dis Manibus AGILES vixit nnos LX  Valerius Victorinus  legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos III Valeri-- PPM
EDCS-29100130.translation: To the Divine Manes. Agiles lived for 60 years. Valerius Victorinus of Legio VII Claudia lived for 3 years. Valeri-- PPM.
EDCS-29100130.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, specifically for Agiles, who lived 60 years, and Valerius Victorinus, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, who lived only 3 years, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29100131.text_edition: LO vixit nnos XXXVIII Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus fratri et Ulpia Navia coniunx eius et Aurelius Vitalis et VAIAV IIMRIVV
EDCS-29100131.translation: LO lived 38 years. Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus [set this up] for his brother, and Ulpia Navia, his wife, and Aurelius Vitalis and VAIAV IIMRIVV.
EDCS-29100131.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual who lived 38 years, set up by Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus for his brother, joined by his wife Ulpia Navia, and Aurelius Vitalis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29100133.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo a Procnus miles cohortis I praetoriae
EDCS-29100133.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, from Procnus, a soldier of the First Praetorian Cohort.
EDCS-29100133.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a praetorian soldier named Procnus.

EDCS-29100134.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Augusti Marcus Aurelius Gaius miles legionis VII Claudiae strator consularis votum solvens libens merito posuit Imperatore [] et Comazonte consulibus
EDCS-29100134.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our master Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Gaius, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, a consular strator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of Emperor [] and Comazon.
EDCS-29100134.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Gaius, a soldier and consular strator of Legio VII Claudia, for the health of the emperor, dated to the early 3rd century AD during the consulship of Emperor Elagabalus and Comazon.

EDCS-29100137.text_edition: Fidem domino nostro Constanti Augusto novo anno
EDCS-29100137.translation: Loyalty to our lord Constantius Augustus on the new year.
EDCS-29100137.summary: This inscription expresses loyalty to the emperor Constantius Augustus, likely a New Year's dedication.

EDCS-29100443.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum dedicante Lucio Mario Perpetuo consulari per Restutum magistrum civium Romanorum Dianae sacerdotes eiusdem loci dedicaverunt
EDCS-29100443.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the well-being of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augustus and of the camps, dedicated by Lucius Marius Perpetuus, the consular governor, through Restutus, master of the Roman citizens, the priests of Diana of the same place dedicated this.
EDCS-29100443.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus for the well-being of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna, dedicated by the priests of Diana under the direction of the consular governor Lucius Marius Perpetuus and Restutus, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29601696.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Valerianus vestiarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29601696.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Publius Aelius Valerianus, a clothier, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29601696.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Publius Aelius Valerianus, a clothier, indicating a Mithraic cult dedication, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29601699.text_edition: diebus  decessit hora nocturna VII Aurelius Felicianus praefectus legionis VII Claudiae coniugi dignissimae
EDCS-29601699.translation: He died on [---] days at the seventh hour of the night. Aurelius Felicianus, prefect of Legio VII Claudia, [set this up] for his most worthy wife.
EDCS-29601699.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed woman, set up by her husband Aurelius Felicianus, a prefect of Legio VII Claudia, recording her time of death, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29601700.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Pomponius Valerianus miles legionis VII Claudiae beneficiarius consularis vixit annos XXVIIII militavit annos X VA Pomponia soror eius
EDCS-29601700.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Pomponius Valerianus, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, a consular beneficiarius, lived 29 years, served 10 years. Pomponia, his sister, [set this up].
EDCS-29601700.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of Gaius Pomponius Valerianus, a soldier and consular beneficiarius of Legio VII Claudia, set up by his sister Pomponia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29601701.translation: [...]us, a veteran of the legion [...], his brother.
EDCS-29601701.summary: This fragment likely commemorates a veteran of a legion, possibly erected by his brother, but much of the context is missing.

EDCS-29601702.text_edition: Dis Manibus vivite felices nostrisque profundite Manis et memores sitis nobiscum vos esse futuros Seviae Aquilinae coniugi Aurelius Marinus beneficiarius bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29601702.translation: To the Divine Manes. Live happily and pour libations to our Manes, and be mindful that you will be with us in the future. Aurelius Marinus, a beneficiarius, placed this for his well-deserving wife, Sevia Aquilina.
EDCS-29601702.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sevia Aquilina, dedicated by her husband Aurelius Marinus, a beneficiarius, including a formulaic address to the living about remembering the dead, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29601703.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Dalmatius praepositus vicsit sic annis LX per instantiam Butiici Canis egit uriente L EI
EDCS-29601703.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Dalmatius, a praepositus, lived for 60 years in this manner, through the persistence of Butiicus Canis acted upon urgent L EI.
EDCS-29601703.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Dalmatius, a praepositus who lived 60 years, with the inscription's erection possibly facilitated by Butiicus Canis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29601704.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Vespasiani filius Domitianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XII imperator XXII consul XVI censor perpetuus pater patriae iter Scorfularum vetustate et incursu Danuvi corruptum operibus iteratis O LEG
EDCS-29601704.translation: The Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 12th time, acclaimed imperator for the 22nd time, consul for the 16th time, perpetual censor, father of his country, restored the Scorfulae road, which had been ruined by age and the Danube's overflow, with renewed works. O LEG.
EDCS-29601704.summary: This inscription records Emperor Domitian's restoration of a road, damaged by age and the Danube river, with new construction works, likely by a legion (O LEG). It dates to 92/93 AD, based on Domitian's titulature.

EDCS-29700202.text_edition: Diti Patri et Proserpinae Reginae Quintus Sabinius Vitalis primus Augustalium
EDCS-29700202.translation: To Father Dis and Queen Proserpina, Quintus Sabinius Vitalis, chief of the Augustales.
EDCS-29700202.summary: This is a dedication to the chthonic deities Dis Pater and Proserpina by Quintus Sabinius Vitalis, a prominent member of the Augustales.

EDCS-29700203.text_edition: Cereri Augustae Caius Severius Valens decurio et quaestor II VI municipii Aeli Viminacii ob honorem decurionatus posuit
EDCS-29700203.translation: To Ceres Augusta, Gaius Severius Valens, decurion and quaestor II VI of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, dedicated this in honor of his decurionate.
EDCS-29700203.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Severius Valens to the goddess Ceres Augusta, commemorating his tenure as decurion and quaestor in the municipium Aelium Viminacium.

EDCS-29700204.text_edition: Dianae Marcus Laelius Maximus legatus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis
EDCS-29700204.translation: To Diana, Marcus Laelius Maximus, legate of the Seventh Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion.
EDCS-29700204.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Marcus Laelius Maximus, a high-ranking officer of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-29700205.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Aelius Civilis miles legionis VII Claudiae centuria Aufidini Victoris primi pili hoc signum fecit
EDCS-29700205.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Aelius Civilis, a soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, from the century of Aufidinius Victor, the chief centurion, made this statue.
EDCS-29700205.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Hercules Augustus by Aelius Civilis, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, serving in the century of the primus pilus.

EDCS-29700206.summary: This is a simple votive dedication to Hercules by Caius Iulius Arrianus.

EDCS-29700207.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Fab votum libens solvit
EDCS-29700207.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Valerius Fab[ius?] willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29700207.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Valerius Fab[ius?], indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29700208.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae  decurio aedilis coloniae Viminacii ex voto libens merito posuit
EDCS-29700208.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, a decurion and aedile of the colony of Viminacium, willingly and deservedly dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29700208.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mithras by a decurion and aedile of Colonia Viminacium, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29700209.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Antonini Augusti Pii et Veri Caesaris veterani legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis probati Serviano et Varo et Pontiano et Attico consulibus missi honesta missione per Curtium Iustum legatum Augusti pro praetore qui et legatum legionis et sunt numero CCXXXIX       Ulpius Pharnax Valerius Maximus Sextius Maximus  I   Valerius Aquilinus beneficarius Silius Felix Aemilius Myrismus Passidenius Meri Valerius Maximus Aelius Marcellinus Publius Aelius Fortunatus s Valerius Valens Pupinius Latinus Longinius Alexander Aelius Longus Publius Aelius Augustinus cornicularius Aelius Aquilinus Iulius Longinus Aelius Maximus Aelius Festus cohors III Aelius Rufus Iulius Niarchus Lucius Valerius Proculus Aelius Apollonius Valerius Clemens Valerius Alexander Aelius Paulinus Aelius Marcianus Cornelius Flavianus Valerius Alexander cohors IIII Aelius Secundus Flavius Magnus Aelius Firmus Cornelius Rufus Valerius Crispus Flavius Quintus Aurelius Vale Aelius Marcianus
EDCS-29700209.translation: For the well-being of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Antoninus Augustus Pius and Verus Caesar, 239 veterans of the Seventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, honorably discharged by Curtium Justus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, who was also the legate of the legion, dedicated this when Servianus, Varus, Pontianus, and Atticus were consuls. Among them are Ulpius Pharnax, Valerius Maximus, Sextius Maximus, Valerius Aquilinus, a beneficiarius, Silius Felix, Aemilius Myrismus, Passidenius Meri, Valerius Maximus, Aelius Marcellinus, Publius Aelius Fortunatus, Valerius Valens, Pupinius Latinus, Longinius Alexander, Aelius Longus, Publius Aelius Augustinus, a cornicularius, Aelius Aquilinus, Julius Longinus, Aelius Maximus, Aelius Festus, of cohort III: Aelius Rufus, Julius Niarchus, Lucius Valerius Proculus, Aelius Apollonius, Valerius Clemens, Valerius Alexander, Aelius Paulinus, Aelius Marcianus, Cornelius Flavianus, Valerius Alexander, of cohort IIII: Aelius Secundus, Flavius Magnus, Aelius Firmus, Cornelius Rufus, Valerius Crispus, Flavius Quintus, Aurelius Vale, Aelius Marcianus.
EDCS-29700209.summary: This lengthy inscription records a dedication by 239 honorably discharged veterans of Legio VII Claudia for the well-being of Emperors Antoninus Pius and Lucius Verus, dating to 166 AD based on the listed consuls, and includes a detailed list of the veterans by name and unit affiliation.

EDCS-29700212.text_edition: pro salute Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae Augustae coniugis domini nostri Phillippi Augusti et matris Philippi nobilissimi Caesaris et castrorum domum suam et balneum refecit et paravit Tiberius Claudius Marcellinus eques Romanus dec IIII  Marc- Aurel- Un dec V
EDCS-29700212.translation: For the well-being of Marcia Otacilia Severa, the most holy Augusta, wife of our lord Philippus Augustus, and mother of Philippus, the most noble Caesar, and of the camps, Tiberius Claudius Marcellinus, a Roman knight, decurion of the 4th (century), and Marcus Aurelius Undecurion of the 5th (century), restored and prepared his house and a bath.
EDCS-29700212.summary: This inscription records the renovation of a house and bath by Tiberius Claudius Marcellinus and Marcus Aurelius, for the well-being of Empress Marcia Otacilia Severa and her son, dating to the reign of Philippus I (244-249 AD).

EDCS-29700213.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Antiochus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos LX et Publius Aelius Donatus alumnus eius vixit annos VII Publius Aelius Agathangelus et Publius Aelius Primitivos sic liberti et heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-29700213.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Publius Aelius Antiochus, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, lived 60 years, and Publius Aelius Donatus, his foster son, lived 7 years. Publius Aelius Agathangelus and Publius Aelius Primitivos, their freedmen and heirs, ensured this was made.
EDCS-29700213.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Antiochus, a veteran, and his foster son Publius Aelius Donatus, set up by their freedmen and heirs, Publius Aelius Agathangelus and Publius Aelius Primitivos.

EDCS-29700214.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Priscus miles legionis VII Claudiae stipendiorum XXV vixit annos L Publius Aelius Ursulus filius et Publius Aelius Capito libertus heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-29700214.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Publius Aelius Priscus, a soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, served 25 years and lived 50 years. Publius Aelius Ursulus, his son, and Publius Aelius Capito, his freedman, his heirs, ensured this was made.
EDCS-29700214.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Priscus, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, erected by his son, Publius Aelius Ursulus, and his freedman and heir, Publius Aelius Capito.

EDCS-29700215.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Marcianus stator legati legionis VII Claudiae stipendiorum XXVI Cutius annorum XIII Aprilis annorum XII IVLISRISCRIANI Apria stipendiorum X Aurelia Cutia coniugi et filis et nepoti et Helvius Octavianus nepos heres avunculo et contubernalibus posuerunt Antiochiano et Orfito consulibus
EDCS-29700215.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Aurelius Marcianus, a messenger of the legate of the Seventh Claudian Legion, served 26 years; Cutius, 13 years old; Aprilis, 12 years old; Apria, served 10 years. Aurelia Cutia, his wife, and Helvius Octavianus, his grandson and heir, erected this to her husband, sons, grandson, and messmates, when Antiochianus and Orfitus were consuls.
EDCS-29700215.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Cutia and Helvius Octavianus for Aurelius Marcianus, a legate's messenger, along with his sons Cutius and Aprilis, and Apria, also commemorated, dating to 270 AD based on the listed consuls.

EDCS-29700216.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Rufus tesserarius legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XL militavit nnos XX Aurelius Longinus et Aurelius Aulutralis milites legionis eiustem sic conheredes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29700216.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Marcus Aurelius Rufus, a tesserarius of the Seventh Claudian Legion, lived 40 years, served 20 years. Aurelius Longinus and Aurelius Aulutralis, soldiers of the same legion, as co-heirs, set this up for their well-deserving comrade.
EDCS-29700216.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Rufus, a tesserarius of Legio VII Claudia, set up by his fellow soldiers and co-heirs, Aurelius Longinus and Aurelius Aulutralis.

EDCS-29700217.text_edition: C(aio)? Domitio Cai filio riculo  custodi armorum legionis VII Claudiae vixit annis XXXXII et Neriae Claudiae vixit annis XXVII coniugi eius Domitia Anthusa mater et Domitii Digna Potentinus Ingenua Potentina Surrentus filii et Domitius Iulius nepos bene merentibus posuerunt
EDCS-29700217.translation: To Gaius Domitius, son of Gaius, Riculus, custodian of arms of the Seventh Claudian Legion, who lived for 42 years, and to Neria Claudia, his wife, who lived for 27 years. Domitia Anthusa, his mother, and Domitii Digna, Potentinus, Ingenua, Potentina, Surrentus, his children, and Domitius Iulius, his grandson, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29700217.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of a legionary arms custodian and his wife, erected by his mother, children, and grandson, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-29700218.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Helvius Probus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos LIII hic situs est Pontia Optata coniugi suo pientissimo bene merenti posuit Quintus Helvius Optatus patri suo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29700218.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Helvius Probus, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, lived 53 years. He lies here. Pontia Optata placed this for her most devoted and well-deserving husband. Quintus Helvius Optatus placed this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-29700218.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, set up by his wife and son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700220.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Paternio Titi filio Ianuario veterano legionis IIII Flaviae et Iuniae Ianuariae Paternia Ianuaria filia et heres II V A
EDCS-29700220.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Lucius Paternius, son of Titus, Ianuarius, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, and Junia Ianuaria. Paternia Ianuaria, his daughter and heir, made this.
EDCS-29700220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion and Junia Ianuaria, erected by their daughter and heir Paternia Ianuaria, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700221.text_edition: IVSAIII faciend(um) curavit Caius Terentius Cai filius Claudia Catullus Viruno veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis ex signifero contirunculo et contubernali pientissimo Crispino et Iuliano II consulibus
EDCS-29700221.translation: Gaius Terentius, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, Catullus Viruno, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, formerly a standard-bearer, saw to the erection of this, for his most devoted tent-mate and comrade Crispinus. In the consulship of Crispinus and Julianus II.
EDCS-29700221.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by a veteran to his tent-mate and comrade, dated to the consulship of Crispinus and Julianus II, which corresponds to 224 AD.

EDCS-29700222.text_edition: manus veteranus legionis  vixit annos  hic situs est Titus Flavius  legionis eiusdem heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29700222.translation: [---] veteran of the [---] legion, lived for [---] years. He lies here. Titus Flavius [---], heir of the same legionary, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29700222.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an unnamed veteran, erected by his heir, Titus Flavius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700223.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Caio Valerio Valeriano no VL militavit annos XVI RVI f
EDCS-29700223.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [To] Caius Valerius Valerianus, [who] served for 16 years. [He died at age ---].
EDCS-29700223.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, commemorating Caius Valerius Valerianus who served in the military for 16 years.

EDCS-29700224.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Lucilius ET fEilius#filius#EIL Herculanus decurio aedilis municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit nnos XXXIII Claudia Hygia marito bene merenti
EDCS-29700224.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Lucilius Herculanus, son of Eilius, decurion and aedile of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, lived for 33 years. Claudia Hygia [placed this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29700224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a municipal decurion and aedile of Viminacium, Lucius Lucilius Herculanus, erected by his wife Claudia Hygia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700225.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sexto Valerio Valenti decurioni municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit annos LXIII Valerii Primus Herculianus Valentinus heredes patri pientissimo bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29700225.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Sextus Valerius Valens, decurion of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, who lived for 63 years. Valerii Primus, Herculianus, and Valentinus, his heirs, placed this for their most devoted and well-deserving father.
EDCS-29700225.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a decurion of Viminacium, Sextus Valerius Valens, erected by his three sons and heirs, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700226.text_edition: Dis Manibus Felici qui vixit nnis  diebus XV et iano qui vixit annis diebus XXIII mas Augustalis col(oniae) s et Vibia
EDCS-29700226.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Felix, who lived for [---] years, 15 days, and Ianuarius, who lived for [---] years, 23 days. [---]mas Augustalis of the colony and Vibia [---].
EDCS-29700226.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for two individuals, Felix and Ianuarius, with an Augustalis and a woman named Vibia as dedicants, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700227.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Theodotae rarissimae feminae quae vixit annis XXXI mensibus IIII diebus XVII Aurelius Gallicus coniugi bene de se meritae
EDCS-29700227.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelia Theodota, a most rare woman, who lived for 31 years, 4 months, 17 days. Aurelius Gallicus [placed this] for his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-29700227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Theodota, erected by her husband Aurelius Gallicus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700228.text_edition: Dis Manibus Rufina Valentis vixit annis VIIII Mir Rufi mater et Valens Geti pater bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-29700228.translation: To the Divine Manes. Rufina, daughter of Valens, lived for 9 years. Mira Rufi, her mother, and Valens Geti, her father, made this for their well-deserving child.
EDCS-29700228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Rufina, erected by her parents, Mira Rufi and Valens Geti, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29700229.text_edition: Valerius  et Marcus Valerius Barbario heredes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29700229.translation: Valerius [...] and Marcus Valerius Barbario, heirs, erected this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29700229.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Valerius [...] and Marcus Valerius Barbario for a well-deserving individual, likely a family member.

EDCS-29700230.text_edition: in terra quam procul a patria moribus et forma pariter laudabilis ut quis cernere non posset pulchrior an melior nunc tumulus super et superest Petronia nomen annos bis denos adque duos tetuli  mariti qui caruit vita quom sic caret hac anima nec carum cinerem at sic terram asportare paternam quivit et hic miserum ut discruciet stimulus
EDCS-29700230.translation: In earth, how far from her homeland, equally praiseworthy in character and form, so that one could not discern whether she was more beautiful or better. Now a tomb is above her, and Petronia, her name, remains. I bore twenty-two years, I, her husband, who was deprived of life when she is thus deprived of this soul. He could not carry the dear ashes, but thus the earth of his homeland, and here a stimulus to torture the wretched man.
EDCS-29700230.summary: This is an epitaph for Petronia, seemingly erected by her grieving husband who laments her death far from her homeland and his inability to bring her ashes back.

EDCS-29700231.text_edition: nus et Gaius Iulius Marcellinus matri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29700231.translation: nus and Gaius Julius Marcellinus placed this for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-29700231.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument by Gaius Julius Marcellinus and another individual (name partially lost) for their mother.

EDCS-29700233.text_edition: Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Cocceius Cocceianus vixit nnos L et Cocceia Sabina vixit nnos V Aemilia Ma coniux et Coccei-
EDCS-29700233.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead and to eternal security. Cocceius Cocceianus lived 50 years, and Cocceia Sabina lived 5 years. Aemilia Ma, wife, and Coccei-
EDCS-29700233.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cocceius Cocceianus and Cocceia Sabina by Aemilia Ma, likely their wife and mother, and possibly others from the Cocceius family.

EDCS-29700234.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording an act by Gaius Rufus, but the full context is lost.

EDCS-29700235.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Alpinius Alcimus ornatus orrnamentis decurionalibus municipii Aureli Augusti Margi et Lucius Valerius Trofimas Augustalis municipii supra scripti
EDCS-29700235.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Sextus Alpinius Alcimus, adorned with the decurional ornaments of the municipium Aureli Augusti Margi, and Lucius Valerius Trofimas, Augustalis of the aforementioned municipium.
EDCS-29700235.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Alpinius Alcimus, a decurion, and Lucius Valerius Trofimas, an Augustalis, both holding offices in the municipium Aureli Augusti Margi.

EDCS-29700236.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Antonius Narcissus de suo posuit et porticus fecit
EDCS-29700236.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Antonius Narcissus dedicated this from his own funds and built the porticoes.
EDCS-29700236.summary: This inscription records Marcus Antonius Narcissus's dedication of a monument and construction of porticoes to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating a religious dedication and public work.

EDCS-29700240.text_edition: Deo Heroni collitores ipsius Theodotus Gude pater  Victorinus Mucianus Valentinus Rodo Natus Victorinus Dometianus sic Septuminus sic  Zinama Herodes Hermogenes Iulius Hermogenes Maximinus Marcus votum posuerunt libentes merito
EDCS-29700240.translation: To the god Hero, his worshippers Theodotus, Gude the father, Victorinus, Mucianus, Valentinus, Rodo, Natus, Victorinus, Dometianus, Septuminus, Zinama, Herodes, Hermogenes, Iulius, Hermogenes, Maximinus, Marcus, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-29700240.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Hero, dedicated by a group of his worshippers including Theodotus, Gude, and several others, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.

EDCS-29700241.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Marcus |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae Gallienae ex voto posuit
EDCS-29700241.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Pater. Aurelius Marcus, centurion of the Fourth Flavian Legion 'Galliena,' placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-29700241.summary: This is a votive altar or monument dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Pater by Aurelius Marcus, a centurion of the Legio IIII Flavia Galliena, fulfilling a vow he had made.

EDCS-29700242.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulio Victorino equiti Romano vixit nnos V diem uno sic Iulius Florianus |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae et Victorina filio karissimo
EDCS-29700242.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Iulius Victorinus, a Roman knight, who lived 5 years and one day. Iulius Florianus, centurion of the Fourth Flavian Legion, and Victorina, to their dearest son.
EDCS-29700242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Victorinus, a Roman knight who died young, erected by his parents, Iulius Florianus, a centurion of Legio IIII Flavia, and Victorina.

EDCS-29700243.text_edition: Nymphis salutaribus  Catius Celer pontifexs sic  Lucilla coniuge et Lucio et Sexsto sic filiis
EDCS-29700243.translation: To the health-giving Nymphs. Catius Celer, pontifex, with Lucilla his wife, and Lucius and Sextus, his sons.
EDCS-29700243.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the health-giving Nymphs by Catius Celer, a pontifex, together with his wife Lucilla and their two sons, Lucius and Sextus.

EDCS-29700244.text_edition: Dis et Deabus sacrum Caius Sentius Primus IIIIIIvir Augustalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29700244.translation: Sacred to the Gods and Goddesses. Gaius Sentius Primus, sexvir Augustalis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29700244.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Gods and Goddesses by Gaius Sentius Primus, a sexvir Augustalis, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29700245.summary: This is a religious dedication to the goddess Fortuna by Betuvia Respecta.

EDCS-29700247.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Cassius Celer aedilis decurio quaestor IIvir augur IIvir quinquennalis coloniae Scupinorum
EDCS-29700247.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Cassius Celer, aedile, decurion, quaestor, duumvir, augur, duumvir quinquennalis of the colony of Scupi.
EDCS-29700247.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Cassius Celer, a prominent citizen of the colony of Scupi who held numerous magistracies including aedile, decurion, quaestor, duumvir, augur, and duumvir quinquennalis.

EDCS-29700248.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Caius Lucilius Valerianus aram libens posuit
EDCS-29700248.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, Gaius Lucilius Valerianus willingly placed this altar.
EDCS-29700248.summary: This inscription records Gaius Lucilius Valerianus's voluntary dedication of an altar to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-29700250.text_edition: Invicto Imperatori Pio Felici Gallieno Augusto dis animo voltuque compari res publica
EDCS-29700250.translation: To the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Emperor Gallienus Augustus, in spirit and countenance comparable to the gods, the Republic...
EDCS-29700250.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by 'the Republic' to the Emperor Gallienus, praising him as invincible, pious, fortunate, and comparable to the gods.

EDCS-29700252.text_edition: D(is)? M(anibus) us us nus us  Aelius  legionis vixit hic situs est  ANI filio bene merenti posuit  Marcus Aurelius Mestrius veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit annis LXXX Marcus Aurelius Mestrianus filius patri et filio posuit
EDCS-29700252.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius, of the legion, lived here. He is buried here. [---] for his well-deserving son, Marcus Aurelius Mestrius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia, lived eighty years. Marcus Aurelius Mestrianus, his son, placed this for his father and son.
EDCS-29700252.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aelius and Marcus Aurelius Mestrius, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, placed by Mestrius' son Marcus Aurelius Mestrianus for both his father and son.

EDCS-29700253.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Dindius Marcellus cornicularius legati legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XL hic situs est quodT#quod#QUOT testamento fieri praeceperat famula Parthenope n eris sic faciendum curavit quod opus est Idibus Augustis  Aeliano consulibus
EDCS-29700253.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Dindius Marcellus, cornicularius of the legate of the Seventh Legion Claudia, lived 40 years. He is buried here. As he had ordered in his will, his slave Parthenope took care that it should be done thus, on the Ides of August, in the consulship of Aelianus.
EDCS-29700253.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Dindius Marcellus, a cornicularius of the legate of Legio VII Claudia, erected posthumously according to his will by his slave Parthenope in the consulship of Aelianus.

EDCS-29700255.text_edition: Marcus Licinius Marci filius Voltinia Luco Optatus veteranus legionis I Italicae m[ilitavit &
EDCS-29700255.translation: Marcus Licinius, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe, from Luco, Optatus, a veteran of the First Legion Italica, served [---]
EDCS-29700255.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription for Marcus Licinius Optatus, a veteran of Legio I Italica, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-29700256.text_edition: Quintus Petronius Marci filius Scaptia Rufus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis deducticius titulum fieri iussit
EDCS-29700256.translation: Quintus Petronius, son of Marcus, of the Scaptian tribe, Rufus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia, 'Pia Fidelis,' a deducticius, ordered this titulus to be made.
EDCS-29700256.summary: This is an inscription commissioned by Quintus Petronius Rufus, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, who was a 'deducticius,' likely for his own funerary monument.

EDCS-29700257.text_edition: Caius Rauconius Cai filius Scaptiae Verecundus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit in signis nnos XXXII deductus deductione vixit nnos LXX hic situs est
EDCS-29700257.translation: Caius Rauconius, son of Caius, of the Scaptian tribe, Verecundus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia, 'Pia Fidelis,' served in the standards for 32 years, settled by deduction, lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-29700257.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Rauconius Verecundus, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, who served for 32 years and lived to be 70.

EDCS-29700258.text_edition: Caius Vibius Arator miles frumentarius legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis militavit nnos XVII vixit nnos XXXX hic situs est Iulia Obulcia filio piissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-29700258.translation: Caius Vibius Arator, a frumentarius soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia, 'Pia Fidelis,' served 17 years, lived 40 years. He is buried here. Iulia Obulcia took care that this should be made for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-29700258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Vibius Arator, a frumentarius of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, who served for 17 years, commissioned by his mother Iulia Obulcia.

EDCS-29700259.text_edition: N legionis IIII arius  fabricae legionis eiusdem vixit annis LXV hic situs est Obellia Prima uxor bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-29700259.translation: [---] of the Fourth Legion [---] of the workshop of the same legion, lived 65 years. He is buried here. Obellia Prima, his wife, took care that this should be made for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29700259.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed individual from the workshop of Legio IIII, who lived 65 years, commissioned by his wife Obellia Prima.

EDCS-29700260.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sextus Caelidius Secundus Sexti Caelidi Lupi et Mataurae Cominiae filius=O#filius#FILIO Aemilia StobiOs#Stobis#STOBOS cui ordo coloniae Scupinorum honores aedilitatis et decurionatus contulit vixit annos XVIII dies XXXX sic hic situs est
EDCS-29700260.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sextus Caelidius Secundus, son of Sextus Caelidius Lupus and Mataura Cominia, from Aemilia Stobi, for whom the order of the colony of Scupi conferred the honors of aedile and decurion. He lived 18 years and 40 days. He is buried here.
EDCS-29700260.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Caelidius Secundus, a young man from Aemilia Stobi who had been granted the honors of aedile and decurion in the colony of Scupi, and who died at 18 years old.

EDCS-29700261.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Iulius Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit annos LXXVI hic situs est Suillia Firmina marito bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29700261.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Iulius Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, lived 76 years. He is buried here. Suillia Firmina placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29700261.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Iulius Augustalis, a citizen of the colony of Scupi, who lived 76 years, dedicated by his wife Suillia Firmina.

EDCS-29700262.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Suestius aedili<s=C>#aedili<s>#AEDILIC vixit annos XXXIIII Caius Valerius Claudius filius eius vixit nnos IIII hic siti sunt Claudia Gallita
EDCS-29700262.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius Valerius Suestius, aedile, lived 34 years. Caius Valerius Claudius, his son, lived 4 years. They are buried here. Claudia Gallita [placed this].
EDCS-29700262.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Valerius Suestius, an aedile who lived 34 years, and his son Caius Valerius Claudius, who lived 4 years, commissioned by Claudia Gallita, likely the wife and mother.

EDCS-29700263.text_edition: Dis Manibus tha vixit annos XXXII hic sita est ONON OLON monumentum faciendum curavit
EDCS-29700263.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tha, who lived for 32 years, lies here. Onon Olon had this monument made.
EDCS-29700263.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a woman named Tha, dedicated by Onon Olon.

EDCS-29700264.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Alexander vixit annis XVIIII Caties Alexandri filio pientissimo posuit
EDCS-29700264.translation: To the Divine Manes. Alexander, who lived for 19 years. Caties placed this for Alexander, his most dutiful son.
EDCS-29700264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man named Alexander, dedicated by his father Caties.

EDCS-29700265.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Ambibia Maria, stating that she lived for an unspecified duration.

EDCS-29700266.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caelidia Secunda vixit annos L hic sita est Claudius Herculanus maritus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29700266.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caelidia Secunda, who lived for 50 years, lies here. Claudius Herculanus, her husband, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29700266.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelidia Secunda, set up by her husband Claudius Herculanus.

EDCS-29700267.text_edition: Catinia Catini liberta Festa vixit nnos XXX hic sita est Marcus Catinius Crispus libertae et coniugi pientissimae faciendum curavit
