EDCS-29700267.translation: Catinia Festa, freedwoman of Catinus, who lived for 30 years, lies here. Marcus Catinius Crispus had this made for his freedwoman and most dutiful wife.
EDCS-29700267.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Catinia Festa, a freedwoman and wife, dedicated by Marcus Catinius Crispus.

EDCS-29700268.text_edition: Claudia Ingenua Claudi Secularis filia vixit annos XXXX hic sita est Caius IulI(ius)#Iul(ius)#IUI Val coniugi bene merenti fE(aciendum)#f(aciendum)#E curavit
EDCS-29700268.translation: Claudia Ingenua, daughter of Claudius Secularus, who lived for 40 years, lies here. Gaius Julius Val had this made for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29700268.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Ingenua, dedicated by her husband Gaius Julius Val.

EDCS-29700269.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Claudius Ursio vixit nnos LV hic situs est Valeria Lysimache marito bene merenti cum Urso filio faciendum curavit
EDCS-29700269.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Claudius Ursio, who lived for 55 years, lies here. Valeria Lysimache had this made for her well-deserving husband, with their son Ursus.
EDCS-29700269.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Claudius Ursio, dedicated by his wife Valeria Lysimache and their son Ursus.

EDCS-29700270.text_edition: Lucius Cornelius Luci libertus domo Methymneus vixit annos LXXV
EDCS-29700270.translation: Lucius Cornelius, freedman of Lucius, from Methymna, lived 75 years.
EDCS-29700270.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius, a freedman originally from Methymna, who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-29700271.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Euhelpis...
EDCS-29700271.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (spirits of the dead) for Euhelpis.

EDCS-29700272.text_edition: Ianuaria vixit annos XXXV hic sita est XP
EDCS-29700272.translation: Ianuaria lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-29700272.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ianuaria, stating she lived for 35 years and is interred at this location.

EDCS-29700273.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulia vixit nnos XXX hic sita est Soda Celeris fE(ilia)#f(ilia)#E faciendum curavit
EDCS-29700273.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia, who lived for 30 years, lies here. Soda Celeris, her daughter, had this made.
EDCS-29700273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia, dedicated by her daughter Soda Celeris.

EDCS-29700274.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Gaius Julius Glaucus...
EDCS-29700274.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (spirits of the dead) for Gaius Julius Glaucus.

EDCS-29700275.text_edition: Muc Neri liberta vixit annis XX hic sita est Quintus Nerius Fortunatus libertae et coniugi bene merenti de suo titulum faciendum curavit
EDCS-29700275.translation: Muc Neri, a freedwoman, who lived for 20 years, lies here. Quintus Nerius Fortunatus had this title made from his own funds for his well-deserving freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-29700275.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedwoman named Muc Neri, dedicated by Quintus Nerius Fortunatus, her freedman and husband.

EDCS-29700276.text_edition: Caius Scribonius Myras vixit annos VII hic situs est Caius Iulius Varenus
EDCS-29700276.translation: Gaius Scribonius Myras lived for 7 years; he lies here. Gaius Julius Varenus [set this up].
EDCS-29700276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Gaius Scribonius Myras, set up by Gaius Julius Varenus, likely his relative.

EDCS-29700277.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Herculanus vixit annos LX hic situs est Maria Munatia faciendum curavit Maria Munatia vixit annos LX viva sibi et marito faciendum curavit et Valerius Fortunatus libertus communiEs#communis#COMUNES fecit
EDCS-29700277.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Valerius Herculanus, who lived for 60 years, lies here. Maria Munatia had this made. Maria Munatia, who lived for 60 years, had this made for herself and her husband while she was alive, and Valerius Fortunatus, their common freedman, made it.
EDCS-29700277.summary: This is a joint funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Herculanus and Maria Munatia, prepared by Maria Munatia during her lifetime and executed by their common freedman, Valerius Fortunatus.

EDCS-29700278.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Varronius Urbanus vixit annos XLVI Cornelia Secunda coniugi bene merenti posuit  Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Secunda vixit annos LX viva se posuit Publius Aelius Firmus et Varroni Maximus et Valens et Firmina fili matri bene merenti
EDCS-29700278.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Varronius Urbanus, who lived for 46 years. Cornelia Secunda placed this for her well-deserving husband. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Cornelia Secunda, who lived for 60 years, placed this for herself while she was alive. Publius Aelius Firmus and Varroni Maximus and Valens and Firmina, her children, [placed this] for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-29700278.summary: This is a double funerary inscription: one for Lucius Varronius Urbanus, placed by his wife Cornelia Secunda, and another for Cornelia Secunda herself, set up during her lifetime and completed by her children.

EDCS-29700279.text_edition: hic situs est Caius Valerius Valens et Iulia Valeria fratri faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-29700279.translation: Here lies Gaius Valerius Valens. Iulia Valeria took care to have this made for her brother.
EDCS-29700279.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Valerius Valens, set up by his sister Iulia Valeria.

EDCS-29700280.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Valerius Maximus vixit annos L hic situs est Valerius Euporus qui et Maximus filius et Lucius Manlius Valentinus C et Servie viviE#vivi#VIVE bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-29700280.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Maximus lived for 50 years; he lies here. Valerius Euporus, also known as Maximus, his son, and Lucius Manlius Valentinus and Servius took care to have this made for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29700280.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Maximus, erected by his son Valerius Euporus (Maximus) and others, likely dating from the Imperial period based on common dedicatory formulas.

EDCS-29700281.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Marcia vixit nnos XXXVIII Caius Sentius Primus uxori bene merenti
EDCS-29700281.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpia Marcia lived for 38 years. Gaius Sentius Primus [set this up] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29700281.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Marcia, dedicated by her husband Gaius Sentius Primus.

EDCS-29700282.text_edition: tranquilla per annos aemulaque in cunctis forma seni Pylio
EDCS-29700282.translation: Serene through the years, and a rival in all things to the Pylian elder.
EDCS-29700282.summary: This appears to be a poetic funerary inscription, possibly for a woman, praising her enduring tranquility and beauty, comparing her to the wise Pylian elder Nestor.

EDCS-29700291.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Lucius Alexandrius CGSIA.
EDCS-29700291.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to the Manes, likely for Lucius Alexandrius, with his professional or military affiliation abbreviated.

EDCS-29700292.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae sanctissimae ceterisque diis deabusquae omnibus sacrum Marcus Aurelius Titianus beneficiarius consularis legionis VII Claudiae votum solvit libens merito Caio Aufidio Victorino Tiberio Claudio Proculo consulibus
EDCS-29700292.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva the holiest, and all other gods and goddesses. Marcus Aurelius Titianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Seventh Legion Claudia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of Gaius Aufidius Victorinus and Tiberius Claudius Proculus.
EDCS-29700292.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, and other deities by Marcus Aurelius Titianus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio VII Claudia, dated to the consulship of Gaius Aufidius Victorinus and Tiberius Claudius Proculus (200 CE).

EDCS-29700295.text_edition: Dominae Reginae  et Domino et Bono Evento sic pro salute Augusti Ulpius Martinus strator consularis legionis VII Claudiae Severianae Alexandrianae  votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-29700295.translation: To the Mistress Queen and the Lord and Good Fortune, thus for the welfare of Augustus. Ulpius Martinus, a strator consularis of the Seventh Legion Claudia Severiana Alexandriana, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-29700295.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to a 'Mistress Queen,' a 'Lord,' and 'Good Fortune' for the welfare of the emperor (Augustus), by Ulpius Martinus, a cavalry officer of Legio VII Claudia Severiana Alexandriana, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29700296.text_edition: Deae Iunoni Atilius Felicianus votum posuit
EDCS-29700296.translation: To the goddess Juno, Atilius Felicianus placed a vow.
EDCS-29700296.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Juno by Atilius Felicianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29700297.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Dometianus sic ex voto suscepto posuit
EDCS-29700297.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Valerius Dometianus thus placed [this] in accordance with his undertaken vow.
EDCS-29700297.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by Valerius Dometianus as a fulfillment of a vow he had made.

EDCS-29700298.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo L Aurelius Vitalis ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus pro
EDCS-29700298.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Aurelius Vitalis, adorned with decurional honors, for...
EDCS-29700298.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Lucius Aurelius Vitalis, who held the honors of a decurion, likely for some benefit or in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29700299.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Augusti Titus Aurelius Atta miles legionis IIII Flaviae strator consularis  posuit
EDCS-29700299.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the welfare of our lord Augustus. Titus Aurelius Atta, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, a strator consularis, placed this.
EDCS-29700299.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the welfare of the emperor, by Titus Aurelius Atta, a cavalry officer of Legio IIII Flavia.

EDCS-29700300.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Plato miles cohortis I Cilicum vixit annos XL militavit [annos &
EDCS-29700300.translation: Gaius Julius Plato, soldier of the First Cohort of Cilicians, lived for 40 years, served for [X] years.
EDCS-29700300.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Plato, a soldier of the First Cohort of Cilicians, recording his age and years of military service.

EDCS-29700301.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Severus miles cohortis I Aureliae Dardanorum vixit annos XLVII Iulius Varanus filio pientissimo ---
EDCS-29700301.translation: Gaius Iulius Severus, a soldier of the First Aurelian Cohort of the Dardanians, lived for 47 years. Iulius Varanus [set this up] for his most dutiful son [---].
EDCS-29700301.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Severus, a soldier, dedicated by his father Iulius Varanus.

EDCS-29700302.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Iulianus miles legionis VII Claudiae beneficiarius consularis vixit nnos XXXX Valeria BRAE sibi et coniugi Valerius Iulius et Valeria Iuliana patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29700302.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Iulianus, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia, a beneficiarius consularis, lived for 40 years. Valeria Brae [set this up] for herself and her husband. Valerius Iulius and Valeria Iuliana placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-29700302.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Iulianus, a soldier and beneficiarius consularis, set up by his wife Valeria Brae for herself and him, and by his children Valerius Iulius and Valeria Iuliana for their father.

EDCS-29700303.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Cocceius Sextillianus sic decurio municipii Margi vixit nnos XXVI menses V Valeria Licinia coniux marito desideratissimo et Cocceiae Colendae filiae dulcissimae et sibi sequiturae viva faciendum curavit monumentum
EDCS-29700303.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Cocceius Sextillianus, decurion of the municipality of Margus, lived for 26 years and 5 months. Valeria Licinia, his wife, while alive, took care to have this monument made for her most longed-for husband, and for her sweetest daughter Cocceia Colenda, and for herself, as she would follow them.
EDCS-29700303.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Cocceius Sextillianus, a decurion, and his daughter Cocceia Colenda, commissioned by his wife Valeria Licinia during her lifetime for all three of them.

EDCS-29700306.text_edition: VICRA X  militavit nnos XIIII Aurelia Faustina marito carissimo et generi sic pientissimo et Aurelia filia patri bene merenti et marito dulcissimo et sibi viva sua pecunia
EDCS-29700306.translation: VICRA X, served for 14 years. Aurelia Faustina [set this up] for her dearest husband and most dutiful son-in-law, and Aurelia, the daughter, for her well-deserving father and sweetest husband, and for herself, while alive, with her own money.
EDCS-29700306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual whose name is mostly lost (VICRA X) and who served 14 years, set up by Aurelia Faustina for her husband and son-in-law, and by Aurelia (the daughter) for her father and husband, and for herself.

EDCS-29700307.text_edition: Ecclesiam protegant Petrus et Paulus apostoli sanctiqueique
EDCS-29700307.translation: May Peter and Paul, the holy apostles, protect the Church.
EDCS-29700307.summary: This is a Christian inscription, likely an invocation or prayer, asking the apostles Peter and Paul to protect the Church.

EDCS-29700308.text_edition: Herculi Naisati Claudia Plotina ex voto deo posuit
EDCS-29700308.translation: To Hercules Naisatis, Claudia Plotina placed [this] from a vow to the god.
EDCS-29700308.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Naisatis by Claudia Plotina, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29700309.text_edition: Lucius Vecilius Cai filius Lemonia Modestus equo publico de quinque decuriis sevir aedilis duovir iure dicundo coloniae Hispellatium quaestor II augur praefectus fabrum tribunus militum legionis VI ferratae in Syria praefectus cohortis I Thracum Syriacae in Moesia equitatae cohors I Thracum Syriaca praefecto suo
EDCS-29700309.translation: Lucius Vecilius Modestus, son of Gaius, of the Lemonian tribe, with a public horse, from the five decuriae, sevir, aedile, duumvir for administering justice in the colony of Hispellatium, quaestor for the second time, augur, prefect of engineers, military tribune of the Sixth Ironclad Legion in Syria, prefect of the First Thracian Cohort of Syrians, cavalry unit in Moesia. The First Thracian Cohort of Syrians (set this up) for their prefect.
EDCS-29700309.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the First Thracian Cohort of Syrians to their prefect, Lucius Vecilius Modestus, detailing his extensive military and civic career.

EDCS-29700310.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Sergius Paulus veteranus cohortis I Thracum Syriacae ex equite hic situs est vixit annos LXX vivo se sibi et Pupae coniugi bene merenti posuit servate nepotes
EDCS-29700310.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Sergius Paulus, a veteran of the First Thracian Cohort of Syrians, formerly a cavalryman, is laid here. He lived 70 years. While alive, he himself and his well-deserving wife Pupa set this up. Preserve, grandsons!
EDCS-29700310.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sergius Paulus, a veteran cavalryman, set up by himself and his wife Pupa during their lifetime, with an address to his grandsons.

EDCS-29700311.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Augusti liberto Aprioni Augustali coloniae Ratiariae hic situs est vixit annos LXV Consia Valeria se viva sibi et coniugi suo cum Publis Aelis Proculo Avito decurionibus coloniae Ratiariae et Publis Aelis Aproniano Valente Proculliano filiis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-29700311.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Aprion, freedman of Augustus, Augustalis of the colony Ratiaria, is laid here. He lived 65 years. Consia Valeria, while alive, set this up for herself and her husband, along with Publius Aelius Proculus and Aelius Avitus, decurions of the colony Ratiaria, and Publius Aelius Apronianus, Valens, and Procullianus, their well-deserving sons.
EDCS-29700311.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Aprion, an Augustalis of Ratiaria, erected by his wife Consia Valeria for herself and her husband, including their sons who were decurions.

EDCS-29700313.summary: This is a brief inscription, likely a dedication or a funerary monument, set up by Tertinius Herculanus.

EDCS-29700314.text_edition: qui vixit nnos interfectus a stationariis cum Diurpagisa filio suo qui vixit nnos XVIII bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-29700314.translation: who lived for [---] years, killed by stationarii (roadside guards) with his son Diurpagis, who lived 18 years. (Set up) for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29700314.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual and his 18-year-old son, Diurpagis, who were killed by roadside guards.

EDCS-29700315.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Liciinnio Valeriano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Publio LinvicOinio#Linvicinio#LINVIOINIO GalleFnio sic#Gallenio sic#GALLFNIO Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-29700315.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, father of his country, consul, proconsul; and to the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-29700315.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Valerian and Gallienus, listing their imperial titles, likely for a monument or public work during their joint reign (c. 253-260 CE).

EDCS-29700316.text_edition: Imperatori  Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli et Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae Augustae matri castrorum
EDCS-29700316.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, father of his country, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul; and to Marcia Otacilia Severa, most holy Augusta, mother of the camps.
EDCS-29700316.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab and his wife, Empress Marcia Otacilia Severa, listing their imperial titles, likely for a monument or public work during their reign (c. 244-249 CE).

EDCS-29700317.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Antonini filius divi Veri Parthici maximi frater divi Hadriani nepos divi Traiani Parthici pronepos divi Nervae abnepos Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXXIII imperator VIII consul III pater patriae   Imperatoris Caesaris Flavi Valeri Constantini Pii Felicis maximi victoris ac trimphatoris semper Augusti  milia XII M
EDCS-29700317.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus, son of the divine Antoninus, brother of the divine Verus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 33rd time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country. From the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Maximus Victor and Triumphator, always Augustus, 12 miles.
EDCS-29700317.summary: This inscription is a milestone or boundary marker, identifying a point 12 miles from a location associated with Emperor Constantine, and also referencing the imperial lineage of Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-29700318.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate  consuli  ICIIIOR a colonia Scupinorum milia passuum I
EDCS-29700318.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, consul [---] one mile from the colony of Scupi.
EDCS-29700318.summary: This inscription is a milestone or boundary marker, indicating a point one mile from the colony of Scupi, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian and listing his imperial titles (c. 117-138 CE).

EDCS-29700319.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus
EDCS-29700319.translation: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan, great-grandson of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-29700319.summary: This is a fragmentary imperial inscription, identifying Emperor Antoninus Pius by his full name and imperial lineage, likely part of a larger dedication or monument (c. 138-161 CE).

EDCS-29700322.summary: This inscription records a simple statement of approval by Gaianus, likely indicating his judgment or ratification of a matter.

EDCS-29700323.translation: Lev approved and signed what was just.
EDCS-29700323.summary: This inscription documents Lev's approval and signature, suggesting an official act of validation.

EDCS-30100671.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Marcus Petronius Pierio decurio quinquennalis municipii Aelii Viminacii ob honorem flamonii Marci Petroni Rustici fili sui qui et Cai Valeri ex libris X et SS Ccentum milibus nummum rei publicae dono dedit
EDCS-30100671.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus. Marcus Petronius Pierio, decurion and quinquennalis of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, for the honor of his son Marcus Petronius Rusticus' flaminiate, who also (named) Gaius Valerius, out of 10 and a half books (or 10,500 units of currency), gave a hundred thousand sesterces to the republic as a gift.
EDCS-30100671.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and Emperor Septimius Severus by Marcus Petronius Pierio, who donated 100,000 sesterces to the republic in honor of his son's flaminiate.

EDCS-30100672.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae ceterisque dis deabusque pro salute dominorum nnostrorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum
EDCS-30100672.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva, and all other gods and goddesses, for the well-being of our lords Severus and Antoninus Augusti.
EDCS-30100672.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to a group of gods and goddesses for the well-being of the emperors Severus and Antoninus, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30100673.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Gaius Iulius Valens veteranus legionis VII Claudiae ex beneficiario consularis nunc decurio Viminaci templum a solo refecit
EDCS-30100673.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Gaius Julius Valens, veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, formerly a beneficiary of the consular governor, now a decurion of Viminacium, rebuilt the temple from its foundations.
EDCS-30100673.summary: This inscription records the reconstruction of a temple to Mithras by Gaius Julius Valens, a veteran and decurion, indicating a dedication to the god Mithras.

EDCS-30100674.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Feliculae vixit annis XXXXV libertae et coniugi Lucius Valerius Eutychus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30100674.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Valeria Felicula, who lived for 45 years, his freedwoman and wife, Lucius Valerius Eutychus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-30100674.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Valerius Eutychus to his freedwoman and wife, Valeria Felicula.

EDCS-30100675.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Procula vixit nnos XXXIII titulum posuit Aurelius Victor miles cohortis II Aureliae novae Sacorum coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-30100675.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Procula lived for 33 years. Aurelius Victor, a soldier of the Second Aurelian New Cohort of the Sacae, placed this monument for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-30100675.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aurelius Victor, a soldier, for his wife Aurelia Procula.

EDCS-30100848.text_edition: Marcus Titius Marci filius Quirina Proculus Fabia Roma praefectus cohortis I Alpinorum tribunus militum legionis IIII Flaviae felicis vixit annos XXX menses IIII dies XIII Marcus Titius Zosimus patrono bene merenti fecit Dis Manibus
EDCS-30100848.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Titius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Fabia Roma, prefect of the First Cohort of Alpini, military tribune of the Fourth Flavian Happy Legion, lived for 30 years, 4 months, 13 days. Marcus Titius Zosimus made this for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-30100848.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Titius Proculus, a military officer, by his client Marcus Titius Zosimus.

EDCS-30100849.text_edition: Deo Toto VITIONI Aurelius  Agathominus sic miles legionis IIII Flaviae cataractarum stationis Dianae honeste votum libiens posuit
EDCS-30100849.translation: To the God Totus Vitionius, Aurelius Agathominus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion of the Diana Cataract station, willingly placed this vow honorably.
EDCS-30100849.summary: This inscription records a religious vow made by Aurelius Agathominus, a soldier, to the god Totus Vitionius.

EDCS-30200391.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Augustianus decurio duumviralicius municipii Celegerorum vixit nnos XXXV Bella coniugi rarissimo pientissima sic et filii patri bene merito memoriam
EDCS-30200391.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Augustianus, decurion and duumvir of the municipality of Celegera, lived for 35 years. Bella, his most devoted and rarest wife, and his sons placed this memorial for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-30200391.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Bella, his wife, and their sons to Aurelius Augustianus, a municipal official.

EDCS-30200712.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Flavianus decurio coloniae Ratiariae vixit annos L stat titulus bene meriti filii coniuxque posBuerunt#posuerunt#POBUERUNT
EDCS-30200712.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Flavianus, decurion of the colony of Ratiaria, lived for 50 years. His well-deserving sons and wife placed this monument.
EDCS-30200712.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the sons and wife of Aurelius Flavianus, a municipal decurion, to his memory.

EDCS-30200717.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Alexander |(centurio) legionis VII Claudiae et Iulia Onesime coniunx eius Aurelianeni alumnae suae bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-30200717.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Alexander, centurion of the Seventh Claudian Legion, and his wife Julia Onesime, placed this for Aurelianena, their well-deserving foster daughter.
EDCS-30200717.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Alexander, a centurion, and his wife Julia Onesime to their foster daughter, Aurelianena.

EDCS-30200718.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Heliodorus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae vixit annos XXXXVI militavit nnos XXVI Marcus Calidius Bassus et Marcus Ulpius Maximus heredes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-30200718.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Heliodorus, veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, lived for 46 years, served for 26 years. Marcus Calidius Bassus and Marcus Ulpius Maximus, his heirs, placed this for the well-deserving deceased.
EDCS-30200718.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his heirs, Marcus Calidius Bassus and Marcus Ulpius Maximus, to Marcus Aurelius Heliodorus, a veteran.

EDCS-30200719.text_edition: Lucius Caesius Luci filius Aniensi Flaccus Caesara sic Augusta |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae felicis vixit annos X
EDCS-30200719.translation: Lucius Caesius, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, Flaccus Caesara, thus Augusta, centurion of the Fourth Flavian Happy Legion, lived for 10 years.
EDCS-30200719.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caesius Flaccus Caesara Augusta, a centurion of Legio IIII Flavia Felix, erected presumably by his family or comrades, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-30200720.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Fabio Capreolo liberto optimo Marcus Fabius Paternus tribunus legionis VII Claudiae patronus
EDCS-30200720.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Fabius Paternus, tribune of the Seventh Claudian Legion, patron, (set this up) for Marcus Fabius Capreolus, an excellent freedman.
EDCS-30200720.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Marcus Fabius Paternus, a military tribune, to his excellent freedman, Marcus Fabius Capreolus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-30200721.text_edition: Valerius Isauricus Collina IsauraO#Isaura#ISAURO decurio ex singularibus militavit nnos XV vixit nnos XXXIIII Lucius Nonius Marcellinus fratri pientissimo posuit
EDCS-30200721.translation: Valerius Isauricus, of the Collina tribe, Isaura, a decurion from the singularis (elite guards), served for 15 years, lived for 34 years. Lucius Nonius Marcellinus set this up for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-30200721.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Nonius Marcellinus for his brother, Valerius Isauricus, a decurion who served in the singularis for 15 years, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30200722.text_edition: Lucius Oppius Titi filius Claudia Secundus Varvaria primus pilus legionis VII Claudiae praefectus kastrorum legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis
EDCS-30200722.translation: Lucius Oppius, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, Secundus Varvaria, primus pilus of the Seventh Claudian Legion, prefect of the camp of the Seventh Claudian Pious and Loyal Legion.
EDCS-30200722.summary: This inscription records the distinguished military career of Lucius Oppius Secundus Varvaria, outlining his roles as primus pilus and camp prefect of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, likely a dedicatory or honorific monument from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-30200723.text_edition: NI ETVI IR legionis VII Claudiae vixit nnos XXXVII Laricia quae et Meter coniugi piissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30200723.translation: NI ETVI IR of the Seventh Claudian Legion, lived for 37 years. Laricia, also known as Meter, set this up for her most devoted and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-30200723.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Laricia, also known as Meter, for her husband, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, who lived for 37 years, probably from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-30200726.text_edition: Dis Manibus Manius Servius Mani libertus Thallus Augustalis municipii Aeli Viminacii vixit nnos LXX Manius Servius Silvanus filius et heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30200726.translation: To the Divine Manes. Manius Servius Thallus, freedman of Manius, Augustalis of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, lived for 70 years. Manius Servius Silvanus, his son and heir, set this up for his well-deserving (father).
EDCS-30200726.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Manius Servius Silvanus to his father, Manius Servius Thallus, a freedman and Augustalis of Aelium Viminacium, who lived to 70 years, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30200728.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Lucidus vixit nnos L
EDCS-30200728.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Aurelius Lucidus lived 50 years.
EDCS-30200728.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Titus Aurelius Lucidus, noting he lived for 50 years.

EDCS-30200730.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Proculae vixit annos XI menses III dies II Caius Iulius Herculanus et Iulia Longina filiae bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-30200730.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Iulia Procula, who lived for 11 years, 3 months, 2 days. Caius Iulius Herculanus and Iulia Longina set this up for their well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-30200730.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caius Iulius Herculanus and Iulia Longina to their young daughter, Iulia Procula, who died at the age of 11, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30200731.inscription_type: carmina, inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30200731.text_edition: Lupus anemola sic hic havetat sic quot sic comidi sic mecum haveo sic  ego Maurentia in hunc sic monumentum sic titulum posui Lupo virginio meo cum quem sic quinquaginta annis beni sic laboravi adque inculpatim convixi et Argenteo Samarconi fratris sic vel subulellae matris meae ipsum titulum feci
EDCS-30200731.translation: Lupus, a breeze, thus here he rests, thus what I consumed, thus with me I have. Thus I, Maurentia, placed this titulus on this monument for Lupus, my virginus, with whom I worked well and lived blamelessly for fifty years, and I made the titulus itself for Argenteus Samarco, my brother or little sister of my mother.
EDCS-30200731.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Maurentia for her companion Lupus, with whom she lived for fifty years, and also mentions Argenteus Samarco, a relative, distinguished by its unique, somewhat informal and grammatically unusual Latin, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-30200732.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, beginning with the name Marcus.

EDCS-30200733.text_edition: pietati IR Pudenti et Varena  Crispinill
EDCS-30200733.translation: To piety, IR Pudentius and Varena Crispinilla.
EDCS-30200733.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to piety, mentioning Pudentius and Varena Crispinilla.

EDCS-30200734.translation: To the Divine Manes. Pomp. Fa. P. I.
EDCS-30200734.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, likely for a person with the abbreviated names Pomp(onius/onia) Fa(bius/bia) P.I.

EDCS-30200736.text_edition: Parvolus hic situs est Ulpius Iovinus nomine qui vixit nnos V menses VI dies XXIII hic solus pater hunc humavit parvolum cuius desiderio lumen intentus dolet Ulpius Valerianus beneficiarius legati pater a quo sibi sperabat
EDCS-30200736.translation: Here lies the little one, Ulpius Iovinus by name, who lived for 5 years, 6 months, 23 days. Here alone his father buried this little one, whose absence causes his father, Ulpius Valerianus, a beneficiarius legati, pain and loss of light, from whom he had hoped (for a future).
EDCS-30200736.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from a grieving father, Ulpius Valerianus, a beneficiarius legati, for his young son, Ulpius Iovinus, who died at a tender age, expressing his deep sorrow and lost hopes, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30200737.text_edition: M -D sacerdos se vivo monumentum posuit in partem suam monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-30200737.translation: M.D. (possibly Manibus Dis) a priest set up this monument for himself while alive for his part. The monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-30200737.summary: This inscription records that a priest (whose name is abbreviated or omitted) erected a monument for himself during his lifetime, explicitly stating that it is not intended for his heirs, suggesting a personal or religious dedication, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30200740.text_edition: Dis Manibus Soianus Paterionis vixsit sic annis LXXX Po coniugi rarissimo et sibi et Marco Aurelio Paterioni filio filio merenti p(osuit)
EDCS-30200740.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Soianus Paterionis lived thus for 80 years. Po set this up for her rarest spouse, and for herself, and for their deserving son Marcus Aurelius Paterioni.
EDCS-30200740.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a woman named Po for her husband, herself, and their son, likely a tombstone for a family burial.

EDCS-30200742.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Gellius Exoratus praefectus cohortis V Lucensium votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-30200742.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Gellius Exoratus, prefect of the 5th Cohort of Lucenses, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200742.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest by a military prefect, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30200743.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Scribonius Faustus veteranus etI#et#EI Aurelia Pasina VCRCX
EDCS-30200743.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Scribonius Faustus, a veteran, and Aurelia Pasina...
EDCS-30200743.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Scribonius Faustus, a veteran, and Aurelia Pasina.

EDCS-30200745.text_edition: di opus perfectum curante Ulpio Ulpiano Albino II et Maximo consulibus
EDCS-30200745.translation: The work was completed by divine will, under the care of Ulpius Ulpianus Albinus II and Maximus, consuls.
EDCS-30200745.summary: This inscription records the completion of a construction project, attributing it to divine will and dating it to the consulship of Ulpius Ulpianus Albinus (for the second time) and Maximus.

EDCS-30200746.text_edition: Dei Zbelsurdi SIL  Catulus Munatianus G pro salute parentium et fratrum
EDCS-30200746.translation: To the god Zbelsurdus, S. I. L. Catulus Munatianus G. for the health of his parents and brothers.
EDCS-30200746.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Zbelsurdus by Catulus Munatianus, made for the well-being of his parents and brothers.

EDCS-30200748.text_edition: vixit annos LXXX Marcus Ulpius Surio matri pientissimae posuit
EDCS-30200748.translation: He lived 80 years. Marcus Ulpius Surio set this up for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-30200748.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Ulpius Surio for his exceptionally devoted mother, who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-30200751.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Lucius miles legionis IIII Flaviae Alexandrianae votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-30200751.translation: Marcus Aurelius Lucius, a soldier of the 4th Flavian Alexandrian Legion, willingly and deservedly set up this vow.
EDCS-30200751.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by a soldier of the Legio IIII Flavia Alexandriana, fulfilling a vow to an unspecified deity.

EDCS-30200752.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Attianus miles cohortis  Aureliae Dardanorum
EDCS-30200752.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Attianus, soldier of the Aurelian Cohort of the Dardanians.
EDCS-30200752.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Attianus, a soldier from a Dardanian cohort.

EDCS-30200753.text_edition: vixit annos IIII positus est Aurelius Bitus pater et Aurelia Candida mater filio carissimo et nepoti et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-30200753.translation: He lived for 4 years. Aurelius Bitus, father, and Aurelia Candida, mother, while living, placed this for their dearest son and grandson, and for themselves.
EDCS-30200753.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, set up by his parents, who also include themselves and their grandson in the monument, likely a family tombstone.

EDCS-30200754.text_edition: us Bassi fratri bene merenti hic situs est
EDCS-30200754.translation: [...]us Bassus, to his well-deserving brother. He is buried here.
EDCS-30200754.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Bassus to his brother, indicating a burial site.

EDCS-30200755.translation: Candidus and his sons placed this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-30200755.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Candidus and his sons for a well-deserving son.

EDCS-30200756.text_edition: Deae Fortunae pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Augusti et Caesaris Aelius Flavius Restutus sua pecunia Grato et SeleAuco#Seleuco#SELAUCO consulibus Aurelius Dexter sacerdos posuit lI(ibens)#l(ibens)#I merito
EDCS-30200756.translation: To the goddess Fortuna, for the welfare of our lords Augustus and Caesar. Aelius Flavius Restutus, with his own money, in the consulship of Gratus and Seleucus. Aurelius Dexter, priest, placed this willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-30200756.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Fortuna for the health of the emperors, erected by a priest named Aurelius Dexter with funds from Aelius Flavius Restutus, dated to the consulship of Gratus and Seleucus (221 AD).

EDCS-30200757.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius C S votum posuit
EDCS-30200757.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius C.S. placed this vow.
EDCS-30200757.summary: This inscription records a vow made and dedicated by Gaius C.S. to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30200758.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis deabusque omnibus Marcus Aurelius Marcianus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Se[verianae Ale][xandrianae] piae vindicis Maximo et Urbano consulibus
EDCS-30200758.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and all other gods and goddesses. Marcus Aurelius Marcianus, consular beneficiarius of the 1st Severian Alexandrian Pious Vindicator Legion, [set this up] in the consulship of Maximus and Urbanus.
EDCS-30200758.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter and all other deities by a military officer, Marcus Aurelius Marcianus, dated to the consulship of Maximus and Urbanus (234 AD).

EDCS-30200759.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aepilofio sic Sanc Oriens Cornelius Mide Publius Aelius Cocaius veterani legionis VII Claudiae Severianae ex voto posuerunt Maximo et Aeliano consulibus
EDCS-30200759.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Paternus Aepilofius (sic), Sanctus Oriens, Cornelius Mide, and Publius Aelius Cocaius, veterans of the 7th Claudian Severian Legion, set this up by vow in the consulship of Maximus and Aelianus.
EDCS-30200759.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest, and other deities, set up by several veterans of the Legio VII Claudia Severiana, dated to the consulship of Maximus and Aelianus (227 AD).

EDCS-30200761.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Cassius Candidus miles legionis VII Claudiae discens epibeta sic vixit annos XXI menses  dies IIII militavit annos
EDCS-30200761.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cassius Candidus, a soldier of the 7th Claudian Legion, a discens epibata (junior officer/trainee), lived for 21 years and [---] months and 4 days, served as a soldier for [---] years.
EDCS-30200761.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young soldier, Lucius Cassius Candidus, of the Legio VII Claudia, indicating his age and military service, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-30200763.text_edition: ? posuerunt Vitalis us Iulite Iulius Iulianus patri bene merenti et matri vivae
EDCS-30200763.translation: [...] placed this. Vitalis, [son] of Iulite, and Julius Julianus for their well-deserving father and their living mother.
EDCS-30200763.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vitalis and Julius Julianus to their deceased father and their living mother.

EDCS-30200766.text_edition: Caius Cassius Lo veteranus cohortis I Thracum Syriacae vixit annis C Caius Cassius Alexander Titus Flavius Feliculanus Flavia Prima heredes patrono bene merenti posuerunt hic situs est
EDCS-30200766.translation: Gaius Cassius, an old veteran of the 1st Thracian Syrian Cohort, lived for 100 years. Gaius Cassius Alexander, Titus Flavius Feliculanus, Flavia Prima, heirs, placed this for their well-deserving patron. He is laid here.
EDCS-30200766.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a centenarian veteran, Gaius Cassius, set up by his heirs, Gaius Cassius Alexander, Titus Flavius Feliculanus, and Flavia Prima.

EDCS-30200767.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Valens miles cohortis II Aureliae Dardanorum vixit annis L militavit annis XVIII Rustia Respecta coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30200767.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Valens, a soldier of the 2nd Aurelian Cohort of Dardanians, lived for 50 years and served for 18 years. Rustia Respecta placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-30200767.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Valens, a soldier, set up by his wife Rustia Respecta, commemorating his age and years of service.

EDCS-30200768.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Herculanus veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis se vivo posuit et Antoniae Vibiae coniugi bene merenti vixit annos L hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-30200768.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Herculanus, a veteran of the Legion VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected this during his lifetime for himself and for Antonia Vibia, his well-deserving wife. He lived for 50 years. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-30200768.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a tomb by Gaius Iulius Herculanus, a veteran, for himself and his wife Antonia Vibia during his lifetime, explicitly stating it is not for an heir.

EDCS-30200769.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Florius Lib vixit annos XXV et Aurelia Imation vixit annos XXX Publius Floria Quarta fratri et matri bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-30200769.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Florius Lib lived for 25 years, and Aurelia Imation lived for 30 years. Publius Floria Quarta erected this for her well-deserving brother and mother.
EDCS-30200769.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Publius Floria Quarta to her deceased brother, Publius Florius Lib, and her mother, Aurelia Imation, both of whom died young.

EDCS-30200770.text_edition: inus Dinitralis Tarsa es eques cohortis I Thracum Syriacae turma Longi vixit nnos LXI meruit stipendia XXVIII ex testamento eius heres faciendum curavit h(ic) situs e(st)
EDCS-30200770.translation: [---]inus Dinitralis Tarsa, a horseman of the First Thracian Cohort of Syria, in the troop of Longus, lived for 61 years and served 28 stipends. His heir saw to its erection according to his will. He is buried here.
EDCS-30200770.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates a cavalryman, Dinitralis Tarsa, from a Thracian cohort, detailing his age, years of service, and indicating his heir fulfilled his testamentary wish for the monument.

EDCS-30200772.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cale vixit annis XXII Aelius Fortunatus uxori bene merenti posuit locum
EDCS-30200772.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cale lived for 22 years. Aelius Fortunatus erected this place for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-30200772.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cale, aged 22, dedicated by her husband Aelius Fortunatus.

EDCS-30200773.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Severa sibi se vivo sic simul et Aelis Secundino et Ianuario et Suro filiis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-30200773.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudia Severa erected this for herself during her lifetime, and likewise for her well-deserving sons Aelius Secundinus, Ianuarius, and Surus.
EDCS-30200773.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Claudia Severa during her lifetime for herself and her three sons, Aelius Secundinus, Ianuarius, and Surus.

EDCS-30200774.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Iulius Rufus vixit nnos XIX et Iulia Iulitta vixit annos V hic siti sunt Lucius Iulius Valens pater filiis bene merentibus et sibi vivus posuit
EDCS-30200774.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Iulius Rufus lived for 19 years, and Iulia Iulitta lived for 5 years. They are buried here. Lucius Iulius Valens, their father, erected this during his lifetime for his well-deserving children and for himself.
EDCS-30200774.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lucius Iulius Valens' erection of a tomb during his lifetime for his two deceased children, Lucius Iulius Rufus and Iulia Iulitta, and for himself.

EDCS-30200775.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Trebia Lucia vixit annos XXXV Sall Valens veteranus cum filiis eius coniugi bene merenti et sibi vivo posuit
EDCS-30200775.translation: To the Divine Manes. Trebia Lucia lived for 35 years. Sall. Valens, a veteran, together with her sons, erected this for his well-deserving wife and for himself during his lifetime.
EDCS-30200775.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument by Sall. Valens, a veteran, and his sons for his deceased wife, Trebia Lucia, and for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-30200776.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Marcus vixit annis XVIIII a latronibus interfectus Valerius Eutychus et Sextilia Frontina filio bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-30200776.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Marcus lived for 19 years, killed by robbers. Valerius Eutychus and Sextilia Frontina erected this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-30200776.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valerius Eutychus and Sextilia Frontina to their son, Valerius Marcus, who died at age 19, having been killed by robbers.

EDCS-30200777.text_edition: vitam insidiis in sacra urbe finivit Publius Maius Clementinus eques Romanus filio dulcissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30200777.translation: [He] ended his life by treachery in the sacred city. Publius Maius Clementinus, a Roman knight, erected this for his dearest, well-deserving son.
EDCS-30200777.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly a cenotaph, by Publius Maius Clementinus, a Roman knight, commemorating his son who died in Rome by treachery.

EDCS-30200778.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Tiberi filio Quirina Mercuriali praefecto cohortis I Montanorum Claudia Aristonice marito suo cum Claudio Aristonicole li S
EDCS-30200778.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Mercurialis, son of Tiberius, of the Quirina tribe, Prefect of the First Cohort of Montani. Claudia Aristonice, his wife, with Claudius Aristonicole, [---]
EDCS-30200778.summary: This is an honorific dedication by Claudia Aristonice and Claudius Aristonicole to Tiberius Claudius Mercurialis, her husband, who served as Prefect of the First Cohort of Montani.

EDCS-30200780.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Let vixit nnos LX  TVA OV
EDCS-30200780.translation: Marcus Aurelius Let lived 60 years. TVA OV.
EDCS-30200780.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Let, stating his age at death.

EDCS-30200781.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cemeia Mes[soris...]
EDCS-30200781.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes, mentioning Cemeia Messoris.

EDCS-30200782.text_edition: L Valeriae Ser coniugi pientissimae bene meritae memoriam posuit
EDCS-30200782.translation: [He] placed this memorial for L. Valeria Sera, his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-30200782.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a husband in memory of his beloved and deserving wife, L. Valeria Sera.

EDCS-30200783.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Martialis ex protectoribus vixit annos centum bene merito patrono suo posuit Maxentius gener et servius suus
EDCS-30200783.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Martialis, formerly a protector, lived for one hundred years. Maxentius, his son-in-law, and his own slave Servius, dedicated this to their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-30200783.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maxentius, the son-in-law, and Servius, a slave, to their patron Flavius Martialis, a former protector, who lived to be one hundred years old.

EDCS-30200785.text_edition: Licini Auguste semper vincas  SIC X SIC XX  Naiss-
EDCS-30200785.translation: Licinius Augustus, may you always be victorious! Thus [may you conquer for] 10 [years], thus [may you conquer for] 20 [years]! From Naissus.
EDCS-30200785.summary: This inscription is an acclamation or wish for the emperor Licinius Augustus's continued victory, possibly from Naissus, dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-30200794.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Cretus Papiria lateres cusit sic e Ratiaria
EDCS-30200794.translation: Lucius Valerius Cretus Papiria hammered bricks, thus from Ratiaria.
EDCS-30200794.summary: This inscription records Lucius Valerius Cretus, of the Papirian tribe, as a brick-maker from Ratiaria.

EDCS-30200797.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Atalantes vixit annis L Publius Aelius Augusti libertus Menander |(centurio) ofEficinarum#officinarum#OEFICINAR coniugi pientissimae bene merenti hic sita est
EDCS-30200797.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Atalanta lived for 50 years. Publius Aelius Menander, freedman of Augustus and centurion of the workshops, dedicated this to his most devoted and well-deserving wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-30200797.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Aelius Menander, an Augustan freedman and centurion, to his beloved wife Flavia Atalanta, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-30300712.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae IF LAP Iulius Eutychus patronus et contubernalis - faciendum curavit
EDCS-30300712.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia [---]. Julius Eutychus, her patron and domestic partner, took care of having this made.
EDCS-30300712.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Julius Eutychus for Julia [name incomplete], identifying her as his patroness and domestic partner.

EDCS-30300713.text_edition: Caio Iulio R Caius Iulius Ingenuus patri et fratribus bene merentibus fecit et Iulia Thr=RHepte#Threpte#TRHEPTE vixit annos LX mater
EDCS-30300713.translation: To Gaius Julius R[---]. Gaius Julius Ingenuus made this for his well-deserving father and brothers. And Julia Threpte, his mother, lived for 60 years.
EDCS-30300713.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Julius Ingenuus to his father and brothers, also mentioning his mother, Julia Threpte, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-30300714.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Marci filio Sergia Apollinari hic situs Apollo
EDCS-30300714.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Apollinaris, son of Marcus, of the Sergian tribe, Apollo is buried here.
EDCS-30300714.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial place of Marcus Ulpius Apollinaris, dedicated by Apollo, likely a freedman or dependent.

EDCS-30300715.text_edition: Dis Manibus MarIo vernae vixit annos Aelia Cai liberta  marito optimo
EDCS-30300715.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marius, a home-born slave, who lived for many years. Aelia, freedwoman of Gaius, to her excellent husband.
EDCS-30300715.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia, a freedwoman of Gaius, to her excellent husband Marius, who was a home-born slave.

EDCS-30300716.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcia ipsa viva sibi et caro coniugi Ravio defuncto hanc memoriam posuit
EDCS-30300716.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Marcia, while still alive, placed this memorial for herself and for her dear departed husband Ravius.
EDCS-30300716.summary: This is a funerary inscription, a memorial placed by Aurelia Marcia for herself and her deceased husband Ravius during her lifetime.

EDCS-30300717.text_edition: LDIas qui s vixit annos  Aurelius Ep coniugi et sibi suis  pientissimis
EDCS-30300717.translation: To [---] L. Dius, who lived for many years. Aurelius Ep[---] to his most devoted spouse, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-30300717.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Ep[---] for his most devoted spouse, and also for himself and his family, commemorating L. Dius who lived for many years.

EDCS-30300718.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eutychus Atili Theofae sic servus <Q=A>uartae#Quartae#AUARTE fecit
EDCS-30300718.translation: To the Divine Manes. Eutychus, slave of Atilius Theofa, made this for Quartus.
EDCS-30300718.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, set up by Eutychus, a slave of Atilius Theofa, for Quartus.

EDCS-30300719.text_edition: AN Alexander filius matri bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-30300719.translation: Alexander, his son, took care to have this made for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-30300719.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Alexander, a son, ensured the monument was made for his deserving mother.

EDCS-30600116.text_edition: Lucili Flavi Valentis praepositi cohortis Flaviae
EDCS-30600116.translation: Of Lucilius Flavius Valens, prefect of the Flavian cohort.
EDCS-30600116.summary: This fragment likely comes from a military inscription, identifying Lucilius Flavius Valens as the prefect of the Flavian cohort.

EDCS-30800054.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aemilio Aemiliano Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae cons(uli) proconsuli ab Viminacio milia passuum CC
EDCS-30800054.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Aemilius Aemilianus, Pius, Felix, Unconquered, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul. From Viminacium, 200 miles.
EDCS-30800054.summary: This inscription is a milestone or honorary dedication to the Emperor Aemilianus, indicating a distance of 200 miles from Viminacium, likely dating to his brief reign in 253 AD.

EDCS-30800337.text_edition: Indolis egregiae et pius at morior Iuvenilem eripuere cito rumpentes stamina Parcae quem supremam artem docuit dulcis Cytherea fecerat is doctus rapidas tam naviter artes Pieridum Aonia manantes vertice Tymbrio heu facinus rapido caruisti tramite lucem ter quinos uno crescens anno amplius annos emensus senio accumulato in membris imis Marcus Messius  et tata Maxima heredes duo monumentum memoriae
EDCS-30800337.translation: Of outstanding character and pious, yet I die. The Fates quickly snatched away my youth, breaking the threads, though sweet Cytherea taught him the highest art. He, learned, so swiftly mastered the rapid arts of the Pierides, flowing from the Aonian peak of Tymbrius. Alas, by a swift path you lost the light, having measured thirteen years, growing by more than one year annually. Marcus Messius and his grandmother Maxima, two heirs, made this monument to his memory, now burdened by deep old age in his limbs.
EDCS-30800337.summary: This is a funerary inscription in verse, lamenting the premature death of a talented individual named Marcus Messius, who died at the age of thirteen, and was erected by his grandmother Maxima and another heir.

EDCS-30800342.text_edition: Amphilochii  Fortunae aeternae domus Furianae procuratores Cai Furi Octaviani clarissimi viri Furius Alcimus et Pontius Uranius pecunia Octavianina faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-30800342.translation: For Amphilochius. Furius Alcimus and Pontius Uranius, procurators of the eternal household of Fortuna, belonging to Gaius Furius Octavianus, a most distinguished man, took care to have this made with Octavianus' money.
EDCS-30800342.summary: This is an inscription recording the construction or dedication of a structure related to Amphilochius and the eternal household of Fortuna, funded by Gaius Furius Octavianus and overseen by his procurators, Furius Alcimus and Pontius Uranius.

EDCS-30800343.text_edition: Deo Andino sacrum Tiberius Claudius Certus beneficiarius consularis legionis VII Claudiae votum solvit libens merito Clemente et Prisco consulibus
EDCS-30800343.translation: Sacred to the god Andinus. Tiberius Claudius Certus, a consular beneficiarius of the VII Claudia Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of Clemens and Priscus.
EDCS-30800343.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Tiberius Claudius Certus, a beneficiarius of Legion VII Claudia, to the god Andinus, dating to the consulship of Clemens and Priscus (230 AD).

EDCS-30800344.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Auggustorum Iuliae Augustae matris castrorum Thallion eorundem vilicus votum solvit libens merito Pompeiano et Avito consulibus
EDCS-30800344.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augusti, and Julia Augusta, mother of the camps. Thallion, their overseer, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-30800344.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, and Empress Julia Augusta, made by Thallion, their overseer, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus (207 AD).

EDCS-30800345.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Catulla vixit annis XX Popar filiae bene merenti
EDCS-30800345.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Catulla lived for 20 years. Popar to her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-30800345.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Popar for her well-deserving daughter, Aurelia Catulla, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-30900018.text_edition: Herculi Invicto pro salute domini nostri Augusti Aurelius Herodes pro salute sua et suorum ex viso posuit
EDCS-30900018.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the well-being of our master Augustus, Aurelius Herodes, for his own well-being and that of his family, placed this in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-30900018.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Aurelius Herodes for the health of the emperor and his own family, following a divine vision, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30900019.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Popilius Hermes votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30900019.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Popilius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30900019.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording that Lucius Popilius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-30900020.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cereri Augustae sacrum Publius Aelius Martinus votum posuit
EDCS-30900020.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Ceres Augusta. Publius Aelius Martinus placed this vow.
EDCS-30900020.summary: This is a sacred votive inscription dedicated by Publius Aelius Martinus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Ceres Augusta.

EDCS-30900021.text_edition: Sedato Augusto pro salute Impperatorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum et Genio collegii fabrum Quintus Aelius Antoninus decurio primus biselliarius magister collegii supra scripti dono dedit
EDCS-30900021.translation: To Sedatus Augustus, for the well-being of the Emperors Severus and Antoninus Augusti, and to the Genius of the guild of craftsmen, Quintus Aelius Antoninus, first decurion, biselliarius, master of the aforesaid guild, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-30900021.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Sedatus Augustus, the reigning emperors, and the Genius of a craftsmen's guild by Quintus Aelius Antoninus, a prominent member of the guild, likely dating to the Severan dynasty.

EDCS-30900023.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Aurelio Chresto decurioni aedilicio coloniae Ulpiae Ratiariae vixit nnos LX et Marco Aurelio Iuliano decurioni aedili coloniae Ratiariae data immunitate munerum ordinariorum ob instantiam aquaeducti vixit annos XL Flavia Iulia socro et marito bene merentibus
EDCS-30900023.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Marcus Aurelius Chrestus, a decurion of aedilician rank of the colony Ulpia Ratiaria, who lived 60 years; and for Marcus Aurelius Julianus, a decurion and aedile of the colony Ratiaria, granted immunity from ordinary public duties due to his diligence with the aqueduct, who lived 40 years. Flavia Julia [set this up] for her well-deserving father-in-law and husband.
EDCS-30900023.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Flavia Julia for her father-in-law, Marcus Aurelius Chrestus, and her husband, Marcus Aurelius Julianus, both decurions of Ulpia Ratiaria, with special mention of Julianus's public service on an aqueduct.

EDCS-30900024.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Ponti Silvini decurionis aedilis coloniae Ratiariae Iulia Marcia coniugi
EDCS-30900024.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Gaius Pontius Silvinus, a decurion and aedile of the colony Ratiaria. Julia Marcia [set this up] for her husband.
EDCS-30900024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Pontius Silvinus, a decurion and aedile of the colony Ratiaria, erected by his wife, Julia Marcia.

EDCS-30900025.text_edition: Serviliae Valeriae uxori Iuli Valentis IIviri AL
EDCS-30900025.translation: To Servilia Valeria, wife of Julius Valens, duumvir.
EDCS-30900025.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Servilia Valeria, the wife of Julius Valens, who held the office of duumvir.

EDCS-30900030.text_edition: LL IH COONE et THAPERNEV titulum posuit
EDCS-30900030.translation: L. L. IH COONE and THAPERNEV placed this titulus.
EDCS-30900030.summary: This inscription records the placement of a titulus by two individuals, L. L. IH Coone and Thaperneu.

EDCS-31100116.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavius Omn libens votum posuit
EDCS-31100116.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Flavius Omn willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-31100116.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Flavius Omn.

EDCS-31100117.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Valerius Secundinus  libens votum solvit
EDCS-31100117.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Valerius Secundinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31100117.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius Valerius Secundinus' fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-31100118.summary: This short inscription identifies a Gaius as a priest.

EDCS-31100119.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto legio IIII Flavia felix Gordiana devota numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-31100119.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, the Fourth Flavian Legion, fortunate and Gordianic, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-31100119.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Legio IIII Flavia Felix Gordiana to Emperor Gordian III, expressing loyalty and devotion to his divine status and imperial authority, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-31100120.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Licinia coniunx Ulpi Adiutoris vixit nnos LXXXX Ulpii Equester et Decimus matri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-31100120.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Ulpia Licinia, wife of Ulpius Adiutor, lived 90 years. Ulpii Equester and Decimus placed this for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-31100120.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Equester and Ulpius Decimus to their mother, Ulpia Licinia, who lived to an old age.

EDCS-31100122.text_edition: Inpia fata mea Falconi mortemorte qui plus non vixi quam  Romulus
EDCS-31100122.translation: Impious fates brought death to my Falcon, who lived no longer than Romulus.
EDCS-31100122.summary: This is a funerary inscription lamenting the premature death of someone named Falcon, comparing his short life to that of Romulus.

EDCS-31100139.text_edition: Invicto deo Ision Caesaris nostri servus vilicus vectigalis Illyrici templum omni re instructum a solo pecunia sua faciendum curavit
EDCS-31100139.translation: To the unconquered god Ision, a slave of our Caesar, manager of the Illyrian tax revenue, took care to have this temple, fully equipped with everything, built from the ground up with his own money.
EDCS-31100139.summary: This inscription records the construction of a fully equipped temple to the god Ision, funded entirely by Ision, a slave and imperial estate manager in Illyricum, indicating a private devotional act by a high-ranking imperial servant.

EDCS-31100140.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et felicitati stationis Herculanus vilicus
EDCS-31100140.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the good fortune of the station, Herculanus, the vilicus, dedicated this.
EDCS-31100140.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Herculanus, the vilicus, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the good fortune of a station, likely a post or outpost.

EDCS-31100142.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo VPP dis deabusque et Genio stationis pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Augusti Valerianus speculator legionis IIII Flaviae Severianae Alexandrianae votum solvit libens merito Augusto Severo Alexandro Augusto II et Aufidio Marcello II consulibus
EDCS-31100142.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to the paternal deities, goddesses, and to the Genius of the station, for the well-being of our master Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus, Valerianus, a scout of the Fourth Flavian Severian Alexandrian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of Augustus Severus Alexander Augustus II and Aufidius Marcellus II [226 CE].
EDCS-31100142.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, various deities, and the Genius of a station, made by Valerianus, a scout of the Legio IIII Flavia, for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander, explicitly dated to 226 CE.

EDCS-31100143.text_edition: pro salute sua et suorum Eburniani et Gauri et Attae filiorum et Aeliae Bonae coniugis et Antoniae Victorinae nurus et Corneliae Lucae sororis ex voto posuit
EDCS-31100143.translation: For his own well-being and that of his family: his sons Eburnianus, Gaurus, and Atta; his wife Aelia Bona; his daughter-in-law Antonia Victorina; and his sister Cornelia Luca, he placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-31100143.summary: This is a votive inscription for the health and well-being of an unnamed dedicant and his extensive family, including his sons, wife, daughter-in-law, and sister, placed in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31100145.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Publius Aelius Maximus.
EDCS-31100145.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (Spirits of the Departed) for Publius Aelius Maximus.

EDCS-31100146.text_edition: Ulpie Ionice have bene valeas qui me salutas Dis Manibus Claudia Rufina vixit annis XXX Ulpius Ionicianus vixit annis XXV Ulpius Rufinus vixit annis V hic siti sunt Marcus Ulpius Ionicus coniugi et filiis bene merentibus et sibi vivus faciendum curavit
EDCS-31100146.translation: Ulpia Ionice, hello, fare well, you who greet me. To the Spirits of the Departed: Claudia Rufina lived 30 years. Ulpius Ionicianus lived 25 years. Ulpius Rufinus lived 5 years. They are buried here. Marcus Ulpius Ionicus had this made for his well-deserving wife and sons, and for himself while living.
EDCS-31100146.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Ulpius Ionicus for his wife Claudia Rufina and his sons Ulpius Ionicianus and Ulpius Rufinus, who died young, with Ionicus also reserving a place for himself.

EDCS-31100147.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing a number 'XIII' and a partial or full name 'Anoiclus'.

EDCS-31100150.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nanea Prudentis filia posuit coniugi suo caro militi legionis quartae vixit annos XXX Aelio Paeto bene merenti
EDCS-31100150.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Nanea, daughter of Prudens, placed this for her dear husband Aelius Paetus, a soldier of the Fourth Legion, who lived for 30 years and was well-deserving.
EDCS-31100150.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely a tombstone or monument) dedicated by Nanea to her husband, Aelius Paetus, a soldier of the Fourth Legion, who died at age 30.

EDCS-31100151.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anna Furiae Caeciliae serva vixit annis XXX Prisca soror eius vixit nnis XX Firmus filiis piissimis posuit
EDCS-31100151.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Anna, slave of Furia Caecilia, lived for 30 years. Prisca, her sister, lived for 20 years. Firmus placed this for his most devoted daughters.
EDCS-31100151.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely a tombstone or monument) dedicated by Firmus to his two deceased daughters, Anna and Prisca, who were slaves, Anna dying at 30 and Prisca at 20 years old.

EDCS-31100152.text_edition: Sancto Casebono sacrum pro salutem sic Marci Antoni Rufi Felicissimus libertus
EDCS-31100152.translation: Sacred to Saint Casebonus, for the well-being of Marcus Antonius Rufus, by Felicissimus, his freedman.
EDCS-31100152.summary: This is a religious dedication to Saint Casebonus, made by Felicissimus, a freedman, for the health of his patron Marcus Antonius Rufus.

EDCS-31200131.text_edition: Posuerunt Herculi Bitus Biti Sautes Pa Conus Coni Tato Postumi
EDCS-31200131.translation: Bitus, son of Biti, Sautes, Pa Conus, son of Coni, and Tato, son of Postumi, placed this for Hercules.
EDCS-31200131.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by multiple individuals: Bitus son of Biti, Sautes, Pa Conus son of Coni, and Tato son of Postumi.

EDCS-31200891.text_edition: Dis Manibus Voconiae Epicharis vixit annis XV Eros MAEI sibi et coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-31200891.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Voconia Epicharis, who lived for 15 years. Eros M. A. E. I. for himself and his most devoted wife.
EDCS-31200891.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely a tombstone or monument) placed by Eros for himself and his deceased wife, Voconia Epicharis, who died at 15 years old.

EDCS-31200892.text_edition: STALAAFAVE vixit nnnos VI Marcus Aurelius Felicianus decurio municipii DoD(mavianorum)#Do(mavianorum)#DD coniugi rarissimae bene merenti fecit
EDCS-31200892.translation: Stalaia, farewell! She lived for 6 years. Marcus Aurelius Felicianus, decurion of the municipality of Domavianum, made this for his most rare and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-31200892.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Felicianus, a decurion, to his wife, Stalaia, who died at age six, likely a very young child given the context of 'wife'.

EDCS-31400056.text_edition: Dis Manibus Annaeus Saturnini filius vixit annos XXX Marcellina Annaei filia vixit annos VII Iusta Annaei filia vixit annos III titulum posuit  Marcella Verecunndia marito et filiis communibus
EDCS-31400056.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Annaeus, son of Saturninus, lived for 30 years. Marcellina, daughter of Annaeus, lived for 7 years. Iusta, daughter of Annaeus, lived for 3 years. Marcella Verecundia placed this inscription for her husband and their common children.
EDCS-31400056.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely a tombstone or monument) dedicated by Marcella Verecundia to her deceased husband, Annaeus, and their two young daughters, Marcellina and Iusta, who died at ages 7 and 3 respectively.

EDCS-31400147.text_edition: Deo  Marcus Sulpicius Zoticus Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae templum a solo restituit
EDCS-31400147.translation: To the God. Marcus Sulpicius Zoticus, Augustalis of the colony Ratiaria, restored the temple from its foundations.
EDCS-31400147.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording the restoration of a temple from its foundations by Marcus Sulpicius Zoticus, an Augustalis of the colony Ratiaria.

EDCS-31400148.text_edition: O EANIAmina pro salute suam sic suorumque votum posuerunt Antiochiano et Orfito consulibus
EDCS-31400148.translation: O Eania Mina. For their own salvation and that of their family, they placed this vow during the consulship of Antiochianus and Orfitus.
EDCS-31400148.summary: This inscription records a vow made by an individual or group named O Eania Mina for the salvation of themselves and their family, dated to the consulship of Antiochianus and Orfitus (likely 270 CE).

EDCS-31400149.text_edition: Publius Iulius Secundus ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus dedit |(denarios) II milia se vivo collegio fabrorum
EDCS-31400149.translation: Publius Iulius Secundus, honored with the ornaments of a decurion, gave 2,000 denarii to the college of craftsmen during his lifetime.
EDCS-31400149.summary: This inscription records a donation of 2,000 denarii made by Publius Iulius Secundus, a decurion, to the college of craftsmen during his lifetime.

EDCS-31400151.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeli Salvi Silvini vixit annos XIIII hic situs est us
EDCS-31400151.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Salvius Silvinus lived for 14 years. He lies here.
EDCS-31400151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Salvius Silvinus, likely placed by his family or guardians after his death.

EDCS-31400152.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fidelicula sibi se viva fecit  ITHDVP
EDCS-31400152.translation: To the Divine Manes. Fidelicula made this for herself while still alive. ITHDVP.
EDCS-31400152.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Fidelicula commissioned her own monument during her lifetime.

EDCS-31400154.text_edition: Dis Manibus Larsinia Ingenua vixit annis XXXX Publius Aelius Dionysius veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae felicis ex signifero coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400154.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Larsinia Ingenua lived for 40 years. Publius Aelius Dionysius, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, formerly a standard-bearer, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-31400154.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely a tombstone or monument) dedicated by Publius Aelius Dionysius, a veteran and former standard-bearer of Legio IIII Flavia Felix, to his wife, Larsinia Ingenua, who died at age 40.

EDCS-32500075.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Valeri Felicis ornati ornamentis decurionalibus qui vixit A nnis L et Cassiae Alexandrae quae vixit annis XXX Aurelius GCaianus#Gaianus#CAIANUS amicis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-32500075.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Caius Valerius Felix, honored with the ornaments of a decurion, who lived for 50 years, and for Cassia Alexandra, who lived for 30 years. Aurelius Gaianus placed this for his well-deserving friends.
EDCS-32500075.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Gaianus to his friends, Caius Valerius Felix, a decurion who died at 50, and Cassia Alexandra, who died at 30.

EDCS-32500078.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Ploto vixit annos V dies LVII Marcus Ulpius Trophimus Augustalis et ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus municipii Singiduni et Claudia Valentina filio
EDCS-32500078.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Ulpius Plotus, who lived 5 years and 57 days. Marcus Ulpius Trophimus, Augustalis and adorned with the decurional ornaments of the municipality of Singidunum, and Claudia Valentina [set this up] for their son.
EDCS-32500078.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Ulpius Trophimus, an Augustalis and decurional ornament, and Claudia Valentina, for their young son Marcus Ulpius Plotus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32500079.text_edition: coniugi eius quae vixit nnos LXXXX Ulpia Primitiva patronis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-32500079.translation: To his wife, who lived 90 years. Ulpia Primitiva placed this for her well-deserving patrons.
EDCS-32500079.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Ulpia Primitiva for her well-deserving patrons, one of whom was a woman who lived to 90 years old.

EDCS-32500081.text_edition: Dis Manibus Attius Victoris vixit annis LXX Aia Atti filia vixit annis XXX Propingus sic Atti filius vixit annis XXV Gresa Dasantis vixit annis LXXX titulum posuit Zaca Vaenti coniugi  filiis et matri et sibi vivae bene merentibus
EDCS-32500081.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Attius Victoris lived 70 years. Aia, daughter of Attius, lived 30 years. Propingus, son of Attius, lived 25 years.
