Gresa Dasantis lived 80 years. Zaca Vaenti placed this title for her well-deserving husband, sons, mother, and for herself while living.
EDCS-32500081.summary: This is a familial funerary inscription erected by Zaca Vaenti for her deceased husband Attius Victoris, their children Aia and Propingus, her mother Gresa Dasantis, and also for herself while still alive, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32500082.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Probus vixit annos LXXX Marcus Aurelius Prisgianus sic vixit annos XX Aurelia Maxima coniugi karissimo et filio pientissimo et sibSi#sibi#SISI viva posuit Marcus Aurelius Capericus et Aurelia MaXximina#Maximina#MXXIMINA patri et fratri bene merentibus posuerunt
EDCS-32500082.translation: Marcus Aurelius Probus lived 80 years. Marcus Aurelius Priscianus lived 20 years. Aurelia Maxima placed this for her dearest husband and most devoted son, and for herself while living. Marcus Aurelius Capericus and Aurelia Maximina placed this for their well-deserving father and brother.
EDCS-32500082.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Maxima to her husband Marcus Aurelius Probus and son Marcus Aurelius Priscianus, also including herself while living, and a separate dedication by Marcus Aurelius Capericus and Aurelia Maximina for their father and brother.

EDCS-32500084.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimius LVSANVS T cohortis II Aur
EDCS-32500084.translation: To the Divine Manes. Septimius Lusanus, T[ribune?] of the Second Aurelian Cohort.
EDCS-32500084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius Lusanus, identifying him as an officer in a military cohort.

EDCS-32500085.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Valerius decurio collegii fabrum A B H renovavit votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-32500085.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Valerius, decurion of the college of craftsmen, renewed this, and willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-32500085.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by Aurelius Valerius, a decurion of a college of craftsmen, indicating the renewal of a vow.

EDCS-32500086.text_edition: Aurelius Demas vixit nnos LXXX Aurelius Lucidus et Aurelia Aquilina et Aurelius Vitalis nepotes et heredes avio bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-32500086.translation: Aurelius Demas lived 80 years. Aurelius Lucidus and Aurelia Aquilina and Aurelius Vitalis, his grandchildren and heirs, placed this for their well-deserving grandfather.
EDCS-32500086.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Lucidus, Aurelia Aquilina, and Aurelius Vitalis, for their well-deserving grandfather Aurelius Demas, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-32500087.text_edition: Dianae Antonia Procula ex viso votum solvit  libens merito
EDCS-32500087.translation: To Diana. Antonia Procula fulfilled her vow as a result of a vision, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-32500087.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Antonia Procula to the goddess Diana, offered in gratitude after a vision.

EDCS-32500089.summary: This is a brief inscription likely naming the emperor Hadrian, perhaps from a boundary stone or public work.

EDCS-32500157.text_edition: Iulius Valerius Iuli filius Valens qui et Iulius Fanius Papiria
EDCS-32500157.translation: Julius Valerius, son of Julius, Valens, who is also Julius Fanius Papiria.
EDCS-32500157.summary: This is a fragment likely from a funerary or honorary inscription, identifying an individual by a complex name and filiation, possibly from the Papirian tribe.

EDCS-32602031.text_edition: Iovi et Herculi templum fecit Vecilia Tyranni Augusti liberti procuratoris locus datus ab Appaeo Hermete et Fabiis tribus
EDCS-32602031.translation: Vecilia, freedwoman of Tyrannus, procurator of Augustus, built this temple to Jupiter and Hercules. The ground was given by Appaeus Hermes and three Fabii.
EDCS-32602031.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple dedicated to Jupiter and Hercules by Vecilia, a freedwoman of Tyrannus, a procurator of Augustus, with land provided by Appaeus Hermes and three members of the Fabia gens.

EDCS-33500465.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, the veterans of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, tested, dismissed with honorable discharge by Gaius Gabinius Pompeianus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, and Laelius Maximus, legate of the legion, during the consulships of Priscus and Apollinaris, and Clement and Priscus. Cohort I: Tidius Murenianus TR, Ius Pautalia, Us Sarmizegetusa, Nten Sarmizegetusa, Us beneficiarius consularis Ampelo, Ustus Sirmii, Ius castris, Ratiaria, Xaus Iader, Titianus castris, Alexander Scupis, Bithus Scupis, Saturninus Sarmizegetusa, Mestrius, Lius Dolens Ratiaria, Aurelius Mestula Scupis, Valerius Marcianus Ratiaria, Marcus Cassius Sulpicianus Ratiaria, Valerius Longinus Ratiaria, Marcus Curtius Stambon beneficiarius semestris Ratiaria, Titus Claudius Iustinus Scupis, Titus Aurelius Drigissa Ratiaria, Marcus Aurelius Thamarcus Ratiaria, Titus Aurelius Thithi Ratiaria, Quintus Valerius Quintianus beneficiarius consularis Ratiaria, Marcus Aurelius Dassius Scupis, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius beneficiarius consularis Ratiaria, Titus Antonius Glaucus beneficiarius Mursa, Gaius Iulius Sabinus Ratiaria, Publius Aelius Maximus Ratiaria, Gaius Valerius Maximus Scupis, Gaius Valerius Rufus Ratiaria, Gaius Iulius Sausa Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Priscus Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Attius Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Bonus Scupis, Gaius Iulius Nepos cornicularius praefecti Salonae, Gaius Iulius Marcianus Nicopoli, Gaius Antonius Valens Ratiaria, Marcus Ulpius Paetus Ratiaria, Marcus Ulpius Valens Ratiaria, Marcus Antonius Marcianus beneficiarius tribuni Scupis, Gaius Valerius Maximus Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Sextus Scupis. Cohort II: Titus Aelius Dizo Trimontium, Marcus Aurelius Mestrius Scupis, Marcus Valerius Valens Scupis, Titus Aelius Atilius Salonae, Marcus Aurelius Valens Scupis, Publius Aelius Dassius Ratiaria, Publius Aelius Iulius Sirmii, Titus Aurelius Cupitus Romula, Marcus Licinius Crescens Ratiaria, Marcus Messala Pudens strator consularis Ratiaria, Publius Aelius Primus strator consularis Ratiaria, Aelius Victor Trimontium, Iulius Natalis Ocus, Titus Aurelius Iulius beneficiarius praefecti Scupis, Titus Flavius Dexter Eur, Titus Aurelius Castus tubicen Ratiaria, Marcus Ulpius Messor Sirmii, Gaius Valerius Maximus custos armorum Ratiaria, Titus Aurelius Bithus Ratiaria, Lucius Aurelius Valens custos armorum Ratiaria. Cohort III: Titus Aurelius Tal Ratiaria, Publius Aelius Valens optio Ratiaria, Titus Aurelius Sinna donis donatus Ratiaria, Titus Aurelius Metrodorus signifer Nicopoli, Marcus Aurelius Dines Trimontium, Gaius Valerius Valens Ratiaria, Gaius Valerius Valens donis donatus Remesiana, Marcus Licinius Longinus Remesiana, Marcus Aurelius Germanus Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Valens Remesiana, Publius Aelius Vitalis Remesiana, Marcus Aurelius Dolens donis donatus Remesiana, Lucius Minicius Verissimus donis donatus Remesiana, Marcus Aurelius Daizo Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Timotheus Nicopoli, Gaius Valerius Filiscus Nicopoli, Marcus Iulius Valens Trajanopolis, Publius Aelius Vitalis eques pollio castris. Cohort IIII: Marcus Aurelius Bithus Pautalia, Titus Valerius Valentinus Remesiana, Da Aurelius Sosigenes Thessalonica, Aelius Ingenuus Remesiana, Marcus Ulpius Valens Remesiana, Aurelius Mucco donis donatus Remesiana, Valerius Valens optio Remesiana, Marcus Aurelius Firmus clavicularius castris, Lucius Apicius Sextus tesserarius Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Curia Ratiaria, Marcus Ulpius Valens Ratiaria, Gaius Valerius Valens interpres Remesiana, Gaius Iustienus Sequens Remesiana, Aurelius Saturninus donis donatus Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Dassius Scupis, Marcus Valerius Maximus Pautalia, Gaius Iulius Valentinus S, Gaius Iulius Crescens, Titus Aurelius Curia, Gaius Pyrrus Herculanus, Marcus Aurelius Primus, Nasonius Maximus, Marcus Cocceius M, Iulius. Cohort V: Titus Aelius Herculanus, Lucius Valerius Crispus, Marcus Aurelius Montanus, Gaius Iulius Antonius, Marcus Aurelius Timens, Publius Aelius Longinus, Marcus Aurelius Tara, Marcus Aurelius Maximus custos armorum, Marcus Attius Modestus Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Barbius Salonae, Marcus Ulpius Magnio Remesiana, Gaius Iulius Valens Remesiana, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius Remesiana, Marcus Ulpius Gemellinus signifer Zerna, Gaius Iulius Albanus Remesiana, Marcus Antonius Valens Remesiana, Aurelius Rescuporis Ier, Didius Valens Remesiana. Cohort VI: Tertius To, Titus Aelius Cr, Titus Aurelius Ma, Marcus Aurelius Iu, Publius Aelius Ianuarius, Marcus Aurelius Solinus, Gaius Iulius Clemens, Titus Flavius Maximus, Marcus Aurelius Censorinus, Titus Aurelius Ven, Titus Aurelius Long, Marcus Aurelius Sco, Titus Aelius Felix. Cohort VII: Eques Remesiana, S Remesiana, Valentinus Scupis, Domitius Nicomedia, Dassius tubicen Remesiana, Aurelius Taurus Remesiana, Aurelius Andio Remesiana, Marcus Aurelius Antio signifer Cap, Marcus Valerius Marcellinus Scupis donis donatus, Marcus Aurelius Surus Remesiana, Titus Flavius Valerius Ampelo, Marcus Aurelius Severinus Scupis, Marcus Aurelius Auluzon Remesiana, Titus Claudius Successus Pergamum, Gaius Allius Crescens custos armorum Remesiana, Gaius Valerius Quintianus donis donatus Sirmii, Gaius Valerius Maximus strator Scupis, Gaius Aurelius Mestrius Scupis, I Mursa R, Pollia castris, Anus Scupis, IR Remesiana, Isidorus Cybera, Paulinus Salonae, Nicianus Margi, Postumius Remesiana, Valens Remesiana, S optio Remesiana, S Scupis, Us imaginifer C, Scupis, Us Scupis, Zarmizegetusa, Tus Ancyra, Remesiana, Nn cornicularius Scupis. Cohort VIII: Ius Scupis, O Remesiana, Tis strator Scupis, Ius donis donatus Sirmii, Signifer Scupis, Sirmii, Remesiana, Salonae, S Prupincus donis donatus castris, Marcus Aelius Vitalis castris, Marcus Aurelius Provincialis Remesiana, Gaius Iulius Severus donis donatus Remesiana, Marcus Ulpius Martialis interpres Scupis, Marcus Ulpius Martinus strator Mursa. Cohort VIIII: Titus Aurelius Neptolemus Heraclea, Lucius Apuleius Iustus signifer Remesiana, Marcus Aurelius Valens Scupis, Titus Flavius Aper Sirmii, Gaius Iulius Marcus Remesiana, Titus Flavius Longinus Remesiana, Marcus Ulpius Licinius Sirmii, Gaius Valerius Valens beneficiarius tribuni Remesiana, Gaius Valerius Trophimianus Philippi, Marcus Aurelius Mucatra librarius Remesiana, Gaius Iulius Victorinus Remesiana, Gaius Licinius Paulinus cerarius Scupis, Titus Aurelius Surus, Gaius Iulius Iulianus, Titus Aurelius Macedo, Gaius Valerius Valens, Gaius Valerius Capito, Titus Flavius Vitalis strator legati legionis, Publius Cetenius Iulianus strator, Publius Cetenius Anicianus imaginifer, Gaius Ulpius Victor, Marcus Ulpius Firmus eques, Gaius Terentius Clemens, Gaius Valerius Valens TR, Publius Aelius Candidus. Cohort X: Gaius Antonius Valens donis donatus T, Publius Aelius Silvanus tubicen, Marcus Aurelius Sempronius, Marcus Cocceius Vitalis cornicularius, Gaius Valerius Herculanus, Marcus Ulpius Victor, Tiberius Claudius Victor, Marcus Ulpius Titianus, Publius C Longinus, Titus Aurelius Antiochus optio, Gaius Lucceius Bassus tubicen, Publius Aelius Capito strator, Titus Aurelius Maximus strator, Titus Aurelius Sextus donis donatus, Marcus Ulpius GN, Titus Aurelius, Aelius Iust Salonae.
EDCS-33500465.summary: This lengthy inscription is a list of veterans from the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, discharged with honorable mission during the reigns of Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Antoninus Caesar), providing their names, ranks, and places of origin or assignment by cohorts, dating to between 198 and 209 CE based on the consular dates.

EDCS-34100027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Filete vixit annos LX et Sergius Sultitus Iunior vixit annos XXV hic siti sunt Sergius Sultitianus miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis adiutor prior corniculariorum consularis matri et fratri bene merentibus faciendum curavit
EDCS-34100027.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valeria Filete lived 60 years and Sergius Sultitus Junior lived 25 years. They are buried here. Sergius Sultitianus, soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, senior assistant to the consular cornularii, ensured this was made for his well-deserving mother and brother.
EDCS-34100027.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Sergius Sultitianus, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia, for his deceased mother Valeria Filete and brother Sergius Sultitus Junior, who are buried together.

EDCS-34100028.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcia vixit annos LX Marcellina vixit annos XXX Agricola vixit annos XXI Servius Aelius Valerianus vibus! matri et sorori et fratri bene merentibus faciendum curavit
EDCS-34100028.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcia lived 60 years, Marcellina lived 30 years, Agricola lived 21 years. Servius Aelius Valerianus ensured this was made for his well-deserving mother, sister, and brother, while living.
EDCS-34100028.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Servius Aelius Valerianus for his deceased mother Marcia, sister Marcellina, and brother Agricola, with the dedication made while he was still alive.

EDCS-34100029.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Gaianus vixit nnos LX Aurelia Surisca patrono bene merenti et sibi vibe sic posuit
EDCS-34100029.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Gaianus lived 60 years. Aurelia Surisca set this up for her well-deserving patron and for herself while alive.
EDCS-34100029.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia Surisca for her patron Aurelius Gaianus, who lived 60 years, and for herself, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-34100030.text_edition: thae filius Bessus bene audivit vixit annos L titulum sibi in testamento
EDCS-34100030.translation: Son of Thae, Bessus, who was well-regarded, lived for 50 years. He made this monument for himself in his will.
EDCS-34100030.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bessus, stating that he lived 50 years and arranged for his own monument through his will.

EDCS-36500001.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-36500001.text_edition: Imperator Vespasianus Caesar Augustus tribunicia potestate consul II causaris qui militaverunt in legione II adiutrice pia fidele qui bello inutiles facti ante emerita stipendia ex auctorati sunt et dimissi honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem Nonas Martias Imperatore Vespasiano Caesare Augusto II Caesare Augusti filio Vespasiano consulibus is filio Daco descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio ante aram gentis Iuliae ad podium latere dexteriore  Civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes eIssent#essent#ISSENT cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem Nonas Martias Imperatore Vespasiano Caesare Augusto II Caesare Augusti filio Vespasiano consulibus is filio Daco descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea
EDCS-36500001.translation: Imperator Vespasian Caesar Augustus, in his tribunician power, consul for the second time, has granted citizenship to the soldiers who served in the II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, who, having become unfit for war before completing their stipends, were honorably discharged; and conubium (the right of lawful marriage) with the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted, or if they were bachelors, with those whom they might marry thereafter, provided that each man married only one woman, before the Nones of March, with Imperator Vespasian Caesar Augustus and Caesar Vespasian, son of Augustus, as consuls. This has been transcribed and authenticated from the bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the Capitol before the altar of the Gens Iulia, at the podium on the right side. The same decree regarding citizenship and marriage is repeated for clarity.
EDCS-36500001.summary: This is a military diploma, issued by Emperor Vespasian in 70 AD, granting Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to veterans of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis who received an honest discharge.

EDCS-36600046.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-36600046.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filius Pii nepos divi Hadriani pronepos divi Traiani Parthici abnepos divi Nervae adnepos Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus maximus Brittannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVIII imperator VIII cons(ul) VII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur praetoria civium Romanorum et I Thracum sagittaria veterana et I Flavia Augusta Brittannica |(milliaria) et I Augusta Ituraeorum et I civium Romanorum et cohortibus X et VII et III Batavorum II Aurelia Dacorum et |(milliaria) Hemesenorum et I Lusitanorum II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et |(milliaria) Maurorum III Lusitanorum et I Thracum et VII Breucorum et I Thracum Germanica et I Noricorum et I Alpinorum peditum et I Alpinorum equitum et II Novae et I Campanorum et |(quingenaria) Hemesenorum et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Pomponio Basso legato quinque et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem III Idus Augustas Lucio Iulio Messala Rutiliano et Caio Aemilio Severo Cantabrino consulibus cohortis I Montanorum equitatae cui praeest Valerius Victor ex pedite Tito Flavio Titi filio Titiano Bassiana descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam  Imperator Caesar divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filius divi Pii nepos divi Hadriani pronepos divi Traiani Parthici abnepos divi Nervae adnepos Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus maximus Brittannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVIII imperator VIII consul VII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur praetoria civium Romanorum et I Thracum sagittaria veterana et I Flavia Augusta Brittannica |(milliaria) et I Augusta Ituraeorum et I civium Romanorum et cohortibus X et VII III Batavorum II Aurelia Dacorum et |(milliaria) Hemesenorum et I Lusitanorum II Augusta Thracum et I Montanorum et |(milliaria) Maurorum et III Lusitanorum et I Thracum et VII Breucorum et I Thracum Germanica et I Noricorum et I Alpinorum peditum et I Alpinorum equitum et II Novae et I Campanorum et D Hemesenorum et sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Caio Pomponio Basso legato quinque et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quo quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is iis data aut cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis
EDCS-36600046.translation: Imperator Caesar Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus maximus Brittannicus, son of divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of divine Pius, great-grandson of divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of divine Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 7th time, father of the country, has granted Roman citizenship to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the five alae called Praetoria C. R., I Thracum sagittaria veterana, I Flavia Augusta Brittannica milliaria, I Augusta Ituraeorum, and I C. R., and in the ten cohorts called VII Batavorum, II Aurelia Dacorum, milliaria Hemesenorum, I Lusitanorum, II Augusta Thracum, I Montanorum, milliaria Maurorum, III Lusitanorum, I Thracum, VII Breucorum, I Thracum Germanica, I Noricorum, I Alpinorum peditum, I Alpinorum equitum, II Novae, I Campanorum, and quingenaria Hemesenorum, and who are in Lower Pannonia under legate Pomponius Bassus, having served twenty-five stipends and been honorably discharged, whose names are inscribed below, and conubium (the right of lawful marriage) with the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted, or with those whom they might marry thereafter, provided that each man married only one woman, before the 3rd Ides of August, with Lucius Iulius Messala Rutilianus and Gaius Aemilius Severus Cantabrinus as consuls. For the equitated Cohors I Montanorum, commanded by Valerius Victor, from the infantryman Titus Flavius Titianus, son of Titus, from Bassiana. Transcribed and authenticated from the bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of divine Augustus, near the temple of Minerva. The same decree is repeated.
EDCS-36600046.summary: This is a military diploma, issued by Emperor Commodus in 180 AD, granting Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers honorably discharged after 25 years of service in various units stationed in Lower Pannonia.

EDCS-37200034.summary: This is a short inscription expressing loyalty to the emperor Constantine, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-38100340.text_edition: Lucius Tettius Fonteianus decurio pontifex ex voto
EDCS-38100340.translation: Lucius Tettius Fonteianus, decurion, pontifex, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-38100340.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Lucius Tettius Fonteianus, a local magistrate and priest, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-38100341.text_edition: Herculi Victori Titus Titius Herculanus Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus testamento poni iussit
EDCS-38100341.translation: To Hercules Victor, Titus Titius Herculanus, Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, honored with the ornaments of a decurion, ordered this to be set up by his will.
EDCS-38100341.summary: This inscription is a testamentary dedication to Hercules Victor, ordered by Titus Titius Herculanus, a prominent citizen of the colony of Ratiaria, indicating a statue or altar.

EDCS-38100345.summary: This is a geographical or administrative inscription naming the Roman province of Dacia Ripensis, likely part of a boundary marker or a monumental dedication.

EDCS-38100347.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription in the genitive case, indicating possession, likely from a funerary monument or object belonging to Lucius Coelius Saturninus.

EDCS-38100348.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aurelius Iulianus Iuliani sacerdos eiusdem dei ex provincia Syria Coele regione Cyrrhensi vico Capersina
EDCS-38100348.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Aurelius Iulianus, a priest of the same god from the province of Syria Coele, Cyrrhestica region, village of Capersina.
EDCS-38100348.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Aurelius Iulianus, identifying him as a priest of this deity from a specific region in Syria, likely an altar or votive tablet.

EDCS-38100349.text_edition: Dis Manibus Timoni Dassi decurioni cohortis II Aureliae Dardanorum interfecti sic a Costobocos sic vixit annis L militavit annis XXIII Postumia Spes coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-38100349.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Timon Dassi, decurion of the II Aurelia Dardanorum cohort, killed by the Costoboci, who lived 50 years and served 23 years. Postumia Spes set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-38100349.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Postumia Spes for her husband, Timon Dassi, a decurion killed by the Costoboci, detailing his age and years of service, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-38100350.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Arteas vixit nnos XLV sodales a genio macellIi#macelli#MACELII curante genero Fortunato
EDCS-38100350.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Arteas lived 45 years. The members of the genius of the macellum (market), with his son-in-law Fortunatus overseeing.
EDCS-38100350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Arteas, set up by the association of the genius of the market (sodales a genio macelli) and overseen by his son-in-law Fortunatus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-38100351.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-38100351.text_edition: Dis Manibus Zmaragdus vixit nnos XXXX hic situs est cultorens sic templi Herculis id est Valeri Satyriani sub aedilicio Valeri Eutichetis et quaestura Laevi Iuliani faciendum curavit
EDCS-38100351.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Zmaragdus lived 40 years. He is laid here. The cultores of the temple of Hercules, that is Valerius Satyrianus, under the aedileship of Valerius Eutichetis and the quaestorship of Laevus Iulianus, had this made.
EDCS-38100351.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Zmaragdus, who lived 40 years, indicating he was buried on the site, and was erected by the cultores (worshippers) of the temple of Hercules under specific officials, likely a tombstone or monument associated with the temple.

EDCS-44200287.text_edition: mo veterano vixit annos LXXX Claudia Herete coniugi pientissimo et sibi faciendum et Iulius Martinus filius decurio sacerdotales sic municipii Drobetae curaverunt
EDCS-44200287.translation: Veteran, lived 80 years. Claudia Herete had this made for her most devoted husband and for herself, and Iulius Martinus, her son, a decurion of the priestly college of the municipium of Drobeta, took care of it.
EDCS-44200287.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran who lived 80 years, set up by his wife Claudia Herete for him and herself, with their son Iulius Martinus, a decurion, overseeing its creation, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-44200290.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Fundanio Vestali Publius Fundanius SE bene merenti posuit
EDCS-44200290.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Fundanius, for Publius Fundanius Vestalis, who well deserved it, placed this.
EDCS-44200290.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Fundanius for Publius Fundanius Vestalis, likely a relative or freedman.

EDCS-44200294.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus tribunicia potestate XVI consul III pontifex maximus et Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus tribunicia potestate II consul II divi Antonini Pii filii divi Hadriani nepotes divi Traiani Parthici pronepotes divi Nervae abnepotes restituerunt milia passuum VI
EDCS-44200294.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, pontifex maximus, and Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, sons of divine Antoninus Pius, grandsons of divine Hadrian, great-grandsons of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandsons of divine Nerva, have restored six thousand paces.
EDCS-44200294.summary: This inscription records the restoration of six miles (milia passuum VI) by the co-emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, likely a milestone or a public works inscription dating to 162 AD.

EDCS-44200295.text_edition: Deae Dianae Amabilis Rufri Iusti clarissimi viri ancilla pro salute sua et suorum ex voto posuit
EDCS-44200295.translation: To the Goddess Diana, Amabilis, the slave of Rufrius Iustus, a most distinguished man, placed this by vow for her own well-being and that of her family.
EDCS-44200295.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated by a female slave, Amabilis, to the goddess Diana for her own health and that of her household, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-44200296.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Felix beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae votum solvit libens merito aram posuit
EDCS-44200296.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Flavius Felix, a beneficiarius consularis of the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow and placed this altar.
EDCS-44200296.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a Roman soldier, Titus Flavius Felix, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio IIII Flavia, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-44200297.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Iulius Argaeus Augustalis coloniae Scupinorum vixit annos LXX Vitales sic coniux eius vixit annos LV Maxima filia eorum vixit annos XXXVII Iulius Ingenuus miles legionis IIII Flaviae filius eorum parentibus piissimis posuit
EDCS-44200297.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Iulius Argaeus, an Augustalis of the colony of Scupi, lived 70 years. Vitales, his wife, lived 55 years. Maxima, their daughter, lived 37 years. Iulius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Fourth Flavian Legion, their son, placed this for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-44200297.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulius Ingenuus, a soldier of Legio IIII Flavia, to his parents, Lucius Iulius Argaeus and Vitales, and his sister Maxima, recording their ages and civic roles.

EDCS-44200298.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Octavia Ingenua vixit annos XXV Octavius Leucianus pater et Iulius Argaeus coniugi carissimae posuerunt
EDCS-44200298.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Octavia Ingenua lived 25 years. Octavius Leucianus, her father, and Iulius Argaeus placed this for their dearest wife.
EDCS-44200298.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Octavius Leucianus (her father) and Iulius Argaeus (her husband) to Octavia Ingenua, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-44800122.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Montano nomine Marci Luci Romani Marinius Augustalis coloniae Drubetae ex votum sic in possessione sua posuit
EDCS-44800122.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Montanus, on behalf of Marcus Lucius Romanus, Marinius, an Augustalis of the colony of Drobeta, placed this by vow on his own property.
EDCS-44800122.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Montanus by Marinius, an Augustalis of Colonia Drobeta, for Marcus Lucius Romanus, placed on his own land in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-46700097.text_edition: Caius Plotius Valens filio contra votum sibi et Ulpiae Dianillae coniugi et Plotiis Plotiae Plotinae Valentinae filiis vivos sic faciundum curavit
EDCS-46700097.translation: Gaius Plotius Valens, contrary to his vow for his son, took care to have this made while alive for himself, and for Ulpia Dianilla, his wife, and for their children Plotia Plotina Valentina.
EDCS-46700097.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Gaius Plotius Valens during his lifetime for himself, his wife Ulpia Dianilla, and their daughters Plotia, Plotina, and Valentina, indicating a premature death of a son.

EDCS-51500039.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Crescentilla vixit nnos XXVII Aurelius Valens veteranus ex beneficiario consularis coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-51500039.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Crescentilla lived 27 years. Aurelius Valens, a veteran and former beneficiarius consularis, [placed this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-51500039.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Valens, a veteran and former beneficiarius consularis, to his well-deserving wife, Iulia Crescentilla, who lived 27 years.

EDCS-51500040.text_edition: Saeviae Basillae vixit nnis L hic sita est Marcus Antonius Fronto veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-51500040.translation: To Saevia Basilla, who lived 50 years and lies here. Marcus Antonius Fronto, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and loyal, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-51500040.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Antonius Fronto, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, to his well-deserving wife Saevia Basilla, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-53701325.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Kapitoni liberto qui casu Viminacium Dasmini a latronibus atrocissimam mortem perpessus est Flavia Va mater filio S
EDCS-53701325.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Flavius Kapito, a freedman, who by chance at Viminacium, at Dasmini, suffered a most cruel death at the hands of robbers. Flavia [---], his mother, [placed this] for her son.
EDCS-53701325.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Flavius Kapito, a freedman who tragically died at the hands of robbers in Viminacium at a place called Dasmini, erected by his mother, Flavia.

EDCS-54701316.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Baebio EuYtychi#Eutychi#EYTYCHI Augustali municipii Aeli Viminacii qui vixit annos LXV et Baebiae Marcellae eius Titus Baebius Abascantus Augustalis eiusdem municipii patronis
EDCS-54701316.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Titus Baebius Eutychus, an Augustalis of the municipium Aelium Viminacium, who lived 65 years, and for Baebia Marcella, his [wife]. Titus Baebius Abascantus, an Augustalis of the same municipium, [placed this] for his patrons.
EDCS-54701316.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Baebius Abascantus, an Augustalis of Municipium Aelium Viminacium, to his patrons, Titus Baebius Eutychus (an Augustalis who lived 65 years) and Baebia Marcella.

EDCS-56900051.text_edition: Deo Mithrae Soli Aelius Cai libens votum solvit
EDCS-56900051.translation: To the god Mithras Sol, Aelius Cai willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-56900051.summary: This inscription records Aelius Cai fulfilling a vow to the god Mithras Sol, indicating a dedicatory offering in a Mithraic context.

EDCS-57100067.text_edition: Severi Pertinacis et Marci Aureli Aurel(i) Antonini Pii Felicis Caius Iulius Hermo
EDCS-57100067.translation: Severus Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix. Gaius Julius Hermo [dedicated this].
EDCS-57100067.summary: This inscription fragment likely recorded a dedication by Gaius Julius Hermo to the emperors Severus Pertinax and Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix).

EDCS-57100069.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Verina vixit annos XXX Grattius Severus ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-57100069.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Verina, who lived for 30 years. Grattius Severus, adorned with decurional honors, [erected this] for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-57100069.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Grattius Severus, a man honored with decurional rank, for his devoted wife, Julia Verina, who died at age 30.

EDCS-57100071.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Aelius Severus.
EDCS-57100071.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Aelius Severus.

EDCS-57100072.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mercuri qui vixit nnos XXV Lucius Licinius Polycarpus et Epictesis filio piissimo fecerunt
EDCS-57100072.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Mercurius, who lived for 25 years. Lucius Licinius Polycarpus and Epictesis made this for their most devoted son.
EDCS-57100072.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Licinius Polycarpus and Epictesis for their most devoted son, Mercurius, who died at age 25.

EDCS-57100082.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating ownership or dedication by Titus Cornelius Moschus.

EDCS-57200156.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Vitali vixit nnis LXV hic situs est et Luciae Valeriae filiae pientissimae vixit nnis XVIII hic sita est Valeria Hediste coniugi bene merenti et filiae dulcissimae se viva posuit
EDCS-57200156.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Lucius Valerius Vitalis, who lived for 65 years, he is buried here. And for Lucia Valeria, a most devoted daughter, who lived for 18 years, she is buried here. Valeria Hediste, while still living, placed this for her well-deserving husband and most sweet daughter.
EDCS-57200156.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Valeria Hediste during her lifetime for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Valerius Vitalis (who died at 65), and their most sweet and devoted daughter, Lucia Valeria (who died at 18).

EDCS-61800278.text_edition: Nomina mulierorum sic nuptarum Aurelia Bazis mater SP Aurelia Victorina M Aurelia Sissis Zenobi Aurelia Cunas Apolloni Aurelia Ursula Iuliani Aurelia Fortunata AEPE Aurelia Silvina MVC Antonia Prisca Alexandri Aureli=ILa#Aurelia#AUREILA Mamades Silvini Ulpia Graptilla Celeris Clodia Iusta Nati Aurelia Bonas CrisT
EDCS-61800278.translation: Names of women thus married: Aurelia Bazis, mother of SP; Aurelia Victorina, M; Aurelia Sissis, wife of Zenobius; Aurelia Cunas, wife of Apollonius; Aurelia Ursula, wife of Julianus; Aurelia Fortunata, AEPE; Aurelia Silvina, MVC; Antonia Prisca, wife of Alexander; Aurelia Mamades, wife of Silvinus; Ulpia Graptilla, wife of Celer; Clodia Iusta, wife of Natus; Aurelia Bonas, wife of CrisT.
EDCS-61800278.summary: This inscription records a list of women and, for many, the names of their husbands or other familial connections, likely related to a specific social or religious group, or a legal document concerning marital status.

EDCS-64100126.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To [Terentius Zenis (?)], soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia, of 5 years' service, and [---].
EDCS-64100126.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a soldier of Legion VII Claudia, noting his years of service.

EDCS-64100127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Aemilius Aurelius Secundus, veteran, formerly decurion of the ala of the Illyrian cavalry unit, who lived 70 years. Aemilius Aurelius Polytimus Augustalis [set this up].
EDCS-64100127.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Aurelius Secundus, a veteran and former decurion of an Illyrian cavalry unit, erected by Aemilius Aurelius Polytimus, an Augustalis.

EDCS-64100128.text_edition: Numini Apollinis sacrum Publius Aelius Zoticus Augustalis coloniae Ratiariae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus ab ordine splendidissimo
EDCS-64100128.translation: Sacred to the divinity of Apollo. Publius Aelius Zoticus, Augustalis of the colony Ratiaria, adorned with decurional honors by the most splendid order.
EDCS-64100128.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the divinity of Apollo by Publius Aelius Zoticus, an Augustalis of the colony Ratiaria, who was also honored with decurional rank.

EDCS-64100129.summary: This inscription indicates that Fortunadus crafted something for Agathemerus, possibly a dedication or a funerary monument.

EDCS-64100131.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Libero Patri Cornutianus ex voto posuit
EDCS-64100131.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Liber Pater, Cornutianus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-64100131.summary: This inscription records Cornutianus dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Liber Pater as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-64100132.summary: This inscription lists the names Lulius and Lucius Vivius Sennans Aemilianus, likely indicating individuals associated with the monument.

EDCS-64100134.text_edition: Deae Dardanicae Marcus Aurelius Valerianus beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-64100134.translation: To the Dardanian Goddess. Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, beneficiarius consularis of the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-64100134.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio IIII Flavia, to the Dardanian Goddess.

EDCS-64100136.translation: To the goddess Isis, Eufronius (placed this) as a vow.
EDCS-64100136.summary: This inscription records Eufronius dedicating an offering or monument to the goddess Isis as a vow.

EDCS-64100137.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XVIIII pater patriae censor consul VIII peditibus et equitibus qui militant in cohortibus sex I Cretum I et II Bracaraugustanorum II Chalcidenorum VII Gallorum Ubiorum quae sunt in Moesia sub Sexto Vettuleno Ceriale qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant item remigibus qui militant in classe quae est in provincia eadem sub eodem qui sena et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas
EDCS-64100137.translation: The Emperor Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, acclaimed imperator for the nineteenth time, father of the country, censor, consul for the eighth time, granted citizenship to the infantry and cavalry serving in the six cohorts (the First of Cretans, the First and Second of Bracaraugustans, the Second of Chalcidenians, the Seventh of Gauls, the Ubii) which are in Moesia under Sextus Vettulenus Cerialis, who had served 25 or more stipends; likewise, to the rowers serving in the fleet which is in the same province under the same commander, who had served 26 or more stipends. Their names are subscribed below. He granted citizenship to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of marriage with the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted to them, or, if any were bachelors, with those whom they would later marry, provided each took only one wife.
EDCS-64100137.summary: This is a military diploma from the reign of Emperor Vespasian (dated to 77 CE) granting Roman citizenship and the right of conubium (legal marriage) to auxiliary soldiers and marines who had completed their service in Moesia.

EDCS-66500002.translation: To Mercury the Bringer, Ulpius Viator.
EDCS-66500002.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Ulpius Viator to Mercury Allator, an epithet likely referring to Mercury as a bringer or transporter.

EDCS-67200316.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo -- Iulius In decurio coloniae
EDCS-67200316.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Iulius, a decurion of the colony.
EDCS-67200316.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iulius, who held the position of a decurion in a Roman colony.

EDCS-67200317.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Titico vixit annos XL Aureliae! Severae sic et Aurelio! Aproniano sic bene merenti posuerunt Aureliae Bilae vixit annos LX et Ronelae vixit annos III
EDCS-67200317.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Titicus, who lived for 40 years. For Aurelia Severa and for Aurelius Apronianus, well-deserving, Aurelia Bila, who lived for 60 years, and Ronela, who lived for 3 years, placed this.
EDCS-67200317.summary: This funerary inscription was set up for Aurelius Titicus (who lived 40 years) and for the well-deserving Aurelia Severa and Aurelius Apronianus, by Aurelia Bila (who lived 60 years) and Ronela (who lived 3 years), indicating a complex familial dedication.

EDCS-67500091.translation: To Mars of the Cavalry, for the health of our lords, the Emperors Severus and Antoninus. Atrius, a cavalryman of the Second Aurelian Cohort of Dardanian Antoninians [set this up].
EDCS-67500091.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Mars Equester for the welfare of the emperors Severus and Antoninus, made by a cavalryman named Atrius from the Cohors II Aurelia Dardanorum Antoniniana.

EDCS-68500336.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Flaminius Crispus, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, honored with the ornaments of a decurion by the most splendid order of the Ratiarienses, who lived 15 years. He is laid here. Lucius Flaminius Martialis, his most unhappy father, had this made.
EDCS-68500336.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Flaminius Crispus, a young man honored with decurional insignia by the city of Ratiaria, set up by his grieving father Lucius Flaminius Martialis.

EDCS-68500337.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Titus Iavennus Adiutor and Alpiae Secunda, their parents, and to the Iavennii [---].
EDCS-68500337.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Iavennus Adiutor, Alpiae Secunda, and members of the Iavennii family, likely erected by their children or relatives.

EDCS-68500338.translation: [To the] aedile, quaestor, duumvir. Titus Flavius Philodespotus, Augustalis of the colony of Ratiaria, [to his] deserving patrons.
EDCS-68500338.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Titus Flavius Philodespotus, an Augustalis of Colonia Ratiaria, honoring his patrons who held municipal offices like aedile, quaestor, and duumvir.

EDCS-69200449.translation: For the health of our lord Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus and Imperator Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar Pius Augustus, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the camps. Claudius Magnus, physician [made this] to Asclepius and Hygia.
EDCS-69200449.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for the health of the imperial family (Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna), made by Claudius Magnus, a physician, to the gods Asclepius and Hygia.

EDCS-69200450.translation: To Imperator Caesar, grandson of divine Severus, son of Antoninus Magnus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of his country. 22 miles.
EDCS-69200450.summary: This is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Severus Alexander, listing his titles and indicating a distance of 22 miles from a presumed starting point.

EDCS-69200451.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paterno. Lucius Petronius Timachus, veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, from the optiones, placed this vow.
EDCS-69200451.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paterno by Lucius Petronius Timachus, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia who had served as an optio.

EDCS-70100063.translation: To Diana Augusta, for the health of Mummius Sisenna Rutilianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, and his children. Titus Flavius Euangelus, Augustalis of the colony, curator of ATRA, for him.
EDCS-70100063.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta for the well-being of a provincial governor, Mummius Sisenna Rutilianus, and his children, made by Titus Flavius Euangelus, an Augustalis and curator.

EDCS-70200083.translation: ... lived 40 years. Here he/she is buried. Antonia Rufilla oversaw the monument...
EDCS-70200083.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual who lived 40 years, with Antonia Rufilla responsible for the monument.

EDCS-70200085.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely dedicated to a very holy individual, possibly a princeps iuventutis.

EDCS-70200087.translation: To the Genius of the Augusti, the college of artisans, during the prefecture of Gaius Helvius Titianus...
EDCS-70200087.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius of the reigning emperors by a college of artisans, mentioning Gaius Helvius Titianus as a prefect.

EDCS-70200089.translation: A vow of all the gods. Marcus Varius Politicus placed this.
EDCS-70200089.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Varius Politicus, dedicating a vow to all the gods.

EDCS-70200090.translation: To Aesculapius of Ratiaria, a temple, a statue... Hygia ordered... from a sum of money... Antonius P... 3 sesterces.
EDCS-70200090.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the construction of a temple and statue to Aesculapius of Ratiaria, commissioned by Hygia and potentially financed in part by Antonius.

EDCS-70200091.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Domitia Ampelis, who lived 60 years. Here she is buried. Marcus Domitius Maximus, while living, made this for himself and his wife and children.
EDCS-70200091.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Domitius Maximus for himself, his wife Domitia Ampelis, and their children.

EDCS-70200092.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Zmaragdus, Augustalis of the colony Ulpia Ratiaria, honored with decurional ornaments, who lived 25 years, and to Septimia Saturnina, his wife, who...
EDCS-70200092.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Zmaragdus, an Augustalis and decurion of Ulpia Ratiaria who died at 25, and his wife Septimia Saturnina.

EDCS-71100058.translation: To the divine power of Aptos and [---]. Titus Stertinius Crispinus, soldier of the [---] legion, Severiana Alexandriana, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-71100058.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Titus Stertinius Crispinus, a soldier of the Severiana Alexandriana legion, to a local deity named Aptos, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-71100062.translation: [---] through Valerius Cosmus, decurion of the municipality Aelium Viminacium, and Aelius Iulianus and Aurelius Gaius and Aurelius Epicadus and Iulius Gaianus and Valerius Firminus, veterans of the aforementioned legion, as guardians.
EDCS-71100062.summary: This fragmented inscription records the involvement of several veterans from an unspecified legion and a decurion from the municipality of Aelium Viminacium, acting as guardians, suggesting a funerary or testamentary context.

EDCS-71100063.summary: This is a severely fragmented inscription, mentioning Viminacium, and its exact purpose or content is largely undecipherable.

EDCS-71100067.translation: [---]LV [---]Valeria, daughter of [---]ius, made this from his testament.
EDCS-71100067.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription records that Valeria, the daughter of an unnamed individual, carried out the construction based on her father's will.

EDCS-71100128.translation: Sacred to Diana. Titus Minicius Opimianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power for the province of Upper Moesia.
EDCS-71100128.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Titus Minicius Opimianus, a high-ranking imperial official serving as legate of Upper Moesia, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-71300205.translation: The Iuventianian brickworks of the First Norican Legion.
EDCS-71300205.summary: This inscription identifies a specific brickworks, known as the Iuventianian, which belonged to the Legio I Noricorum, indicating its use for military construction or production.

EDCS-71400601.summary: This inscription simply names the Emperor Magnentius Augustus, likely a building inscription or an honorary monument dedicated to him during his brief reign in the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-72700152.translation: To Bonus Eventus. Fonteianus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-72700152.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Fonteianus to Bonus Eventus, a Roman deity personifying Good Outcome, likely for a successful undertaking.

EDCS-74700584.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Coelius Firminus, son of Lucius, of the Romilia tribe, from Ateste. He was a prefect of the Second Cohort of Gauls, military tribune of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, prefect of the Second Ala of Pannonians. He was honored with gifts in the Dacian War. Duumvir of the colony of Atestinae. He lived for [---] years. Syrillus(?), his freedman and heir, took care of having this made.
EDCS-74700584.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Coelius Firminus, a distinguished Roman equestrian officer from Ateste, outlining his extensive military career including service in the Dacian War and later a political role as duumvir, erected by his freedman and heir.

EDCS-75000367.summary: This brief inscription expresses loyalty to Emperor Constantine, likely part of an honorary monument or a public declaration during his reign in the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-75200356.translation: ...placed this. Flavius Modeius, horn-blower of the cohort...
EDCS-75200356.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely from a monument or altar, made by Flavius Modeius, a horn-blower in a Roman military unit.

EDCS-75300186.summary: This is an epitaph for Valerius Polo, indicating he lived to the age of 56.

EDCS-76600334.text_edition: Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto imperatore pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXXV legio IIII Scythica legio V Macedonica
EDCS-76600334.translation: During the consulship of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of Augustus, imperator, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 35th time. The Fourth Scythian Legion and the Fifth Macedonian Legion [were involved].
EDCS-76600334.summary: This inscription fragment provides a date from the reign of Emperor Tiberius (33-34 CE) through his imperial titles and mentions the Fourth Scythian Legion and the Fifth Macedonian Legion, suggesting it was part of a larger monument or building project involving these legions.
